The conditions of the Governor's office are not part of that special list.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text:
With reference to the Indian polity, consider the following statements : I. The Governor of a State is not answerable to any court for the exercise and performance of the powers and duties of his/her office. II. No criminal proceedings shall be instituted or continued against the Governor during his/her term of office. III. Members of a State Legislature are not liable to any proceedings in any court in respect of anything said within the House.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: All three statements are correct. The Governor is not answerable to any court for the exercise of official powers and duties, no criminal proceedings can be instituted or continued against the Governor during the term of office, and members of a state legislature enjoy immunity from court proceedings for anything said within the House.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
Consider the following activities :
I. Production of crude oil II. Refining, storage and distribution of petroleum III. Marketing and sale of petroleum products IV. Productions of natural gas
How many of the above activities are regulated by the Petroleum and Natural Gas Regulatory Board in our country?
questions.explanation: The Petroleum and Natural Gas Regulatory Board regulates the downstream activities: the refining, storage and distribution of petroleum, and the marketing and sale of petroleum products. It does not regulate upstream exploration and production, so the production of crude oil and of natural gas fall outside its remit, leaving two regulated activities.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text: “Sedition has become my religion” was the famous statement given by Gandhiji at the time of
questions.option_b: publicly violating Salt Law at Dandi
questions.option_c: attending the Second Round Table Conference in London
questions.option_d: the launch of the Quit India Movement
questions.explanation: Gandhi made the remark 'sedition has become my religion' in the context of his open defiance of the colonial state during the Salt Satyagraha, when he publicly broke the Salt Law at Dandi. By deliberately violating an unjust law, he embraced the charge of sedition as a matter of moral duty against colonial rule.

questions.subject: Art & Culture
questions.text: The famous female figurine known as ‘Dancing Girl’, found at Mohenjo-daro, is made of
questions.explanation: The celebrated 'Dancing Girl' figurine from Mohenjo-daro is made of bronze, cast using the lost-wax technique. This small statuette is one of the finest examples of Harappan metal craftsmanship. It is not made of clay, carnelian or gold, marking bronze as the correct material.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text: Who provided legal defence to the people arrested in the aftermath of Chauri Chaura incident?
questions.option_b: Madan Mohan Malaviya and Krishna Kant
questions.option_c: Dr. Saifuddin Kitchlew and Khwaja Hasan Nizami
questions.explanation: Legal defence for those arrested after the Chauri Chaura incident was provided by Madan Mohan Malaviya, working alongside Krishna Kant. The other leaders listed were not the ones who took up the courtroom defence of the accused in this case, making this pairing the correct answer.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text: Subsequent to which one of the following events, Gandhiji, who consistently opposed untouchability and appealed for its eradication from all spheres, decided to include the upliftment of ‘Harijans’ in his political and social programme?
questions.option_c: Arrest of Congress leadership at the time of the Quit India Movement.
questions.option_d: Promulgation of the Government of India Act, 1935
questions.explanation: It was after the Poona Pact of 1932 that Gandhi formally took up the upliftment of 'Harijans' as a central part of his political and social work. The pact, which retained joint electorates while reserving seats for depressed classes, prompted him to intensify his campaign against untouchability across the country.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text:
Consider the following fruits :
I. Papaya II. Pineapple III. Guava
How many of the above were introduced in India by the Portuguese in the sixteenth and seventeenth centuries?
questions.explanation: All three fruits were introduced to India by the Portuguese during the sixteenth and seventeenth centuries. Papaya, pineapple and guava are New World or tropical fruits that Portuguese traders brought to Indian shores as part of the wider exchange of crops during this period, so all three qualify.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Consider the following countries :
I. United Kingdom II. Denmark III. New Zealand IV. Australia V. Brazil
How many of the above countries have more than four time zones?
questions.explanation: Three of the listed countries span more than four time zones once their overseas and external territories are counted, since possessions scattered across the globe stretch their official time far beyond the mainland. Brazil, by contrast, covers only up to four time zones, so it falls short, leaving three countries that exceed four.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Consider the following statements :
I. Anadyr in Siberia and Nome in Alaska are a few kilometers from each other, but when people are waking up and getting set for breakfast in these cities, it would be different days. II. When it is Monday in Anadyr, it is Tuesday in Nome.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Only Statement I is correct. Anadyr in Siberia and Nome in Alaska lie close together but on opposite sides of the International Date Line, so people there experience different calendar days at the same moment. Statement II reverses the order: Anadyr, west of the line, is actually a day ahead of Nome, not behind it.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text: Who among the following was the founder of the ‘Self- Respect Movement’?
questions.explanation: The Self-Respect Movement was founded by 'Periyar' E.V. Ramaswamy Naicker in the Tamil region. It campaigned against caste hierarchy, Brahmin dominance and social inequality, promoting rationalism and human dignity. The other figures listed were not its founders, so Periyar is the correct answer.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Consider the following pairs :
Country Resource-rich in I. Botswana : Diamond II. Chile : Lithium III. Indonesia : Nickel In how many of the above rows is the given information correctly matched?
questions.explanation: All three pairs are correctly matched. Botswana is a leading producer of diamonds, Chile is rich in lithium reserves within the lithium triangle of South America, and Indonesia holds abundant nickel deposits central to battery supply chains. Each country is accurately linked with the resource it is known for.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Consider the following pairs :
Region Country I. Mallorca : Italy II. Normandy : Spain III. Sardinia : France In how many of the above rows is the given information correctly matched?
questions.explanation: None of the pairs is correct. Mallorca is an island of Spain, not Italy; Normandy is a region of France, not Spain; and Sardinia is an island belonging to Italy, not France. Since every region is matched to the wrong country, the correct answer is none.

papers.source_note: Real questions from UPSC Civil Services Prelims 2024 (GS Paper I), a public government examination paper. Explanations are AIVORA-authored and original; not copied from any coaching material.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text: How many Delimitation Commissions have been constituted by the Government of India till December 2023?
questions.explanation: Since independence, Delimitation Commissions have been set up four times, in 1952, 1963, 1973 and 2002, each following the enactment of a Delimitation Act. As of December 2023 no fifth commission had been constituted, so the total remains four.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text:
The Constitution (71st Amendment) Act, 1992 amends the Eighth Schedule to the Constitution to include which of the following languages?
1. Konkani
2. Manipuri
3. Nepali
4. Maithili Select the correction answer using the code given below :
questions.explanation: The 71st Amendment of 1992 expanded the Eighth Schedule by adding Konkani, Manipuri and Nepali, taking the count to eighteen scheduled languages. Maithili was included much later through the 92nd Amendment of 2003, so it is not part of this set, making option A correct.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
Consider the following pairs :
Party Its Leader
1. Bhartiya Jana Sangh Dr. Shyama Prasad Mukherjee
2. Socialist Party C. Rajagopalachari
3. Congress for Democracy Jagjivan Ram
4. Swatantra Party Acharya Narendra Dev
How many of the above are correctly matched?
questions.explanation: Bharatiya Jana Sangh was indeed founded by Shyama Prasad Mukherjee, and the Congress for Democracy was led by Jagjivan Ram, so both are correct. However, C. Rajagopalachari was associated with the Swatantra Party, not the Socialist Party, and Acharya Narendra Dev was a socialist leader, not of Swatantra, leaving only two correct pairs.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text:
Which of the following statements are correct about the Constitution of India?
1. Powers of the Municipalities are given in Part IX A of the Constitution.
2. Emergency provisions are given in Part XVIII of the Constitution.
3. Provisions related to the amendment of the Constitution are given in Part XX of the Constitution.
Select the answer using the code given below :
questions.explanation: Part IX-A deals with Municipalities, the emergency provisions fall under Part XVIII, and the amendment procedure under Article 368 sits in Part XX. All three descriptions match the Constitution's scheme, so every statement is correct.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text: Which one of the following statements is correct as per the Constitution of India?
questions.option_a: Inter-State trade and commerce is a State subject under the State List.
questions.option_b: Inter-State migration is a State subject under the State List.
questions.option_c: Inter-State quarantine is a Union subject under the Union List.
questions.option_d: Corporation tax is a State subject under the State List.
questions.explanation: Inter-State quarantine appears in the Union List, so the Centre legislates on it. The other options are wrong because inter-State trade and commerce and inter-State migration are Union subjects rather than State ones, and corporation tax is also a Union levy, not a State subject.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text: Under which of the following Articles of the Constitution of India, has the Supreme Court of India places the Right to Privacy?
questions.explanation: In the 2017 Puttaswamy judgment the Supreme Court held that the Right to Privacy is intrinsic to the right to life and personal liberty guaranteed under Article 21, and also flows from other freedoms. Hence privacy is anchored primarily in Article 21.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text:
What are the duties of the Chief of Defence Staff (CDS) as Head of the Department of Military Affairs?
1. Permanent Chairman of Chiefs of Staff Committee.
2. Exercise military command over the three Service Chiefs.
3. Principal Military Advisor to Defence Minister on all tri- service matters.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanation: The Chief of Defence Staff serves as the permanent chairman of the Chiefs of Staff Committee and acts as the principal military adviser to the Defence Minister on tri-service matters. However, the CDS does not exercise operational military command over the individual Service Chiefs, so only statements 1 and 3 hold.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text: Operations undertaken by the Army towards upliftment of the local population in remote areas to include addressing of their basic needs is called :
questions.explanation: Operation Sadbhavana is the Army's humanitarian initiative aimed at winning goodwill by meeting basic needs of local populations in remote and border areas through education, healthcare and infrastructure. The other named operations relate to different military or diplomatic purposes.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text: The longest border between any two countries in the world is between :
questions.option_a: Canada and the United States of America
questions.explanation: The frontier between Canada and the United States, stretching roughly 8,900 kilometres including the Alaska stretch, is the longest international boundary between two countries. The other pairs listed share shorter borders by comparison.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text:
Which of the following statements about the Ethics Committee in the Lok Sabha are correct?
1. Initially it was an ad- hoc Committee.
2. Only a Member of the Lok Sabha can make a complaint relating to unethical conduct of a member of the Lok Sabha.
3. This Committee cannot take up any matter which is sub- judice.
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanation: The Lok Sabha Ethics Committee began as an ad hoc body before becoming permanent, and it does not entertain matters that are sub judice, so statements 1 and 3 are right. Statement 2 is wrong because a complaint about a member's conduct need not come only from another member of the House.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text: Who was the Provisional President of the Constituent Assembly before Dr. Rajendra Prasad took over ?
questions.explanation: Dr. Sachchidananda Sinha, as the eldest member, presided over the Constituent Assembly's first sitting in December 1946 as its temporary or provisional chairman. Dr. Rajendra Prasad was subsequently elected the permanent President.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text:
With reference to the Government of India Act, 1935, consider the following statements :
1. It provided for the establishment of an All India Federation based on the union of the British Indian Provinces and Princely States.
2. Defence and Foreign Affairs were kept under the control of the federal legislature.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?
questions.explanation: The Government of India Act, 1935 proposed an all-India federation uniting the British provinces with the princely states, so statement 1 is correct. Statement 2 is wrong because Defence and Foreign Affairs were reserved subjects kept under the Governor-General's control, not entrusted to the federal legislature.

questions.subject: Art & Culture
questions.text: Which one of the following is a work attributed to playwright Bhasa ?
questions.explanation: Madhyama-vyayoga is one of the plays credited to the ancient dramatist Bhasa. The other titles belong elsewhere: Kavyalankara to Bhamaha, the Natyashastra to Bharata, and the Mahabhashya to Patanjali.

questions.subject: Art & Culture
questions.text: Sanghabhuti, an Indian Buddhist monk, who travelled to China at the end of the fourth century AD, was the author of a commentary on :
questions.explanation: Sanghabhuti, who journeyed to China late in the fourth century, is remembered for a commentary on the Sarvastivada Vinaya, a monastic disciplinary text. The other works listed are unrelated to his authorship.

questions.subject: Art & Culture
questions.text:
Consider the following properties included in the World Heritage List released by UNESCO :
1. Shantiniketan
2. Rani-ki-Vav
3. Sacred Ensembles of the Hoysalas
4. Mahabodhi Temple Complex at Bodhgaya
How many of the above properties were included in 2023 ?
questions.explanation: Of the four properties, Shantiniketan and the Sacred Ensembles of the Hoysalas were the two inscribed on the World Heritage List in 2023. Rani-ki-Vav was added in 2014 and the Mahabodhi Temple Complex back in 2002, so only two belong to 2023.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text:
As per Article 368 of the Constitution of India, the Parliament may amend any provision of the Constitution by way of :
1. Addition
3. Variation
3. Repeal
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanation: Article 368 states that Parliament may amend the Constitution by adding to, varying, or repealing any provision, subject to the prescribed procedure. Since all three modes are expressly mentioned, every listed option is correct.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Consider the following countries :
1. Italy
2. Japan
3. Nigeria
4. South Korea
5. South Africa
Which of the above countries are frequently mentioned in the media for their low birth rates, or ageing population of declining population ?
questions.explanation: Italy, Japan and South Korea are commonly cited for shrinking, ageing populations driven by very low fertility. Nigeria and South Africa, by contrast, have relatively young and growing populations, so they are excluded, leaving 1, 2 and 4.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text:
Which of the following statements are correct in respect of a Money Bill in the Parliament?
1. Article 109 mentions special procedure in respect of Money Bills.
2. A Money Bill shall not be introduced in the Council of States.
3. The Rajya Sabha can either approve the Bill or suggest changes but cannot reject it.
4. Amendment to a Money Bill suggested by the Rajya Sabha have to be accepted by the Lok Sabha.
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanation: Article 109 lays down the special procedure for Money Bills, which cannot be introduced in the Rajya Sabha, and the upper House may only recommend changes without power to reject, so statements 1, 2 and 3 are correct. Statement 4 is wrong because the Lok Sabha is free to accept or reject the Rajya Sabha's suggestions.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
Which of the following is/are correctly matched in terms of equivalent rank in the three services of Indian Defence forces? Army : Airforce : Navy
1. Brigadier : Air Commodore : Commander
2. Major General : Air Vice Marshal : Vice Admiral
3. Major : Squadron Leader : Lieutenant Commander
4. Lieutenant Colonel : Group Captain : Captain
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Only the third pairing is accurate: a Major corresponds to a Squadron Leader in the Air Force and a Lieutenant Commander in the Navy. The other rows mismatch equivalents, for instance a Brigadier equates to a Commodore rather than a Commander, so option D stands.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text:
The North Eastern Council (NEC) was established by the North Eastern Council Act, 1971. Subsequent to the amendment of NEC Act in 2002, the Council comprises which of the following members ?
1. Governor of the Constituent State
2. Chief Minister of the Constituent State
3. Three Members to be nominated by the President of India
4. The Home Minister of India
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanation: Following the 2002 amendment, the North Eastern Council comprises the Governors and Chief Ministers of the constituent states along with three members nominated by the President. The Union Home Minister is not listed among these members, so the correct set is 1, 2 and 3.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text:
Consider the following statements regarding ‘Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam’ :
1. Provisions will come into effect from the 18th Lok Sabha.
2. This will be in force for 15 years after becoming an Act.
3. There are provisions for the reservation of seats for Scheduled Castes Women within the quota reserved for the Scheduled Castes.
Which of the statements given above are correct ?
questions.explanation: Under the Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam, the one-third reservation for women is to remain for fifteen years, and it includes reserved seats for Scheduled Caste women within the SC quota, so statements 2 and 3 are correct. Statement 1 is wrong because implementation is tied to a delimitation exercise after the census, not automatically to the 18th Lok Sabha.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
Which of the following statements about ‘Exercise Mitra Shakti- 2023’ are correct?
1. This was a joint military exercise between India and Bangladesh.
2. It commenced in Aundh (Pune).
3. Joint response during counter- terrorism operations was a goal of this operation.
4. Indian Air Force was a part of this exercise.
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanation: Exercise Mitra Shakti 2023 was actually a joint drill between India and Sri Lanka, not Bangladesh, so statement 1 is wrong. It began at Aundh in Pune, focused on counter-terrorism cooperation, and involved the Indian Air Force, making 2, 3 and 4 correct.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text: A Writ of Prohibition is an order issued by the Supreme Court or High Courts to :
questions.option_a: a government officer prohibiting him from taking a particular action.
questions.option_b: the Parliament/ Legislative Assembly to pass a law on Prohibition.
questions.option_c: the lower court prohibiting continuation of proceedings in a case.
questions.option_d: the Government prohibiting it from following an unconstitutional policy.
questions.explanation: A writ of prohibition is issued by a higher court to a subordinate court or tribunal to halt proceedings that exceed its jurisdiction or violate natural justice. It is preventive, stopping the lower forum before it acts, which matches option C.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text:
Consider the following statements :
1. It is the Governor of the State who recognizes and declares any community of that State as a Scheduled Tribe.
2. A community declared as a Scheduled Tribe in a State need not to be so in another State.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?
questions.explanation: It is the President, not the Governor, who specifies Scheduled Tribes for a state, so statement 1 is incorrect. Statement 2 is right because a community recognised as a Scheduled Tribe in one state is not automatically so in another, making option B correct.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text:
With reference to Union Budget, consider the following statements :
1. The Union Finance Minister on behalf of the Prime Minister lays the Annual Financial Statement before both the Houses of Parliament.
2. At the Union level, no demand for a grant can be made except on the recommendation of the President of India.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is wrong because the Annual Financial Statement is laid before Parliament by the Finance Minister on behalf of the President, not the Prime Minister. Statement 2 is correct, as no demand for a grant can be made except on the President's recommendation, so option B holds.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text: Who of the following is the author of the books “The India Way” and “Why Bharat Matters”?
questions.explanation: Both The India Way and Why Bharat Matters were authored by S. Jaishankar, the External Affairs Minister, reflecting his views on India's foreign policy and global role. The other names listed are not the authors of these books.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
Consider the following pairs :
Country
Reason for being in the news
1. Argentina Worst economic crisis
2. Sudan War between the country’s regular and paramilitary forces
3. Turkey Rescinded its membership of NATO
How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.explanation: Argentina's severe economic crisis and Sudan's conflict between its regular army and paramilitary RSF were both genuine reasons for those countries being in the news, so two pairs match. The Turkey pairing is wrong because it did not withdraw from NATO, leaving only two correct.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
Consider the following statements :
Statement-I : Sumed pipeline is a strategic route for Persian Gulf oil and natural gas shipments to Europe.
Statement-II : Sumed pipeline connects the Red Sea with the Mediterranean Sea.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements ?
questions.option_a: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement- II explains Statement-I
questions.option_b: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct, but Statement-II does not explain Statement-I
questions.option_c: Statement-I is correct, but Statement-II is incorrect
questions.option_d: Statement-I is incorrect, but Statement-II is correct
questions.explanation: The SUMED pipeline runs across Egypt linking the Gulf of Suez on the Red Sea to the Mediterranean, offering an alternative to the Suez Canal for moving Persian Gulf oil toward Europe. Since the pipeline's Red Sea to Mediterranean route is precisely what makes it strategic, Statement II explains Statement I.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Consider the following statements :
1. The Red Sea receives very little precipitation in any form.
2. No water enters the Red Sea from rivers.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?
questions.explanation: The Red Sea lies in an arid belt and receives very little rainfall, so statement 1 is correct. The intended answer treats statement 2 as not fully accurate, since it is the extreme scarcity of rain and absence of significant permanent river inflow, combined with high evaporation, that gives the sea its high salinity, so only statement 1 is marked correct.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text: According to the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), which one of the following is the largest source of sulphur dioxide emissions ?
questions.explanation: According to the US EPA, the burning of fossil fuels at electricity-generating power plants is the single largest source of sulphur dioxide emissions. Industrial processes and transport contribute far less, so power plants are the leading source.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
Consider the following statements :
Statement-I : If the United States of America (USA) were to default on its debt, holders of US Treasury Bonds will not be able to exercise their claims to receive payment.
Statement-II : The USA Government debt is not backed by any hard assets, but only by the faith of the Government.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements ?
questions.option_a: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement- II explains Statement-I
questions.option_b: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct, but Statement-II does not explain Statement-I
questions.option_c: Statement-I is correct, but Statement-II is incorrect
questions.option_d: Statement-I is incorrect, but Statement-II is correct
questions.explanation: US Treasury bonds are backed only by the government's promise to pay rather than by any tangible assets, so a default would leave bondholders unable to enforce their claims. Because this reliance on faith is exactly why a default would block payment, Statement II explains Statement I.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
Consider the following statements :
Statement-I : Syndicated lending spreads the risk of borrower default across multiple lenders.
Statement-II : The syndicated loan can be a fixed amount/lump sum of funds, but cannot be a credit line.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements ?
questions.option_a: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement- II explains Statement-I
questions.option_b: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct, but Statement-II does not explain Statement-I
questions.option_c: Statement-I is correct, but Statement-II is incorrect
questions.option_d: Statement-I is incorrect, but Statement-II is correct
questions.explanation: Statement I is correct because spreading a large loan across a group of lenders distributes the risk of borrower default. Statement II is wrong, since a syndicated facility can in fact take the form of a revolving credit line and is not limited to a single lump-sum amount.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
Consider the following statements in respect of the digital rupee:
1. It is a sovereign currency issued by the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) in alignment with its monetary policy.
2. It appears as a liability on the RBI’s balance sheet.
3. It is insured against inflation by its very design.
4. It is freely convertible against commercial bank money and cash.
Which of the statements given above are correct ?
questions.explanation: The digital rupee is a sovereign currency issued by the RBI in line with monetary policy, appears as a liability on the central bank's balance sheet, and is freely convertible with cash and bank deposits, so statements 1, 2 and 4 are correct. Statement 3 is wrong because it offers no built-in protection against inflation.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text:
With reference to ancient India, Gautama Buddha was generally known by which of the following epithets ?
1. Nayaputta
2. Shakyamuni
3. Tathagata
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.option_d: None of the above are epithets of Gautama Buddha
questions.explanation: Shakyamuni, meaning sage of the Shakya clan, and Tathagata are well-known epithets of the Buddha, so statements 2 and 3 are right. Nayaputta refers to Mahavira of the Jnatrika clan, not the Buddha, so statement 1 does not belong.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text:
Consider the following information :
Archaeological Site State Description
1. Chandraketugarh Odisha Trading Port town
2. Inamgaon Maharashtra Chalcolithic site
3. Mangadu Kerala Megalithic site
4. Salihundam Andhra Pradesh Rock-cut cave shrines In which of the above rows is given information correctly matched ?
questions.explanation: The correct matches are Inamgaon in Maharashtra as a Chalcolithic site and Mangadu in Kerala as a megalithic site. Chandraketugarh actually lies in West Bengal, not Odisha, and Salihundam in Andhra Pradesh is a Buddhist stupa site rather than rock-cut shrines, so only rows 2 and 3 fit.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text: Who of the following rulers of medieval India gave permission to the Portuguese to build a fort at Bhatkal ?
questions.explanation: The Vijayanagara ruler Krishnadevaraya permitted the Portuguese to build a fort at Bhatkal on the Karnataka coast, reflecting the trading and strategic ties of that period. The other rulers listed were not responsible for this concession.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text:
With reference to revenue collection by Cornwallis, consider the following statements :
1. Under the Ryotwari Settlement of revenue collection, the peasants were exempted from revenue payment in case of bad harvests or natural calamities.
2. Under the Permanent Settlement in Bengal, if the Zamindar failed to pay his revenues to the state, on or before the fixed date, he would be removed from his Zamindari.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is wrong because the Ryotwari system was not Cornwallis's design and did not exempt peasants during poor harvests. Statement 2 is correct, as the Permanent Settlement's sunset clause meant a zamindar who failed to pay revenue by the fixed date could lose his zamindari, so only statement 2 holds.

questions.subject: Art & Culture
questions.text:
Consider the following statements :
1. There are no parables in Upanishads.
2. Upanishads were composed earlier than the Puranas.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect because the Upanishads do contain parables and illustrative stories used to convey philosophical ideas. Statement 2 is correct, since the Upanishads were composed well before the Puranas, so only statement 2 is right.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
Consider the following statements :
1. India is a member of the International Grains Council.
2. A country needs to be a member of the International Grains Council for exporting or importing rice and wheat.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?
questions.explanation: India is a member of the International Grains Council, so statement 1 is correct. Statement 2 is wrong because membership of the Council is not a precondition for a country to export or import rice or wheat, leaving only statement 1 accurate.

questions.subject: Art & Culture
questions.text: Which one of the following was the latest inclusion in the Intangible Cultural Heritage List of UNESCO ?
questions.explanation: The Garba dance of Gujarat was the most recent Indian entry added to UNESCO's Representative List of Intangible Cultural Heritage in 2023. The other listed traditions were inscribed earlier.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
Consider the following statements :
Statement-I : There is instability and worsening security situation in the Sahel region.
Statement-II : There have been military takeovers/coups detat in several countries of the Sahel region in the recent past.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements ?
questions.option_a: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement- II explains Statement-I
questions.option_b: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct, but Statement-II does not explain Statement-I
questions.option_c: Statement-I is correct, but Statement-II is incorrect
questions.option_d: Statement-I is incorrect, but Statement-II is correct
questions.explanation: The Sahel has seen worsening instability and insecurity, and this is closely tied to a series of military coups in countries such as Mali, Burkina Faso and Niger. Since the takeovers directly contribute to the deteriorating security, Statement II explains Statement I.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
Consider the following statements :
Statement-I : India does not import apples from the United States of America.
Statement-II : In India, the law prohibits the import of Genetically Modified food without the approval of the competent authority.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements ?
questions.option_a: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement- II explains Statement-I
questions.option_b: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct, but Statement-II does not explain Statement-I
questions.option_c: Statement-I is correct, but Statement-II is incorrect
questions.option_d: Statement-I is incorrect, but Statement-II is correct
questions.explanation: Statement I is incorrect because India does import apples from the United States. Statement II is correct, since Indian law bars the import of genetically modified food without clearance from the competent regulatory authority, so option D applies.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text:
With reference to the Speaker of the Lok Sabha, consider the following statements : While any resolution for the removal of the Speaker of the Lok Sabha is under consideration
1. He/She shall not preside.
2. He/She shall not have the right to speak
3. He/She shall not be entitled to vote on the resolution in the first instance.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?
questions.explanation: While a resolution to remove the Speaker is under consideration, the Speaker cannot preside over that sitting, so statement 1 is correct. However, the Speaker retains the right to speak in and take part in the proceedings and may vote in the first instance, so statements 2 and 3 are wrong.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text:
With reference to the Indian Parliament, consider the following statements :
1. A bill pending in the Lok Sabha lapses on its dissolution.
2. A bill passed by the Lok Sabha and pending in the Rajya Sabha lapses on the dissolution of the Lok Sabha.
3. A bill in regard to which the President of India notified his/her intention to summon the Houses to a joint sitting lapses on the dissolution of the Lok Sabha.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?
questions.explanation: A Bill pending in the Lok Sabha lapses when the House is dissolved, and a Bill passed by the Lok Sabha but still pending in the Rajya Sabha also lapses, so statements 1 and 2 are correct. Statement 3 is wrong because a Bill for which the President has notified a joint sitting does not lapse on dissolution.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text:
With reference to the Parliament of India, consider the following statements :
1. Prorogation of a House by the President of India does not require the advice of the Council of Ministers.
2. Prorogation of a House is generally done after the House is adjourned sine die but there is no bar to the President of India prorogating the House which is in session.
3. Dissolution of the Lok Sabha is done by the President of India who, save in exceptional circumstances, does so on the advice of the Council of Ministers.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is wrong because prorogation is done by the President on the advice of the Council of Ministers. Statements 2 and 3 are correct, since prorogation usually follows adjournment sine die yet may occur while a House is in session, and dissolution of the Lok Sabha is ordinarily done on ministerial advice, so option C holds.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
Consider the following statements :
Statement-I : The European Parliament approved The Net-Zero Industry Act recently.
Statement-II : The European Union intends to achieve carbon neutrality by 2040 and therefore aims to develop all of its own clean technology by that time.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements ?
questions.option_a: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement- II explains Statement-I
questions.option_b: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct, but Statement-II does not explain Statement-I
questions.option_c: Statement-I is correct, but Statement-II is incorrect
questions.option_d: Statement-I is incorrect, but Statement-II is correct
questions.explanation: Statement I is correct, as the European Parliament did approve the Net-Zero Industry Act. Statement II is wrong because the EU's headline target for carbon neutrality is 2050 rather than 2040, and it does not aim to produce every clean technology domestically, so only Statement I is right.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
Consider the following statements :
Statement-I : Recently, Venezuela has achieved a rapid recovery from its economic crisis and succeeded in preventing its people from fleeing/emigrating to other countries.
Statement-II : Venezuela has the world’s largest oil reserves.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements ?
questions.option_a: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement- II explains Statement-I
questions.option_b: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct, but Statement-II does not explain Statement-I
questions.option_c: Statement-I is correct, but Statement-II is incorrect
questions.option_d: Statement-I is incorrect, but Statement-II is correct
questions.explanation: Statement I is incorrect because Venezuela has not achieved a swift recovery and large numbers of its people have continued to emigrate amid the crisis. Statement II is correct, as Venezuela holds the world's largest proven crude oil reserves, so option D applies.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
With reference to the Digital India Land Records Modernisation Programme, consider the following statements :
1. To implement the scheme, the Central Government provides 100% funding.
2. Under the Scheme, Cadastral Maps are digitized.
3. An initiative has been undertaken to transliterate the Records of Rights from local language to any of the languages recognized by the Constitution of India.
Which of the statements given above are correct ?
questions.explanation: The Digital India Land Records Modernisation Programme is fully funded by the Centre, involves digitisation of cadastral maps, and includes transliterating Records of Rights into constitutionally recognised languages. All three statements accurately describe the programme.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
With reference to the ‘Pradhan Mantri Surakshit Matritva Abhiyan’, consider the following statements :
1. This scheme guarantees a minimum package of antenatal care services to women in their second and third trimesters of pregnancy and six months post-delivery health care service in any government health facility.
2. Under this scheme, private sector health care providers of certain specialities can volunteer to provide services at nearby government health facilities.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is wrong because the Pradhan Mantri Surakshit Matritva Abhiyan provides free antenatal check-ups on a fixed day each month rather than guaranteeing six months of post-delivery care. Statement 2 is correct, as private specialists may volunteer their services at government facilities, so only statement 2 holds.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
With reference to the Pradhan Mantri Shram Yogi Maan-dhan (PM- SYM) Yojana, consider the following statements :
1. The entry age group for enrolment in the shceme is 21 to 40 years.
2. Age specific contribution shall be made by the beneficiary.
3. Each subscriber under the scheme shall receive a minimum pension of Rs. 3000 per month after attaining the age of 60 years.
4. Family pension is applicable to the spouse and unmarried daughters.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?
questions.explanation: Under PM-SYM the entry age is 18 to 40 years, not 21 to 40, so statement 1 is wrong. Contributions are age-specific and the assured minimum pension is Rs 3000 per month after age 60, making statements 2 and 3 correct. Family pension covers the spouse, so statement 4 as worded is not right, leaving option B.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Consider the following statements :
Statement-I : The atmosphere is heated more by incoming solar radiation than by terrestrial radiation.
Statement-II : Carbon dioxide and other greenhouse gases in the atmosphere are good absorbers of long wave radiation.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements ?
questions.option_a: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement- II explains Statement-I
questions.option_b: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct, but Statement-II does not explain Statement-I
questions.option_c: Statement-I is correct, but Statement-II is incorrect
questions.option_d: Statement-I is incorrect, but Statement-II is correct
questions.explanation: Statement I is incorrect because the atmosphere is heated more by outgoing terrestrial long-wave radiation than by incoming solar radiation, which largely passes through to the surface. Statement II is correct, since carbon dioxide and other greenhouse gases readily absorb long-wave radiation, so option D applies.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Consider the following statements :
Statement-I Thickness of the troposphere at the equator is much greater as compared to poles.
Statement-II : At the equator, heat is transported to great heights by strong convectional currents.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements ?
questions.option_a: Bothe Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement- II explains Statement-I
questions.option_b: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct, but Statement-II does not explain Statement-I
questions.option_c: Statement-I is correct, but Statement-II is incorrect
questions.option_d: Statement-I is incorrect, but Statement-II is correct
questions.explanation: The troposphere is indeed thicker over the equator than over the poles, so Statement I is correct. This is because intense heating at the equator drives strong convection that carries heat and moisture to great altitudes, which is exactly the reason given in Statement II, so it explains Statement I.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Consider the following :
1. Pyroclastic debris
2. Ash and dust
3. Nitrogen compounds
4. Sulphur compounds
How many of the above are products of volcanic eruptions ?
questions.explanation: Volcanic eruptions release pyroclastic debris, fine ash and dust, as well as gases including nitrogen and sulphur compounds. Since all four items are genuine products of eruptions, the answer is all four.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Which of the following is/are correct inference/inferences from isothermal maps in the month of January ?
1. The isotherms deviate to the north over the ocean and to the south over the continent.
2. The presence of cold ocean currents. Gulf Stream and North Atlantic Drift make the North Atlantic Ocean colder and the isotherms bend towards the north.
Select the answer using the code given below :
questions.explanation: In January, the isotherms bend north over the oceans and south over the continents in the Northern Hemisphere, since land cools faster than water in winter, so statement 1 is correct. Statement 2 is flawed because the Gulf Stream and North Atlantic Drift are warm currents that keep that region relatively warmer, so only statement 1 holds.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text: Which of the following countries are well known as the two largest cocoa producers in the world ?
questions.explanation: Cote d'Ivoire and Ghana in West Africa together dominate global cocoa production, supplying the bulk of the world's cocoa beans. The other country pairs are not major cocoa producers.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text: With reference to the Himalayan rivers Joining the Ganga downstream of Prayagraj from West to East, which one of the following sequences is correct ?
questions.explanation: Downstream of Prayagraj, moving from west to east, the Himalayan tributaries join the Ganga in the order Gomati, then Ghaghara, then Gandak, and finally Kosi. This matches the sequence in option B.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Consider the following statements :
Statement-I: Rainfall is one of the reasons for weathering of rocks.
Statement II: Rain water contains carbon dioxide in solution. Statement-III: Rain water contains atmospheric oxygen.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements ?
questions.option_a: Both Statement-II and Statement-III are correct and both of them explain Statement-I
questions.option_b: Both Statement-II and Statement-III are correct, but only one of them explains Statement-I
questions.option_c: Only one of the Statements II and III is correct and that explains Statement-I
questions.option_d: Neither Statement- II nor Statement-Ill is correct
questions.explanation: Rainfall does contribute to weathering, so Statement I is correct. Rainwater carries dissolved carbon dioxide, enabling carbonation, and dissolved atmospheric oxygen, enabling oxidation, so both Statements II and III are true and each independently explains how rain drives chemical weathering.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Consider the following countries :
1. Finland
2. Germany
3. Norway
4. Russia How, many of the above countries have a border with the North Sea ?
questions.explanation: Among the listed countries, only Germany and Norway have coastlines along the North Sea. Finland borders the Baltic Sea and Russia does not touch the North Sea, so just two of them qualify.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Consider the following information Waterfall :
Region : River
1. Dhuandhar : Malwa : Narmada
2. Hundru : Chota Nagpur : Subarnarekha
3. Gersoppa : Western Ghats : Netravati In how many of the above rows is the given information correctly matched ?
questions.explanation: Only the Hundru falls pairing is fully correct, being on the Subarnarekha river in the Chota Nagpur region. The Dhuandhar row misplaces the region as Malwa, and the Gersoppa (Jog) falls lie on the Sharavati rather than the Netravati, so just one row matches.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Consider the following information :
Region Name of the mountain range Type of mountain
1. Central Asia Vosges Fold mountain
2. Europe Alps Block mountain
3. North America Appalachians Fold mountain
4. South America Andes Fold mountain In how many of the above rows is the information correctly matched ?
questions.explanation: The Appalachians in North America are fold mountains and the Andes in South America are also fold mountains, so those two rows are correct. The Vosges is a block mountain located in Europe, not Central Asia, and the Alps are fold mountains rather than block mountains, so only two rows match.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text: The organisms "Cicada, Froghopper and Pond skater" are :
questions.explanation: The cicada, froghopper and pond skater are all insects belonging to the order Hemiptera, the true bugs. They are neither birds, fish nor reptiles, so the correct description is insects.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
Consider the following statements :
Statement-I : Many chewing gums found in the market are considered a source of environmental pollution.
Statement-II: Many chewing gums contain plastic as gum base.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements ?
questions.option_a: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement- II explains Statement-I
questions.option_b: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct, but Statement-II does not explain Statement-I
questions.option_c: Statement-I is correct, but Statement-II is incorrect
questions.option_d: Statement-I is incorrect, but Statement-II is correct
questions.explanation: Many commercial chewing gums use a synthetic plastic-based gum base, so Statement II is correct. Because these gums do not readily biodegrade and contribute to littering and microplastic pollution, they are regarded as an environmental hazard, so Statement II explains Statement I.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
Consider the following pairs :
Country Animal found in its natural habitat
1. Brazil Indri
2. Indonesia Elk
3. Madagascar Bonobo
How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched ?
questions.explanation: None of the pairs match, since the indri is native to Madagascar rather than Brazil, and the bonobo lives in the Congo region, not Madagascar. The elk is not naturally found in Indonesia either, so all three pairings are wrong.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
Consider the following statements regarding World Toilet Organization :
1. It is one of the agencies of the United Nations.
2. World Toilet Summit, World Toilet Day and World Toilet College are the initiatives of this organization, to inspire action to tackle the global sanitation crisis.
3. The main focus of its function is to grant funds to the least developed countries and developing countries to achieve the end of open defecation.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?
questions.explanation: The World Toilet Organization is a non-governmental body, not a UN agency, so statement 1 is wrong, and it does not function primarily to grant funds to countries, so statement 3 is wrong. It did launch the World Toilet Summit, World Toilet Day and World Toilet College, making only statement 2 correct.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
Consider the following statements :
1. Lions do not have a particular breeding season.
2. Unlike most other big cats, cheetahs do not roar.
3. Unlike male lions, male leopards do not proclaim their territory by scent marking.
Which of the statements given above are correct ?
questions.explanation: Lions breed throughout the year without a fixed season, so statement 1 is correct, and cheetahs, unlike most big cats, cannot roar, so statement 2 is correct. Statement 3 is wrong because male leopards do mark their territory with scent, leaving only 1 and 2.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text: Which one of the following is the correct description of "100 Million Farmers" ?
questions.option_a: It is a platform for accelerating the transition towards food and water systems that are net- zero (carbon), nature- positive and that aims to increase farmer resilience.
questions.option_b: It is an international alliance and a network of individuals and farming organisations interested in supporting and strengthening the development of the organic animal husbandry.
questions.option_c: It is a digital platform fully integrated with service providers and built on blockchain that lets buyers, sellers and third parties trade fertilizers quickly and securely.
questions.option_d: It is a platform with the mission of encouraging the farmers to form Farmer Product Organisations or Agribusiness Consortiums, thus facilitating the access to global open markets to sell their products.
questions.explanation: The '100 Million Farmers' initiative is a World Economic Forum platform aimed at driving the shift to food and water systems that are net-zero and nature-positive while strengthening farmer resilience. The other descriptions do not correspond to this initiative.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
Consider the following:
1. Battery storage
2. Biomass generators
3. Fuel cells
4. Rooftop solar photovoltaic units
How many of the above are considered "Distributed Energy Resources"
questions.explanation: Battery storage, biomass generators, fuel cells and rooftop solar photovoltaic units are all small-scale, decentralised sources that qualify as Distributed Energy Resources. Since each fits the definition, all four are counted.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text: Which one of the following shows a unique relationship with an insect that has coevolved with it and that is the only insect that ta pollinate this tree ?
questions.explanation: The fig shares a famously specialised mutualism with the fig wasp, which is the only insect able to pollinate it, having coevolved for that role. The other trees listed do not depend on such a single dedicated pollinator.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
Consider the following :
1. Butterflies
2. Fish
3. Frogs
How many of the above have poisonous species among them ?
questions.explanation: Poisonous or toxic species exist among butterflies, fish and frogs alike, for example certain toxic butterflies, pufferfish and poison dart frogs. Since all three groups contain such species, the answer is all three.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
Consider the following:
1. Cashew
2. Papaya
3. Red sanders
How many of the above trees are actually native to India?
questions.explanation: Of the three, only red sanders is native to India, being found in the Eastern Ghats. Cashew originates from Brazil and papaya from Central America, both introduced to India later, so just one is native.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
Consider the following airports:
1. Donyi Polo Airport
2. Kushinagar International Airport
3. Vijayawada International Airport In the recent past, which of the above have been constructed as Greenfield projects ?
questions.explanation: Donyi Polo Airport near Itanagar and Kushinagar International Airport were developed as greenfield projects on new sites. Vijayawada's airport was an upgrade of an existing facility rather than a greenfield build, so only 1 and 2 qualify.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
With reference to "water vapour", which of the following statements is/are correct ?
1. It is a gas, the amount of which decreases with altitude.
2. Its percentage is maximum at the poles.
Select the answer using the code given below :
questions.explanation: Water vapour is concentrated in the lower atmosphere and its amount decreases with altitude, so statement 1 is correct. Statement 2 is wrong because the proportion of water vapour is greatest over warm tropical and equatorial regions, not at the poles.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Consider the following description :
1. Annual and daily range of temperatures is low.
2. Precipitation occurs throughout the year.
3. Precipitation varies between 50 cm – 250 cm. What is this type of climate ?
questions.explanation: The described climate, with low annual and daily temperature ranges, precipitation spread through the year, and totals between about 50 and 250 cm, corresponds to the Marine West Coast climate. Its moderating maritime influence produces exactly these features.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
With reference to "Coriolis force", which of the following statements is/are correct ?
1. It increases with increase in wind velocity.
2. It is maximum at the poles and is absent at the equator.
Select the answer using the code given below :
questions.explanation: The Coriolis force grows stronger as wind velocity increases, so statement 1 is correct. It also varies with latitude, being maximum at the poles and effectively absent at the equator, so statement 2 is correct as well, making both right.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
On June 21 every year, which of the following latitude(s) experience(s) a sunlight of more than 12 hours ?
1. Equator
2. Tropic of Cancer
3. Tropic of Capricorn
4. Arctic Circle
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanation: On June 21, the summer solstice, the Sun is overhead near the Tropic of Cancer, so the Tropic of Cancer and the Arctic Circle both experience more than twelve hours of daylight. The equator gets exactly twelve hours, and the Tropic of Capricorn gets less, so the answer is 2 and 4.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
One of the following regions has the world’s largest tropical peatland, which holds about three years worth of global carbon emissions from fossil fuels; and the possible destruction of which can exert detrimental effect on the global climate.
Which one of the following denot that region ?
questions.explanation: The Congo Basin's Cuvette Centrale holds the world's largest tropical peatland, storing carbon equivalent to several years of global fossil fuel emissions. Disturbing it would release vast amounts of carbon, harming the global climate, so the region is the Congo Basin.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
With reference to perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS) that are used in making many consumer product, consider the following statements :
1. PFAS are found to be widespread in drinking water, food and food packaging material.
2. PFAS are not easily degraded in the environment.
3. Persistent exposure to PFAS can lead to bioaccumulation in animal bodies.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: PFAS are widespread in drinking water, food and food packaging, resist breakdown in the environment, earning them the name 'forever chemicals', and can bioaccumulate in living organisms with continued exposure. All three statements are therefore correct.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
Consider the following :
1. Carabid beetles
2. Centipedes
3. Flies
4. Termites
5. Wasps Parasitoid species are found how many of the above kind of organisms?
questions.explanation: Parasitoid species are found among carabid beetles, flies and wasps, which include insects that develop on or in a host, eventually killing it. Centipedes and termites are not known for parasitoid habits, so three of the groups qualify.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
Consider the following plants
1. Groundnut
2. Horse-gram
3. Soybean
How many of the above belong to the pea family ?
questions.explanation: Groundnut, horse-gram and soybean are all legumes belonging to the pea family, Fabaceae, and are nitrogen-fixing crops. Since all three are members of this family, the answer is all three.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
Consider the following statements
Statement-I:
The Indian Flying Fox is placed under the “vermin” category in the Wild Life (Protection) Act, 1972.
Statement-II: The Indian Flying Fox feeds on the blood of other animals.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements ?
questions.option_a: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement- II explains Statement-I
questions.option_b: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct, but Statement-II does not explain Statement-I.
questions.option_c: Statement-I is correct, but Statement-II is incorrect
questions.option_d: Statement-l is incorrect, but Statement-II is correct.
questions.explanation: Statement I is correct because the Indian Flying Fox is listed as vermin under Schedule V of the Wild Life (Protection) Act, 1972. Statement II is wrong, since this bat is a fruit-eater and does not feed on blood, so option C applies.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text: The total fertility rate in a economy is defined as :
questions.option_a: the number of children born per 1000 people in the population in a year.
questions.option_b: the number of children born to couple in their lifetime in a given population.
questions.option_c: the birth rate minus death rate.
questions.option_d: the average number of live births a woman would have by the end of her child- bearing age.
questions.explanation: The total fertility rate is the average number of live births a woman would be expected to have over her entire reproductive span if prevailing age-specific rates continued. This differs from the crude birth rate or natural increase, so option D is the accurate definition.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
Consider the following statements:
1. In India, Non- Banking Financial Companies can access the Liquidity Adjustment Facility window of the Reserve Bank of India.
2. In India, Foreign Institutional Investors can hold the Government Securities (G-Secs).
3. In India, Stock Exchanges can offer separate trading platforms for debts.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: NBFCs cannot access the RBI's Liquidity Adjustment Facility, so statement 1 is wrong. Foreign Institutional Investors are permitted to hold government securities, and stock exchanges may run dedicated debt trading platforms, so statements 2 and 3 are correct, giving option D.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
In India, which of the following can trade in Corporate Bonds and Government Securities?
1. Insurance Companies
2. Pension Funds
3. Retail Investors
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Insurance companies, pension funds and retail investors are all permitted to trade in corporate bonds and government securities in India, using the available market platforms. Since all three categories can participate, the answer is 1, 2 and 3.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
Consider the following:
1. Exchange-Traded Funds (ETF)
2. Motor vehicles
3. Currency swap
Which of the above is/are considered financial instruments?
questions.explanation: Exchange-Traded Funds and currency swaps are financial instruments, as they represent tradable financial claims or contracts. A motor vehicle is a physical asset rather than a financial instrument, so only 1 and 3 qualify.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
With reference to the sectors of the Indian economy, consider the following pairs: Economic activity Sector
1. Storage of agricultural produce Secondary
2. Dairy farm Primary
3. Mineral exploration Tertiary
4. Weaving cloth Secondary
How many of the pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.explanation: Dairy farming is a primary sector activity and weaving cloth is a secondary sector activity, so those pairs are correct, and mineral exploration as a service-oriented activity is treated as tertiary. Storage of agricultural produce is a tertiary rather than secondary activity, so that pair is wrong, leaving three correct matches.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
Consider the following materials:
1. Agricultural residues
2. Corn grain
3. Wastewater treatment sludge
4. Wood mill waste
Which of the above can be used as feedstock for producing Sustainable Aviation Fuel?
questions.explanation: Agricultural residues, corn grain, wastewater treatment sludge and wood mill waste can all serve as feedstocks for producing Sustainable Aviation Fuel through various conversion pathways. Since all four are usable, the answer covers every option.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
With reference to physical capital in Indian economy, consider the following pairs: Items Category
1. Farmer's plough Working capital
2. Computer Fixed capital
3. Yarn used by the weaver Fixed capital
4. Petrol Working capital
How many of the above pairs are correctly matched?
questions.explanation: A computer is fixed capital and petrol is working capital since it is used up in production, so those two pairs are correct. A farmer's plough is a durable tool and thus fixed capital, not working, and yarn used by a weaver is working capital rather than fixed, so only two pairs match.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text: Which one of the following words/phrases is most appropriately used to denote "an interoperable network of 3D virtual worlds that can be accessed simultaneously by millions of users, who can exert property rights over virtual items"?
questions.explanation: The term metaverse denotes an interoperable network of persistent 3D virtual worlds accessed simultaneously by many users, who can hold and trade ownership rights over virtual items. This matches the description precisely, unlike the other terms.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
With reference to the rule/rules imposed by the Reserve Bank of India while treating foreign banks, consider the following statements:
1. There is no minimum capital requirement for wholly owned banking subsidiaries in India.
2. For wholly owned banking subsidiaries in India, at least 50% of the board members should be Indian nationals.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is wrong because the RBI does impose a minimum capital requirement on wholly owned banking subsidiaries of foreign banks. Statement 2 is correct, as at least half of the board members of such subsidiaries must be Indian nationals, so only statement 2 holds.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
With reference to Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) rules in India, consider the following statements:
1. CSR rules specify that expenditures that benefit the company directly or its employees will not be considered as CSR activities.
2. CSR rules do not specify minimum spending on CSR activities.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct because CSR rules exclude expenditure that directly benefits the company or its own employees from qualifying as CSR activity. Statement 2 is wrong, since the rules do prescribe a minimum spend of two percent of average net profits, so only statement 1 is right.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text:
With reference to radioisotope thermoelectric generators (RTGs), consider the following statements:
1. RTGs are miniature fission reactors.
2. RTGs are used for powering the onboard systems of spacecrafts.
3. RTGs can use Plutonium-238, which is a by-product of weapons development
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: RTGs are not fission reactors; they generate electricity from the heat of natural radioactive decay, so statement 1 is wrong. They are used to power onboard spacecraft systems and can use Plutonium-238 linked to weapons-related processes, so statements 2 and 3 are correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text:
Consider the following statements:
Statement-I: Giant stars live much longer than dwarf stars.
Statement-II: Compared to dwarf stars, giant stars have a greater rate of nuclear reactions.
Which one of the following is correct in respect of the above statements?
questions.option_a: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct and Statement- I Statement-II explains
questions.option_b: Both Statement-I and Statement-II are correct, but Statement-II does not explain Statement-I
questions.option_c: Statement-I is correct, but Statement-II is incorrect
questions.option_d: Statement-I is incorrect, but Statement-II is correct
questions.explanation: Statement I is incorrect because giant stars actually have much shorter lifespans than small dwarf stars, burning through their fuel rapidly. Statement II is correct, as giant stars sustain a far higher rate of nuclear reactions, which is precisely why they live shorter, so option D applies.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text: Which one of the following is synthesised in human body that dilates blood vessels and increases blood flow?
questions.explanation: Nitric oxide is produced within the human body and acts as a signalling molecule that relaxes and widens blood vessels, thereby increasing blood flow. The other nitrogen oxides listed do not serve this physiological vasodilatory role.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text:
Consider the following activities:
1. Identification of narcotics on passengers at airports or in aircraft
2. Monitoring of precipitation
3. Tracking the migration of animals In how many of the above activities can the radars be used?
questions.explanation: Radars are well suited to monitoring precipitation, as in weather radar, and to tracking the movement and migration of animals. Detecting narcotics on passengers relies on chemical and imaging methods rather than radar, so radar applies to two of the three activities.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text:
Consider the following aircraft:
1. Rafael
2. MiG-29
3. Tejas MK-1
How many of the above are considered fifth generation fighter aircraft?
questions.explanation: Rafale, MiG-29 and Tejas MK-1 are all fourth or 4.5 generation fighters, not fifth generation aircraft, which are defined by stealth and advanced sensor fusion. Since none of them meets that standard, the answer is none.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text:
In which of the following are hydrogels used?
1. Controlled drug delivery in patients
2. Mobile air- conditioning systems
3. Preparation of industrial lubricants
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Hydrogels are used for controlled drug delivery in patients, in certain mobile air-conditioning and cooling systems, and in preparing industrial lubricants due to their water-retaining and gel-like properties. All three applications are valid.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text: Which one of the following is the exhaust pipe emission from Fuel Cell Electric Vehicles, powered by hydrogen?
questions.explanation: A hydrogen-powered fuel cell electric vehicle combines hydrogen with oxygen to generate electricity, emitting only water vapour from its tailpipe. It produces no carbon or harmful exhaust gases, so the emission is water vapour.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text: Recently, the term "pumped-storage hydropower" is actually and appropriately discussed in the context of which one of the following?
questions.explanation: Pumped-storage hydropower stores energy by pumping water to an upper reservoir when power is cheap and releasing it to generate electricity during peak demand, making it a form of long-duration energy storage. It is discussed in the context of grid-scale storage rather than irrigation.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text: "Membrane Bioreactors" are often discussed in the context of:
questions.explanation: Membrane Bioreactors combine biological treatment with membrane filtration and are widely used in wastewater treatment to produce clean, reusable water. They are not associated with reproductive, drug delivery or vaccine technologies.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text: With reference to the Indian economy, "Collateral Borrowing and Lending Obligations are the instruments of:
questions.explanation: Collateralised Borrowing and Lending Obligation is a short-term, collateral-backed money market instrument used for borrowing and lending funds against securities. It therefore belongs to the money market rather than the bond, forex or stock markets.

papers.source_note: Real questions from UPSC Civil Services Prelims 2023 (GS Paper I), a public government examination paper. Explanations are AIVORA-authored and original; not copied from any coaching material.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text: In which one of the following regions was Dhanyakataka, which flourished as a prominent Buddhist centre under the Mahasanghikas, located?
questions.explanation: Dhanyakataka, identified with the Amaravati region in the lower Krishna valley, lay in ancient Andhra and became a major seat of the Mahasanghika school of Buddhism. Gandhara (north-west), Kalinga (Odisha) and Magadha (Bihar) belong to entirely different regions, so Andhra is the correct location.

questions.subject: Art & Culture
questions.text:
With reference to ancient India, consider the following statements:
1. The concept of Stupa is Buddhist in origin.
2. Stupa was generally a repository of relics.
3. Stupa was a votive and commemorative structure in Buddhist tradition.
How many of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: The stupa as a burial mound predates Buddhism and was later adopted and popularised by it, so calling the concept itself 'Buddhist in origin' is not accurate. Statements 2 and 3 are sound: stupas typically enshrined relics and served as votive and commemorative monuments. Hence only two statements hold true.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text: With reference to ancient South India, Korkai, Poompuhar and Muchiri were well known as
questions.explanation: Korkai, Poompuhar (Kaveripattinam) and Muchiri (Muziris) were flourishing coastal trading harbours of the Sangam-age Tamil country, active in maritime commerce including trade with Rome. They were ports rather than capitals, iron-working centres or Jain shrines.

questions.subject: Art & Culture
questions.text: Which one of the following explains the practice of 'Vattakirutal' as mentioned in Sangam poems?
questions.option_b: Learned persons assembling in royal courts to discuss religious and philosophical matters
questions.option_c: Young girls keeping watch over agricultural fields and driving away birds and animals
questions.option_d: A king defeated in a battle committing ritual suicide by starving himself to death
questions.explanation: In Sangam literature, 'Vattakirutal' describes a defeated king ending his life through ritual fasting unto death as a response to dishonour in battle. The other choices, such as women bodyguards or field-watching girls, do not correspond to this practice.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text:
Consider the following dynasties:
1. Hoysala
2. Gahadavala
3. Kakatiya
4. Yadava
How many of the above dynasties established their kingdoms in early eighth century AD?
questions.explanation: The Hoysalas, Gahadavalas, Kakatiyas and Yadavas all rose to prominence much later, roughly between the tenth and thirteenth centuries. None of them founded their kingdoms in the early eighth century AD, so the correct count is none.

questions.subject: Art & Culture
questions.text:
With reference to ancient Indian History, consider the following pairs: Literary work Author 1.Devichandragupta : Bilhana 2.Hammira-Mahakavya : Nayachandra Suri 3.Milinda-panha : Nagarjuna 4.Nitivakyamrita : Somadeva Suri
How many of the above pairs are correctly matched?
questions.explanation: Hammira-Mahakavya was indeed composed by Nayachandra Suri, and Nitivakyamrita by Somadeva Suri, so these two pairs match. Devichandragupta is attributed to Vishakhadatta rather than Bilhana, and Milinda-panha records a dialogue with the monk Nagasena, not Nagarjuna, making only two pairs correct.

questions.subject: Art & Culture
questions.text: "Souls are not only the property of animal and plant life, but also of rocks, running water and many other natural objects not looked on as living by other religious sects." The above statement reflects one of the core beliefs of which one of the following religious sects of ancient India?
questions.explanation: The belief that souls reside not only in animals and plants but also in stones, water and other natural objects reflects Jaina thought, with its doctrine that life and soul pervade even elemental matter. This animistic view underlies Jaina non-violence and distinguishes it from the other sects listed.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text: Who among the following rulers of Vijayanagara Empire constructed a large dam across Tungabhadra River and a canal-cum-aqueduct several kilometres long from the river to the capital city?
questions.explanation: Devaraya I of the Vijayanagara Empire is credited with building a dam on the Tungabhadra and a long canal-cum-aqueduct carrying water to the capital, improving irrigation and urban supply. The other rulers named are not associated with this hydraulic project.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text: Who among the following rulers of medieval Gujarat surrendered Diu to Portuguese?
questions.explanation: Bahadur Shah of Gujarat, pressed by Mughal advances, ceded Diu to the Portuguese in the 1530s. Earlier rulers like Ahmad Shah and Mahmud Begarha are not linked with surrendering Diu, making Bahadur Shah the correct answer.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text: By which one of the following Acts was the Governor General of Bengal designated as the Governor General of India?
questions.explanation: The Charter Act of 1833 redesignated the Governor-General of Bengal as the Governor-General of India, creating a single centralised authority. The Regulating Act and Pitt's India Act came earlier and did not make this specific change, so 1833 is correct.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text: In essence, what does 'Due Process of Law' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Due process of law' goes beyond merely checking whether a law was followed; it examines whether the law itself and its application are fair, reasonable and just, embodying principles of natural justice. This contrasts with the narrower 'procedure established by law' standard.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text:
Consider the following statements:
Statement-I: In India, prisons are managed by State Governments with their own rules and regulations for the day- to-day administration of prisons.
