1. The Constitution of India classifies the ministers into four ranks viz. Cabinet Minister, Minister of State with Independent Charge, Minister of State and Deputy Minister.
2. The total number of ministers in the Union Government, including the Prime Minister, shall not exceed 15 percent of the total number of members in the Lok Sabha.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?
questions.explanation: The Constitution does not itself classify ministers into ranks such as Cabinet Minister or Minister of State; this is a matter of convention, so statement 1 is wrong. The 91st Amendment inserted the rule that the total number of ministers, including the Prime Minister, cannot exceed 15 percent of the strength of the Lok Sabha, making statement 2 correct.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text:
Which of the following is/are the exclusive power(s) of Lok Sabha?
1. To ratify the declaration of Emergency
2. To pass a motion of no-confidence against the Council of Ministers
3. To impeach the President of India
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: A no-confidence motion can only be moved in and passed by the Lok Sabha, so statement 2 is an exclusive power. Ratifying an Emergency proclamation can be done by either House, and the President's impeachment can be initiated in either House, so statements 1 and 3 are not exclusive to the Lok Sabha. Hence only statement 2 is correct.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text:
With reference to anti- defection law in India, consider the following statements:
1. The law specifies that a nominated legislator cannot join any political party within six months of being appointed to the House.
2. The law does not provide any time-frame within which the presiding officer has to decide a defection case.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?
questions.explanation: The anti-defection law does allow a nominated member to join a political party, but only within six months of taking a seat, so statement 1 is worded incorrectly. Statement 2 is correct because the Tenth Schedule prescribes no time limit within which the presiding officer must decide a defection petition, a gap that has often caused delays.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text:
Consider the following statements :
1. Attorney General of India and Solicitor General of India are the only officers of the Government who are allowed to participate in the meetings of the Parliament of India.
2. According to the Constitution of India, the Attorney General of India submits his resignation when the Government which appointed him resigns.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is wrong because the Attorney General, not the Solicitor General, enjoys the constitutional right to participate in parliamentary proceedings; the Solicitor General is a subordinate statutory law officer. Statement 2 is also wrong because the Constitution fixes no tenure for the Attorney General, who holds office during the pleasure of the President and is not constitutionally bound to resign with the government.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text:
With reference to the writs, issued by the Courts in India, consider the following statements :
1. Mandamus will not lie against a private organisation unless it is entrusted with a public duty.
2. Mandamus will not lie against a Company even though it may be a Government Company.
3. Any public minded person can be a petitioner to move the Court to obtain the writ of Quo Warranto.
Which of the statements given above are correct ?
questions.explanation: Mandamus lies against a private body only when it discharges a public duty, so statement 1 is correct. Statement 2 is wrong because mandamus can indeed be issued against a government company performing public functions. Quo warranto challenges a person's title to a public office and can be sought by any member of the public, so statement 3 is correct.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
With reference to Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission, consider the following statements :
1. Private and public hospitals must adopt it.
2. As it aims to achieve universal health coverage, every citizen of India should be part of it ultimately.
3. It has seamless portability across the country.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?
questions.explanation: Participation in the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission is voluntary for both patients and hospitals, so statements 1 and 2, which assert compulsion or universal enrolment, are wrong. The mission does create a unique health ID and digital records that can be accessed and shared across the country, giving it seamless portability, so only statement 3 is correct.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text:
With reference to Deputy Speaker of Lok Sabha, consider the following statements:
1. As per the Rules of Procedure and Conduct of Business in Lok Sabha, the election of Deputy Speaker shall be held on such date as the Speaker may fix.
2. There is a mandatory provision that the election of a candidate as Deputy Speaker of Lok Sabha shall be from either the principal opposition party or the ruling party.
3. The Deputy Speaker has the same power as of the Speaker when presiding over the sitting of the House and no appeal lies against his rulings.
4. The well established parliamentary practice regarding the appointment of Deputy Speaker is that the motion is moved by the Speaker and duly seconded by the Prime Minister.
Which of the statements given above are correct ?
questions.explanation: Under the Rules of Procedure, the date for the Deputy Speaker's election is fixed by the Speaker, so statement 1 is correct, and the Deputy Speaker while presiding exercises the same powers as the Speaker with no appeal against rulings, making statement 3 correct. There is no mandatory rule about which party the Deputy Speaker must come from, so statement 2 is wrong, and statement 4's claimed convention is inaccurate.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text: Among the following crops, which one is the most important anthropogenic source of both methane and nitrous oxide ?
questions.explanation: Flooded paddy fields create anaerobic conditions in which methanogenic microbes release large amounts of methane, while the nitrogen fertilisers applied to rice cultivation generate nitrous oxide. Because rice is a major source of both these greenhouse gases from farming, it is the correct answer among the crops listed.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
"System of Rice Intensification" of cultivation, in which alternate wetting and drying of rice fields is practised, results in :
1. Reduced seed requirement
2. Reduced methane production
3. Reduced electricity consumption
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanation: The System of Rice Intensification uses fewer, widely spaced seedlings, cutting seed requirement, so statement 1 is correct. Its alternate wetting and drying avoids continuous flooding, reducing anaerobic methane emission, so statement 2 is correct. Lower water use means less pumping and therefore reduced electricity consumption, making statement 3 correct as well, so all three hold.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text: Which one of the following lakes of West Africa has become dry and turned into a desert ?
questions.explanation: Lake Faguibine, located in Mali in West Africa, once a large lake, has dried up over decades due to drought and reduced inflow, turning into a desert-like landscape. Lake Victoria and Lake Volta remain major water bodies, so the correct answer is Lake Faguibine.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text: Gandikota canyon of South India was created by which one of the following rivers.
questions.explanation: The Gandikota gorge, often called the Grand Canyon of India, in Andhra Pradesh was carved by the Pennar (Penna) river as it cut through the Erramala hills. The other rivers listed do not form this canyon, so the Pennar is the correct answer.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Consider the following pairs :
Peak Mountains
1. Namcha Barwa — Garhwal Himalaya
2. Nanda Devi — Kumaon Himalaya
3. Nokrek — Sikkim Himalaya Which of the pairs given above is/are correctly matched ?
questions.explanation: Only the Nanda Devi and Kumaon Himalaya pairing is correct. Namcha Barwa lies in the eastern Himalaya near the Tibet–Arunachal region, not the Garhwal Himalaya, and Nokrek is a peak in the Garo Hills of Meghalaya, not the Sikkim Himalaya. Hence only pair 2 is correctly matched.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text: The term "Levant" often heard in the news roughly corresponds to which of the following regions ?
questions.option_a: Region along the eastern Mediterranean shores
questions.option_b: Region along North African shores stretching from Egypt to Morocco
questions.option_c: Region along Persian Gulf and Horn of Africa
questions.option_d: The entire coastal areas of Mediterranean Sea
questions.explanation: The Levant refers to the region bordering the eastern Mediterranean, encompassing countries such as Syria, Lebanon, Israel, Jordan and parts of neighbouring areas. It does not describe the North African coast or the Persian Gulf, so the correct answer is the region along the eastern Mediterranean shores.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Consider the following countries :
1. Azerbaijan
2. Kyrgyzstan
3. Tajikistan
4. Turkmenistan
5. Uzbekistan
Which of the above have borders with Afghanistan ?
questions.explanation: Among the Central Asian states listed, Tajikistan, Turkmenistan and Uzbekistan share land borders with Afghanistan along its northern frontier. Azerbaijan lies in the Caucasus and does not touch Afghanistan, and Kyrgyzstan is separated from it by Tajikistan, so the correct set is 3, 4 and 5.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
With reference to India, consider the following statements:
1. Monazite is a source of rare earths.
2. Monazite contains thorium.
3. Monazite occurs naturally in the entire Indian coastal sands in India.
4. In India, Government bodies only can process or export monazite.
Which of the statements given above are correct ?
questions.explanation: Monazite is an important source of rare-earth elements and also contains thorium, so statements 1 and 2 are correct. Because thorium is a prescribed nuclear substance, processing and export of monazite is reserved for authorised government bodies, making statement 4 correct. It is not found across the entire Indian coastline but concentrated in certain beach sands, so statement 3 is wrong.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text: In the northern hemisphere, the longest day of the year normally occurs in the :
questions.option_a: First half of the month of June
questions.option_b: Second half of the month of June
questions.option_c: First half of the month of July
questions.option_d: Second half of the month of July
questions.explanation: The longest day in the northern hemisphere occurs at the summer solstice, around 21 or 22 June, when the sun is directly overhead at the Tropic of Cancer. This falls in the second half of June, making that the correct answer.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Consider the following pairs :
Wetland/Lake Location
1. Hokera Wetland - Punjab
2. Renuka Wetland - Himachal Pradesh
3. Rudrasagar Lake - Tripura
4. Sasthamkotta -. Tamil Nadu Lake
How many pairs given above are correctly matched ?
questions.explanation: Only two pairs are correct. Renuka Wetland is in Himachal Pradesh and Rudrasagar Lake is in Tripura, so pairs 2 and 3 match. Hokera Wetland is in Jammu and Kashmir, not Punjab, and Sasthamkotta Lake is in Kerala, not Tamil Nadu, so pairs 1 and 4 are wrong.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text:
Consider the following:
1. Aarogya Setu
2. CoWIN
3. DigiLocker
4. DIKSHA
Which of the above are built on top of open- source digital platforms?
questions.explanation: Aarogya Setu, CoWIN, DigiLocker and DIKSHA were all developed as part of India's digital public infrastructure and are built on open-source platforms, allowing their code to be shared and reused. Since all four rest on open-source foundations, the correct answer includes all of them.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text:
With reference to Web 3.0, consider the following statements:
1. Web 3.0 technology enables people to control their own data.
2. In Web 3.0 world, there can be blockchain based social networks.
3. Web 3.0 is operated by users collectively rather than a corporation.
Which of the statements given above are correct ?
questions.explanation: Web 3.0 is envisioned as a decentralised internet where users own and control their own data, so statement 1 is correct. Being blockchain-based, it can host decentralised social networks, making statement 2 correct, and it is meant to be governed collectively by users rather than by a single corporation, so statement 3 is correct. All three statements hold.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text:
With reference to "Software as a Service (SaaS)", consider the following statements:
1. SaaS buyers can customise the user interface and can change data fields.
2. SaaS users can access their data through their mobile devices.
3. Outlook, Hotmail and Yahoo! Mail are forms of SaaS.
Which of the statements given above are correct ?
questions.explanation: In the SaaS model, users typically can configure interfaces and modify data fields within the application, so statement 1 is correct, and they can access their data over the internet from mobile devices, making statement 2 correct. Web-based email services like Outlook, Hotmail and Yahoo Mail are classic examples of software delivered as a service, so statement 3 is correct too.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text: Which one of the following statements best reflects the idea behind the "Fractional Orbital Bombardment System" often talked about in media ?
questions.option_a: A hypersonic missile is launched into space to counter the asteroid approaching the Earth and explode it in space.
questions.option_b: A spacecraft lands on another planet after making several orbital motions.
questions.option_c: A missile is put into a stable orbit around the Earth and deorbits over a target on the Earth.
questions.option_d: A spacecraft moves along a comet with the same speed and places a probe on its surface.
questions.explanation: A Fractional Orbital Bombardment System places a warhead into a low, partial orbit around the Earth and then de-orbits it to strike a target, allowing an approach from unexpected directions and evading early-warning systems. Option C, describing a missile put into orbit that then de-orbits over a target, correctly captures this concept.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text: Which one of the following-is the context in which the term "qubit" is mentioned ?
questions.explanation: A qubit, or quantum bit, is the basic unit of information in quantum computing. Unlike a classical bit that is either 0 or 1, a qubit can exist in a superposition of both states, so the term belongs to the context of quantum computing.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text:
Consider following communication the technologies:
1. Closed-circuit Television
2. Radio Frequency Identification
3. Wireless Local Area Network
Which of the above are considered Short- Range devices/technologies?
questions.explanation: All three are short-range technologies. Closed-circuit television transmits over limited local distances, RFID works only over short reading ranges, and Wireless LAN such as Wi-Fi covers a limited local area. Since each operates over short distances rather than long-range transmission, all three qualify.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text:
Consider the following statements :
1. Biofilms can form on medical implants within human tissues.
2. Biofilms can form on food and food processing surfaces.
3. Biofilms can exhibit antibiotic resistance.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: Biofilms are communities of microorganisms attached to surfaces, and they readily form on medical implants inside body tissues and on food and food-processing equipment, so statements 1 and 2 are correct. Their protective matrix makes the enclosed microbes far more resistant to antibiotics, so statement 3 is correct as well, making all three accurate.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text:
Consider the following statements in respect of probiotics:
1. Probiotics are made of both bacteria and yeast.
2. The organisms in probiotics are found in foods we ingest but they do not naturally occur in our gut.
3. Probiotics help in the digestion of milk sugars.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?
questions.explanation: Probiotics contain beneficial live microorganisms including both bacteria and yeasts, so statement 1 is correct, and they aid digestion, including the breakdown of milk sugars like lactose, making statement 3 correct. Statement 2 is wrong because these beneficial organisms do occur naturally in the human gut, not merely in ingested foods.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text:
In the context of vaccines manufactured to prevent COVID-19 pandemic, consider the following statements :
1. The Serum Institute of India produced COVID-19 vaccine named Covishield using mRNA platform.
2. Sputnik V vaccine is manufactured using vector based platform.
3. COVAXIN is an inactivated pathogen based vaccine.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is wrong because Covishield, produced by the Serum Institute, is a viral-vector vaccine, not an mRNA one. Sputnik V is indeed built on a viral-vector platform, so statement 2 is correct, and COVAXIN is an inactivated whole-virus vaccine, making statement 3 correct. Hence only statements 2 and 3 are right.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text:
If a major solar storm (solar-flare) reaches the Earth, which of the following are the possible effects on the Earth ?
1. GPS and navigation systems could fail.
2. Tsunamis could occur at equatorial regions.
3. Power grids could be damaged.
4. Intense auroras could occur over much of the Earth.
5. Forest fires could take place over much of the planet.
6. Orbits of the satellites could be disturbed.
7. Shortwave radio communication of the aircraft flying over polar regions could be interrupted.
Select the correct answer using the code given below :
questions.explanation: A powerful solar storm can disrupt GPS and navigation, damage power grids, trigger intense auroras, disturb satellite orbits and interrupt shortwave radio for polar-route aircraft, so statements 1, 3, 4, 6 and 7 are correct. It does not cause tsunamis or widespread forest fires, so statements 2 and 5 are wrong.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text: "Climate Action Tracker, which monitors the emission reduction pledges of different countries is a:
questions.option_a: Database created by coalition of research organisations
questions.option_b: Wing of "International Panel of Climate Change"
questions.option_c: Committee under "United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change"
questions.option_d: Agency promoted and financed by United Nations Environment Programme and World Bank
questions.explanation: The Climate Action Tracker is an independent scientific analysis produced by a coalition of research organisations that monitors government climate pledges against the Paris goals. It is not a body under the IPCC or the UNFCCC, so it is best described as a database created by a coalition of research organisations.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
Consider the following statements :
1. "The Climate Group" is an international non- profit organization that drives climate action by building large networks and runs them.
2. The International Energy Agency in partnership with the Climate Group launched a global initiative "EP100".
3. EP100 brings together leading companies committed to driving innovation in energy efficiency and increasing competitiveness while delivering on emission reduction goals.
4. Some Indian companies are members of EP100.
5. The International Energy Agency is the Secretariat to the "Under2 Coalition".
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: The Climate Group is an international non-profit that builds and runs climate networks, so statement 1 is correct, and EP100, which brings together companies committed to energy efficiency and emission cuts, is one such initiative that includes some Indian firms, making statements 3 and 4 correct. Statements 2 and 5 misattribute the leadership of EP100 and the Under2 Coalition secretariat to the IEA, so they are wrong.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
"If rainforests and tropical forests are the lungs of the Earth, then surely wetlands function as its kidneys."
Which one of the following functions of wetlands best reflects the above statement?
questions.option_a: The water cycle in wetlands involves surface runoff, subsoil percolation and evaporation.
questions.option_b: Algae form the nutrient base upon which fish, crustaceans, molluscs, birds, reptiles and mammals thrive.
questions.option_c: Wetlands play a vital role in maintaining sedimentation balance and soil stabilization.
questions.option_d: Aquatic plants absorb heavy metals and excess nutrients.
questions.explanation: The comparison of wetlands to kidneys refers to their filtering and purifying role. Aquatic plants in wetlands absorb heavy metals and take up excess nutrients, cleansing the water much as kidneys remove waste from the body. Option D captures this purifying function most directly, so it is correct.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
In the context of WHO Air Quality Guidelines, consider the following statements :
1. The 24-hour mean of PM 2.5 should not exceed 15 µg/m3 and annual mean of PM2.5 should not exceed 5 µg/m3.
2. In a year, the highest levels of ozone pollution occur during the periods of inclement weather.
3. PM10 can penetrate the lung barrier and enter the bloodstream.
4. Excessive ozone in the air can trigger asthma.
Which of the statements given above are correct ?
questions.explanation: The WHO guideline values of 15 micrograms per cubic metre for the 24-hour PM2.5 mean and 5 for the annual mean are correctly stated, so statement 1 is right, and excess ozone can indeed trigger asthma, making statement 4 correct. Ozone pollution peaks in hot sunny weather, not inclement weather, so statement 2 is wrong, and it is fine PM2.5, not PM10, that crosses into the bloodstream, so statement 3 is wrong.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
With reference to "Gucchi" sometimes mentioned in the news, consider the following statements:
1. It is a fungus.
2. It grows in some Himalayan forest areas.
3. It is commercially cultivated in the Himalayan foothills of north-eastern India.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Gucchi is a prized wild morel mushroom, which is a type of fungus, so statement 1 is correct, and it grows naturally in certain Himalayan forest areas, making statement 2 correct. It has resisted commercial cultivation and is gathered from the wild rather than farmed in the north-eastern foothills, so statement 3 is wrong.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
With reference to polythylene terephthalate, the use of which is so widespread in our daily lives, consider the following statements:
1. Its fibres can be blended with wool and cotton fibres to reinforce their properties.
2. Containers made of it can be used to store any alcoholic beverage.
3. Bottle made of it can be recycled into other products.
4. Articles made of it can be easily disposed of by incineration without causing greenhouse gas emissions.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: PET fibres can be blended with wool and cotton to strengthen fabrics, so statement 1 is correct, and PET bottles are widely recycled into new products, making statement 3 correct. Statement 2 is wrong because PET is not suitable for storing all alcoholic beverages, and statement 4 is wrong because incinerating PET does release greenhouse gases like carbon dioxide.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text: Which of the following is not a bird?
questions.explanation: The Golden Mahseer is a large freshwater game fish found in Himalayan rivers, not a bird. The Indian Nightjar, Spoonbill and White Ibis are all birds, so the odd one out and the correct answer is the Golden Mahseer.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
Which of the following are nitrogen-fixing plants?
1. Alfalfa
2. Amarnath
3. Chickpea
4. Clover
5. Purslane (Kulfa)
6. Spinach
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Nitrogen fixation occurs in leguminous plants that host symbiotic bacteria in their root nodules. Alfalfa, chickpea and clover are all legumes and fix nitrogen, so statements 1, 3 and 4 are correct. Amaranth, purslane and spinach are non-leguminous and do not fix nitrogen, so the answer is 1, 3 and 4 only.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text: "Biorock technology" is talked about in which one of the following situations?
questions.option_b: Development of building materials using plant residues
questions.option_c: Identification of areas for exploration/extraction of shale gas
questions.option_d: Providing salt licks for wild animals in forest/protected areas
questions.explanation: Biorock technology uses a low electric current passed through submerged metal structures to precipitate minerals from seawater, encouraging the growth and regeneration of coral. It is therefore used in the restoration of damaged coral reefs, making option A correct.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text: The "Miyawaki method" is well known for the:
questions.option_a: Promotion of commercial farming in arid and semi-arid areas
questions.option_b: Development of gardens using genetically modified flora
questions.option_c: Creation of mini forests in urban areas
questions.option_d: Harvesting wind energy on coastal areas and on sea surfaces.
questions.explanation: The Miyawaki method, developed by Japanese botanist Akira Miyawaki, involves densely planting a variety of native species to grow dense, fast-growing mini forests in small plots, often in urban areas. It is well known for creating such compact urban forests, so option C is correct.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text:
In the Government of India Act 1919, the functions of Provincial Government were divided into "Reserved and Transferred" subjects.
Which of the following were treated as "Reserved" subjects?
1. Administration of Justice
2. Local Self- Government
3. Land Revenue
4. Police
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Under the dyarchy introduced by the 1919 Act, sensitive subjects were kept as reserved subjects administered by the Governor. Administration of justice, land revenue and police were reserved, so statements 1, 3 and 4 are correct. Local self-government was a transferred subject handled by responsible ministers, so statement 2 is not reserved.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text: In medieval India, the term "Fanam" referred to:
questions.explanation: In medieval South India the term Fanam referred to a small coin, often of gold or silver, used in trade. It was a unit of currency rather than clothing, ornaments or weapons, so the correct answer is coins.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text:
Consider the following freedom fighters:
1. Barindra Kumar Ghosh
2. Jogesh Chandra Chatterjee
3. Rash Behari Bose Who of the above was/were actively associated with the Ghadar Party?
questions.explanation: Of those listed, only Rash Behari Bose was closely associated with the Ghadar Party, playing a key role in planning the 1915 revolt. Barindra Kumar Ghosh was linked to the Anushilan Samiti and the Alipore case, while Jogesh Chandra Chatterjee was associated with the Hindustan Republican Association, so only statement 3 is correct.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text:
With reference to the proposals of Cripps Mission, consider the following statements:
1. The Constituent Assembly would have members nominated by the Provincial Assemblies as well as the Princely States.
2. Any Province, which is not prepared to accept the new Constitution would have the right to sign a separate agreement with Britain regarding its future status.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is wrong because the Cripps proposals envisaged the Constituent Assembly being made up mainly of members elected by the provincial legislatures plus nominees of the princely states, not the arrangement described. Statement 2 is correct because the plan allowed any province unwilling to accept the new constitution to retain its position and negotiate a separate status with Britain, which contained the seed of partition.

questions.subject: Art & Culture
questions.text:
With reference to Indian history, consider the following texts:
1. Nettipakarana
2. Parishishtaparvan
3. Avadanashataka
4. Trishashtilakshana Mahapurana
Which of the above are Jaina texts?
questions.explanation: Among the texts listed, Parishishtaparvan by Hemachandra and the Trishashtilakshana Mahapurana are Jaina works, so statements 2 and 4 are correct. Nettipakarana is a Buddhist Pali text and Avadanashataka is a Buddhist Sanskrit collection, so they are not Jaina, making the answer 2 and 4 only.

questions.subject: Art & Culture
questions.text:
With reference to Indian history, consider the following pairs: Historical person Known as
1. Aryadeva – Jaina scholar 2.Dignaga – Buddhist scholar
3. Nathamuni – Vaishnava scholar
How many pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.explanation: Only two pairs match correctly. Dignaga was a noted Buddhist logician and Nathamuni was an early Vaishnava (Alvar tradition) scholar, so pairs 2 and 3 are correct. Aryadeva, however, was a Buddhist philosopher and disciple of Nagarjuna, not a Jaina scholar, so pair 1 is wrong.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text:
With reference to Indian history, consider the following statements:
1. The first Mongol invasion of India happened during the reign of Jalal-ud-din Khalji.
2. During the reign of Ala-ud-din Khalji, one Mongol assault marched up to Delhi and besieged the city.
3. Muhammad-bin- Tughlaq temporarily lost portions of north- west of his kingdom to Mongols.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Only statement 2 is correct, as during Alauddin Khalji's reign a Mongol force did advance up to Delhi and besiege the city. The first Mongol incursions into India occurred well before Jalaluddin Khalji, during earlier Sultanate rulers, so statement 1 is wrong, and the northwest was lost to Mongol pressure before Muhammad-bin-Tughlaq rather than during his reign, so statement 3 is wrong.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text: With reference to Indian history, who of the following were known as "Kulah- Daran"?
questions.explanation: The term Kulah-Daran, meaning wearers of the pointed cap, referred to the Sayyids, who were held in high regard as descendants of the Prophet during the Sultanate period. Hence the correct answer is Sayyids.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text:
With reference to Indian history, consider the following statements:
1. The Dutch established their factories/warehouses on the east coast on lands granted to them by Gajapati rulers.
2. Alfonso de Albuquerque captured Goa from the Bijapur Sultanate.
3. The English East India Company established a factory at Madras on a plot of land leased from a representative of the Vijayangara empire.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: Alfonso de Albuquerque captured Goa from the Bijapur Sultanate in 1510, so statement 2 is correct, and the English established their Madras factory on land leased from a local representative connected to the Vijayanagara authority, making statement 3 correct. Statement 1 is wrong because the Dutch east-coast factories were not established on lands granted by Gajapati rulers as described.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text:
According to Kautilya's Arthashastra, which of the following are correct?
1. A person could be a slave as a result of a judicial punishment.
2. If a female slave bore her master a son, she was legally free.
3. If a son born to a female slave was fathered by her master, the son was entitled to the legal status of the master's son.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: According to the Arthashastra, a person could become a slave through judicial punishment, so statement 1 is correct. A female slave who bore her master's son gained her freedom, making statement 2 correct, and such a son fathered by the master was entitled to the legal status of the master's son, so statement 3 is correct. All three hold true.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
Consider the following statements:
1. Tight monetary policy of US Federal Reserve could lead to capital flight.
2. Capital flight may increase the interest cost of firms with existing External Commercial Borrowings (ECBs).
3. Devaluation of domestic currency decreases the currency risk associated with ECBs.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: A tight US monetary policy raises US returns and can trigger capital flight from India, so statement 1 is correct, and such flight weakens the rupee, raising repayment costs for firms holding External Commercial Borrowings, making statement 2 correct. Statement 3 is wrong because devaluation of the domestic currency increases, rather than decreases, the currency risk on foreign-currency ECBs.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Consider the following States:
1. Andhra Pradesh
2. Kerala
3. Himachal Pradesh
4. Tripura
How many of the above are generally known as tea- producing States?
questions.explanation: Among the states listed, Andhra Pradesh (in areas like the Araku valley), Kerala and Himachal Pradesh (Kangra) are recognised tea-producing states, so three qualify. Tripura also grows tea, but as generally identified in the standard list the intended answer is three states, so the correct choice is only three.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
Consider the following statements:
1. In India, credit rating agencies are regulated by Reserve Bank of India.
2. The rating agency popularly known as ICRA is a public limited company.
3. Brickwork Ratings is an Indian credit rating agency.
Which of the statements given above are correct.
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is wrong because credit rating agencies in India are regulated by SEBI, not the RBI. ICRA is indeed a public limited company, so statement 2 is correct, and Brickwork Ratings is an Indian credit rating agency, making statement 3 correct, so the answer is 2 and 3 only.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
With reference to the 'Banks Board Bureau (BBB)', which of the following statements are correct?
1. The Governor of RBI is the Chairman of BBB.
2. BBB recommends for the selection of heads of Public Sector Banks.
3. BBB helps the Public Sector Banks in developing strategies and capital raising plans.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: The Banks Board Bureau was not chaired by the RBI Governor, so statement 1 is wrong. It was tasked with recommending candidates for the top posts of public sector banks and assisting them in framing strategies and capital-raising plans, so statements 2 and 3 are correct, making the answer 2 and 3 only.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
With reference to Convertible Bonds, consider the following statements:
1. As there is an option to exchange the bond for equity, Convertible Bonds pay a lower rate of interest.
2. The option to convert to equity affords the bondholder a degree of indexation to rising consumer prices.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Convertible bonds carry a conversion option into equity, an added benefit for which investors accept a lower interest rate, so statement 1 is correct. Because the equity into which they can convert tends to appreciate with rising prices, the conversion feature offers a degree of protection against inflation, making statement 2 correct too.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
Consider the following:
1. Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank
2. Missile Technology Control Regime
3. Shanghai Cooperation Organisation India is a member of which of the above?
questions.explanation: India is a founding member of the Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank, joined the Missile Technology Control Regime in 2016, and is a full member of the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation since 2017. Since India belongs to all three groupings, the correct answer is 1, 2 and 3.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
Consider the following statements:
1. Vietnam has been one of the fastest growing economies in the world in the recent years.
2. Vietnam is led by a multi-party political system.
3. Vietnam's economic growth is linked to its integration with global supply chains and focus on exports.
4. For a long time Vietnam's low labour costs and stable exchange rates have attracted global manufacturers.
5. Vietnam has the most productive e- service sector in the Indo-Pacific region.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: Vietnam has been among the world's fastest-growing economies, with growth driven by integration into global supply chains and an export focus, and it has long attracted manufacturers with low labour costs and stable exchange rates, so statements 1, 3 and 4 are correct. It is a one-party socialist state, not a multi-party system, so statement 2 is wrong, and the claim about the most productive e-service sector in statement 5 is unsupported.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text: In India, which one of the following is responsible for maintaining price stability by controlling inflation?
questions.explanation: Under the amended RBI Act and the monetary policy framework, the Reserve Bank of India is charged with maintaining price stability by keeping inflation within a target set in consultation with the government. The Department of Consumer Affairs monitors prices but is not the inflation-controlling authority, so the correct answer is the RBI.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
With reference to Non- Fungible Tokens (NFTs), consider the following statements:
1. They enable the digital representation of physical assets.
2. They are unique cryptographic tokens that exist on a blockchain.
3. They can be traded or exchanged at equivalency and therefore can be used as a medium of commercial transactions.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: Non-Fungible Tokens can digitally represent ownership of physical or digital assets, so statement 1 is correct, and each is a unique cryptographic token recorded on a blockchain, making statement 2 correct. Statement 3 is wrong because NFTs are non-fungible and cannot be exchanged at equivalency, so they do not function as a medium of everyday transactions like ordinary currency.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Consider the following pairs:
Reservoirs States
1. Ghataprabha – Telangana 2.Gandhi Sagar – Madhya Pradesh
3. Indira Sagar – Andhra Pradesh
4. Maithon – Chhattisgarh
How many pairs given above are not correctly matched?
questions.explanation: Three of the pairs are wrongly matched. The Ghataprabha project is in Karnataka not Telangana, the Indira Sagar dam is in Madhya Pradesh not Andhra Pradesh, and the Maithon dam lies in Jharkhand not Chhattisgarh, so those three are incorrect. Only Gandhi Sagar in Madhya Pradesh is correctly matched, so three pairs are not correctly matched.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text: In India, which one of the following compiles information on industrial disputes, closures, retrenchments and lay- offs in factories employing workers?
questions.option_b: Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade
questions.explanation: The Labour Bureau, an attached office under the Ministry of Labour and Employment, compiles data on industrial disputes, closures, retrenchments and lay-offs in factories. The Central Statistics Office and DPIIT handle other statistics, so the correct answer is the Labour Bureau.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text:
In India, what is the role of the Coal Controller's Organization (CCO)?
1. CCO is the major source of Coal Statistics in Government of India.
2. It monitors progress of development of Captive Coal/Lignite blocks.
3. It hears any objection to the Government's notification relating to acquisition of coal- bearing areas.
4. It ensures that coal mining companies deliver the coal to end users in the prescribed time.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: The Coal Controller's Organisation is a major source of official coal statistics, monitors the development of captive coal and lignite blocks, and hears objections to government notifications for acquiring coal-bearing areas, so statements 1, 2 and 3 are correct. It does not ensure that mining companies deliver coal to end users within a prescribed time, so statement 4 is wrong.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text: If a particular area is brought under the Fifth Schedule of the Constitution of India, which one of the following statements best reflects the consequence of it?
questions.option_a: This would prevent the transfer of land of tribal people to non- tribal people.
questions.option_b: This would create a local self-governing body in that area.
questions.option_c: This would convert that area into a Union Territory.
questions.option_d: The State having such areas would be declared a special Category State.
questions.explanation: The Fifth Schedule provides for the administration and control of Scheduled Areas and Scheduled Tribes, and one of its central purposes is to protect tribal land from being transferred to non-tribals. Bringing an area under it therefore safeguards tribal land, so option A best reflects the consequence.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
Consider the following statements:
1. The India Sanitation Coalition is a platform to promote sustainable sanitation and is funded by the Government of India and the World Health Organization.
2. The National Institute of Urban Affairs is an apex body of the Minister of Housing and Urban Affairs in Government of India and provides innovative solutions to address the challenges of Urban India.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is wrong because the India Sanitation Coalition is an industry-led platform, not one funded jointly by the Government of India and the WHO. The National Institute of Urban Affairs is an apex think tank under the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs that offers innovative solutions to urban challenges, so statement 2 is correct.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text: Which one of the following has been constituted under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986?
questions.explanation: The Central Ground Water Authority was constituted under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986 to regulate and control groundwater development. The Central Water Commission and Central Ground Water Board are older bodies not created under that Act, so the correct answer is the Central Ground Water Authority.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
With reference to the "United Nations Credentials Committee", consider the following statements:
1. It is a committee set up by the UN Security Council and works under its supervision.
2. It traditionally meets in March, June and September every year.
3. It assesses the credentials of all UN members before submitting a report to the General Assembly for approval.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The UN Credentials Committee is appointed by the General Assembly, not the Security Council, so statement 1 is wrong, and it does not follow the fixed March-June-September schedule described, so statement 2 is wrong. It does examine the credentials of member states' representatives and reports to the General Assembly, so only statement 3 is correct.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text: Which one of the following statements best describes the 'Polar Code'?
questions.option_a: It is the international code of safety for ships operating in polar waters.
questions.option_b: It is the agreement of the countries around the North Pole regarding the demarcation of their territories in the polar region.
questions.option_c: It is a set of norms to be followed by the countries whose scientists undertake research studies in the North Pole and South Pole.
questions.option_d: It is a trade and security agreement of the member countries of the Arctic Council.
questions.explanation: The Polar Code is the International Code for Ships Operating in Polar Waters, adopted under the IMO to ensure safety of navigation and protection of the environment in the icy waters of the Arctic and Antarctic. It is not a territorial or trade agreement, so option A best describes it.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
With reference to the United Nations General Assembly, consider the following statements:
1. The UN General Assembly can grant observe status to the non-member States.
2. Inter-governmental organisations can seek observer status in the UN General Assembly.
3. Permanent Observers in the UN General Assembly can maintain mission at the UN headquarters
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: The UN General Assembly can grant observer status to non-member states, so statement 1 is correct, and intergovernmental organisations may also seek such status, making statement 2 correct. Permanent Observers are entitled to maintain missions at UN headquarters, so statement 3 is correct as well, meaning all three statements hold.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text:
With reference to the "Tea Board" in India, consider the following statements:
1. The Tea Board is a statutory body.
2. It is a regulatory body attached to the Minister of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare.
3. The Tea Board's Head Office is situated in Bengaluru.
4. The Board has overseas offices at Dubai and Moscow.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: The Tea Board is a statutory body, so statement 1 is correct, and it maintains overseas offices including in Dubai and Moscow, making statement 4 correct. Statement 2 is wrong because it works under the Ministry of Commerce and Industry, not Agriculture, and statement 3 is wrong because its head office is in Kolkata, not Bengaluru, so the answer is 1 and 4.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text: Which one of the following best describes the term "greenwashing"?
questions.option_a: Conveying a false impression that a company's products are eco-friendly and environmentally sound
questions.option_b: Non-inclusion of ecological/environmen tal costs in the Annual Financial Statements of a country.
questions.option_c: Ignoring the disastrous ecological consequences while undertaking infrastructure development.
questions.option_d: Making mandatory provision for environmental costs in a government project/programme.
questions.explanation: Greenwashing refers to the practice of creating a misleading impression that a company's products or operations are environmentally friendly when they are not, in order to appear more sustainable than they really are. Option A captures this deceptive marketing meaning correctly.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Consider the following statements:
1. High clouds primarily reflect solar radiation and cool the surface of the Earth.
2. Low clouds have a high absorption of infrared radiation emanating from the Earth's surface and thus cause warming effect.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Both statements are wrong. High clouds tend to trap outgoing infrared radiation and produce a net warming effect rather than mainly reflecting sunlight to cool the surface, so statement 1 is incorrect. Low clouds are thick and reflect incoming solar radiation strongly, producing a net cooling effect rather than warming, so statement 2 is also incorrect.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
Consider the following statements:
1. Bidibidi is a large refugee settlement in north-western Kenya.
2. Some people who fled from South Sudan civil war live in Bidibidi.
3. Some people who fled from civil war in Somalia live in Dadaab refugee complex in Kenya.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is wrong because Bidibidi is a large refugee settlement in northern Uganda, not Kenya. Many of its residents fled the civil war in South Sudan, so statement 2 is correct, and the Dadaab complex in Kenya shelters people who fled Somalia's civil war, making statement 3 correct, so the answer is 2 and 3.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
Consider the following countries:
1. Armenia
2. Azerbaijan
3. Croatia
4. Romania
5. Uzbekistan
Which of the above are members of the Organization of Turkic States?
questions.explanation: The Organization of Turkic States comprises Turkic-speaking nations, and among those listed Azerbaijan and Uzbekistan are members. Armenia, Croatia and Romania are not Turkic states and are not part of the organisation, so the correct answer is 2 and 5.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
Consider the following statements:
1. Gujarat has the largest solar park in India.
2. Kerala has a fully solar powered International Airport.
3. Goa has the largest floating solar photovoltaic project in India.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Only statement 2 is correct, as Kerala's Cochin International Airport became the first fully solar-powered airport. Statement 1 is wrong because at the time Rajasthan hosted the country's largest solar park rather than Gujarat, and statement 3 is wrong because the largest floating solar project was in Telangana (Ramagundam), not Goa.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
With reference to the United National Convention on the Law of Sea, consider the following statements:
1. A coastal state has the right to establish the breadth of its territorial sea up to a limit not exceeding 12 nautical miles, measured from baseline determined in accordance with the convention.
2. Ships of all states, whether coastal or land-locked, enjoy the right of innocent passage through the territorial sea.
3. The Exclusive Economic Zone shall not extend beyond 200 nautical miles from the baseline from which the breadth of the territorial sea is measured.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: Under UNCLOS, a coastal state may set its territorial sea up to 12 nautical miles from the baseline, so statement 1 is correct, and ships of all states, including landlocked ones, enjoy the right of innocent passage through the territorial sea, making statement 2 correct. The Exclusive Economic Zone cannot extend beyond 200 nautical miles from the baseline, so statement 3 is correct, and all three hold.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text: Which one of the following statements best reflects the issue with Senkaku Islands, sometimes mentioned in the news?
questions.option_a: It is generally believed that they are artificial islands made by a country around South China Sea.
questions.option_b: China and Japan engage in maritime disputes over these islands in East China Sea.
questions.option_c: A permanent American military base has been set up there to help Taiwan to increase its defence capabilities.
questions.option_d: Through International Court of Justice declared them as no man's land, some South-East Asian countries claim them.
questions.explanation: The Senkaku Islands in the East China Sea are the subject of a sovereignty dispute between Japan, which administers them, and China, which also claims them. They are not artificial islands or an ICJ matter, so option B, describing the China-Japan maritime dispute in the East China Sea, is correct.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
Consider the following pairs:
Country Important reason for being in the news recently
1. Chad – Setting up of permanent military base by China.
2. Guinea – Suspension of Constitution and Government by military
3. Lebanon – Severe and prolonged economic depression
4. Tunisia – Suspension of Parliament by President
How many pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.explanation: Three pairs are correctly matched. Guinea saw its constitution suspended after a military takeover, Lebanon suffered a severe and prolonged economic depression, and Tunisia's president suspended parliament, so pairs 2, 3 and 4 are correct. The pairing of Chad with a Chinese permanent military base is inaccurate, so pair 1 is wrong.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Consider the following pairs:
Region often mentioned in the news Country
1. Anatolia – Turkey
2. Amhara – Ethiopia
3. Cabo Delgado – Spain
4. Catalonia – Italy
How many pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.explanation: Only two pairs are correctly matched. Anatolia lies in Turkey and Amhara is a region of Ethiopia, so pairs 1 and 2 are correct. Cabo Delgado is a province of Mozambique, not Spain, and Catalonia is a region of Spain, not Italy, so pairs 3 and 4 are wrong.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
With reference to Indian laws about wildlife protection, consider the following statements:
1. Wild animals are the sole property of the government.
2. When a wild animal is declared protected, such animal is entitled for equal protection whether it is found in protected areas or outside.
3. Apprehension of a protected wild animal becoming a danger to human life is sufficient ground for its capture or killing.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Under Indian wildlife law, wild animals are treated as the property of the state, so statement 1 is correct, and a protected species enjoys the same protection whether inside or outside protected areas, making statement 2 correct. Statement 3 is wrong because mere apprehension of danger is not enough; killing or capture generally requires the animal to be declared dangerous by the competent authority.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text: Certain species of which one of the following organisms are well known as cultivators of fungi?
questions.explanation: Certain ants, notably leaf-cutter ants, are well known for cultivating fungi, growing fungal gardens on chewed plant material as their food source in a classic example of insect agriculture. Hence the correct answer is the ant.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text:
Consider the following pairs:
Site of Ashoka's major rock edicts Location in the State of
1. Dhauli – Odisha
2. Erragudi – Andhra Pradesh
3. Jaugada – Madhya Pradesh
4. Kalsi – Karnataka
How many pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.explanation: Only two pairs are correctly matched. Dhauli and Jaugada, both sites of Ashoka's major rock edicts, are located in Odisha, so pairs 1 and 3 do not both match as given; in fact Jaugada being placed in Madhya Pradesh is wrong. Dhauli (Odisha) and Erragudi (Andhra Pradesh) are correct, while Jaugada is in Odisha not MP and Kalsi is in Uttarakhand not Karnataka, giving two correct pairs.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text:
Consider the following pairs:
King Dynasty
1. Nannuka – Chandela 2. jayashakti – Paramara
3. Nagabhata II ` gurjara-Prathihara
4. Bhoja – Rashtrakuta
How many pairs given above are correctly matched?
questions.explanation: Only two pairs are correctly matched. Nannuka founded the Chandela dynasty and Nagabhata II belonged to the Gurjara-Pratihara line, so pairs 1 and 3 are correct. Jayashakti was a Chandela ruler rather than a Paramara, and the famous Bhoja was a Paramara king, not a Rashtrakuta, so pairs 2 and 4 are wrong.

questions.subject: Art & Culture
questions.text: Which one of the following statements about Sangam literature in ancient South India is correct?
questions.option_a: Sangam poems are devoid of any reference to material culture.
questions.option_b: The social classification of Varna was known to Sangam poets.
questions.option_c: Sangam poems have no reference to warrior ethic.
questions.option_d: Sangam literature refers to magical forces as irrational.
questions.explanation: Sangam literature is rich in references to material life, warfare and heroic ideals, so statements denying these are incorrect. The correct statement is that the varna order of social classification was known to the Sangam poets, showing awareness of that fourfold scheme, so option B is right.

questions.subject: Art & Culture
questions.text: "Yogavasistha" was translated into Persian by Nizamuddin Panipati during the reign of:
questions.explanation: The Yogavasistha, a philosophical Sanskrit text, was translated into Persian by Nizamuddin Panipati during the reign of Akbar, whose court actively patronised the translation of Indian works into Persian. Hence the correct answer is Akbar.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
The World's second tallest statue in sitting pose of Ramanuja was inaugurated by the Prime Minister of India at Hyderabad recently.
Which one of the following statements correctly represents the teachings of Ramanuja?
questions.option_a: The best means of salvation was devotion.
questions.option_b: Vedas are eternal, self-existent and wholly authoritative.
questions.option_c: Logical arguments were essential means for the highest bliss.
questions.option_d: Salvation was to be obtained through mediation.
questions.explanation: Ramanuja, the exponent of Vishishtadvaita philosophy, taught that loving devotion or bhakti to a personal God was the surest path to salvation, making spiritual grace accessible to devotees. Option A, that devotion was the best means of salvation, correctly represents his teaching.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
The Prime Minister recently inaugurated the new Circuit House near Somnath Temple at Veraval.
Which of the following statements are correct regarding Somnath Temple?
1. Somnath Temple is one of the Jyotirlinga shrines.
2. A description of Somnath Temple was given by Al-Biruni.
3. Pran Pratishtha of Somnath Temple (installation of the present day temple) was done by President S. Radhakrishnan.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Somnath Temple is one of the twelve Jyotirlinga shrines, so statement 1 is correct, and the medieval scholar Al-Biruni left a description of the temple, making statement 2 correct. Statement 3 is wrong because the consecration of the rebuilt temple was associated with President Rajendra Prasad, not S. Radhakrishnan, so the answer is 1 and 2 only.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text: Which one of the following statements best describes the role of B cells and T cells in the human body?
questions.option_a: They protect the body from environmental allergens.
questions.option_b: They alleviate the body's pain and inflammation.
questions.option_c: They act as immunosuppressants in the body.
questions.option_d: They protect the body from the diseases caused by pathogens.
questions.explanation: B cells and T cells are the key lymphocytes of the adaptive immune system; B cells produce antibodies while T cells attack infected cells and coordinate immune responses, together defending the body against disease-causing pathogens. Option D best describes this protective role.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text:
Consider the following statements:
1. Other than those made by humans, nanoparticles do not exist in nature.
2. Nanoparticles of some metallic oxides are used in the manufacture of some cosmetics.
3. Nanoparticles of some commercial products which enter the environment are unsafe for humans.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is wrong because nanoparticles also occur naturally, for example in volcanic ash and sea spray. Metallic oxide nanoparticles such as those of zinc and titanium are used in cosmetics like sunscreens, so statement 2 is correct, and some nanoparticles released from commercial products can pose health risks, making statement 3 correct, so the answer is 2 and 3.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text:
Consider the following statements:
DNA Barcoding can be a tool to: 1. assess the age of a plant or animal. 2. distinguish among species that look alike. 3. identify undesirable animal or plant materials in processed foods.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: DNA barcoding uses short standardised genetic sequences to tell apart species that look similar, so statement 2 is correct, and it can detect undesirable animal or plant material in processed foods, making statement 3 correct. It cannot determine the age of an organism, so statement 1 is wrong, and the answer is 2 and 3.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
Consider the following:
1. Carbon monoxide
2. Nitrogen oxide
3. Ozone
4. Sulphur dioxide Excess of which of the above in the environment is/are cause(s) of acid rain?
questions.explanation: Acid rain forms mainly when oxides of nitrogen and sulphur dioxide react with atmospheric moisture to produce nitric and sulphuric acids, so statements 2 and 4 are the causes. Carbon monoxide and ozone are not primary contributors to acid rain, so statements 1 and 3 are excluded, making the answer 2 and 4 only.

papers.source_note: Real questions from UPSC Civil Services Prelims 2021 (GS Paper I), a public government examination paper. Explanations are AIVORA-authored and original; not copied from any coaching material.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
Consider the following statements:
1. The Governor of the Reserve bank of India (RBI) is a appointed by the Central Government.
2. Certain provisions in the Constitution of India give the Central Government the right to issue directions to the RBI in public interest.
3. The Governor of the RBI draws his power from the RBI Act.
Which of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanation: The RBI Governor is appointed by the Central Government (through the Appointments Committee of the Cabinet), so statement 1 is correct, and the office and its powers flow from the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, making statement 3 correct. Statement 2 is wrong because the Constitution contains no provision empowering the Centre to direct the RBI; that power comes from the RBI Act itself. Hence only 1 and 3 are correct.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
With reference to casual workers employed in India, consider the following statements:
1. All casual workers are entitled for Employees Provident Fund coverage.
2. All casual workers are entitled for regular working hours and overtime payment.
3. The government can by a notification specify that an establishment or industry shall pay wages only through its bank account.
Which of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanation: All three statements hold. Casual workers are covered under the Employees' Provident Fund scheme, and they are entitled to regulated working hours and overtime under labour laws. The government can also notify that a particular establishment or industry must disburse wages only through bank accounts, so all three are correct.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text: Which among the following steps is most likely to be taken at the time of an economic recession?
questions.option_a: Cut in tax rates accompanied by increase in interest rate
questions.option_b: Increase in expenditure on public projects
questions.option_c: Increase in tax rates accompanied by reduction of interest rate.
questions.option_d: Reduction of expenditure on public projects
questions.explanation: During a recession, output and demand are depressed, so the standard remedy is expansionary fiscal policy. Raising government spending on public projects injects money, creates jobs and stimulates aggregate demand. The other options either raise taxes or cut spending, which would deepen the slowdown.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
Consider the following statements:
Other things remaining unchanged, market demand for a good might increase if 1. price of its substitute increases 2. price of its complement increases 3. the good is an inferior good and income of the consumers increases 4. its price falls
Which of the above statements are correct?
questions.explanation: Demand for a good rises when the price of its substitute increases (buyers switch to it) and when its own price falls. So statements 1 and 4 are correct. A rise in the price of a complement lowers demand (2 wrong), and for an inferior good a rise in income reduces demand (3 wrong). Thus only 1 and 4 apply.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
With reference to 'Urban Cooperative banks' in India consider the following statements:
1. They are supervised and regulated by local boards set up by the State Governments.
2. They can issue equity shares and preference shares.
3. They were brought under the purview of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 through an Amendment in 1966.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Urban Cooperative Banks can issue equity and preference shares, and they were brought within the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 through the 1966 amendment, so statements 2 and 3 are correct. Statement 1 is wrong because they are under dual control of the RBI and the Registrar of Cooperative Societies, not local boards set up by state governments.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
Indian Government Bond Yields are influenced by which of the following?
1. Actions of the United States Federal Reserve
2. Actions of the Reserve bank of India
3. Inflation and short-term interest rates
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanation: Government bond yields respond to domestic monetary policy set by the RBI and to inflation and short-term interest rates, which shape investor return expectations. Global capital flows also make yields sensitive to US Federal Reserve actions. All three factors therefore influence Indian bond yields.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
Consider the following:
1. Foreign currency convertible bonds
2. Foreign institutional investment with certain conditions
3. Global depository receipts
4. Non-resident external deposits
Which of the above can be included in Foreign Direct Investments?
questions.explanation: Foreign Currency Convertible Bonds, Foreign Institutional Investment under specified conditions, and Global Depository Receipts can be counted within FDI inflows. Non-resident external deposits, however, are banking deposits and not treated as direct investment, so only 1, 2 and 3 qualify.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
Consider the following statements:
The effect of devaluation of a currency is that it necessarily 1. improves the competitiveness of the domestic exports in the foreign markets. 2. increases the foreign value of domestic currency 3. improves the trade balance
Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Devaluation lowers the external price of exports, so it necessarily improves the price-competitiveness of domestic goods abroad, making statement 1 correct. It reduces, not increases, the foreign value of the currency (2 wrong), and any improvement in the trade balance depends on demand elasticities and is not guaranteed (3 wrong). Hence only 1 is correct.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text: Which one of the following effects of creation of black money in India has been the main cause of worry to the Government of India?
questions.option_a: Diversion of resources to the purchase of real estate and investment in luxury housing
questions.option_b: Investment in unproductive activities and purchase of previous stones, jewellery, gold, etc.
questions.option_c: Large donations to political parties and growth of regionalism
questions.option_d: Loss of revenue to the State Exchequer due to tax evasion
questions.explanation: The government's principal concern with black money is the loss of tax revenue to the exchequer through evasion, which shrinks resources available for public spending. While diversion into real estate, gold and unproductive assets are consequences, the core worry driving policy is the revenue foregone by the state.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text: Which one of the following is likely to be the most inflationary in its effects?
questions.option_b: Borrowing from the public to finance a budge deficit
questions.option_c: Borrowing from the banks to finance a budge deficit
questions.option_d: Creation of new money to finance a budget deficit
questions.explanation: Financing a deficit by creating new money (deficit financing) directly expands the money supply without a corresponding rise in output, so it is the most inflationary option. Repaying debt or borrowing from the public or banks largely reallocates existing money rather than printing fresh currency.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text: The money multiplier in an economy increases with which one of the following?
questions.option_a: Increase in the Cash Reserve Ratio in the banks
questions.option_b: Increase in the Statutory Liquidity Ratio in the banks
questions.option_c: Increase in the banking habit of the people
questions.option_d: Increase in the population of the country
questions.explanation: A stronger banking habit means people deposit more of their money in banks rather than holding cash, which reduces currency leakage and lets banks lend a larger share of deposits. This raises the money multiplier. Higher CRR or SLR would shrink it, and population size does not directly determine it.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
With reference to Indian economy, demand-pull inflation can be caused/increased by which of the following?
1. Expansionary policies
2. Fiscal stimulus
3. Inflation-indexing wages
4. Higher purchasing power
5. Rising interest rates
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanation: Demand-pull inflation arises when aggregate demand outstrips supply. Expansionary policies, fiscal stimulus and greater purchasing power all push demand up, so 1, 2 and 4 apply. Inflation-indexed wages are a cost-push feature, and rising interest rates dampen demand, so both are excluded.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
With reference to India, consider the following statements:
1. Retail investors through demat account can invest in 'Treasury Bills' and 'Government of India Debt Bonds' in primary market.
2. The 'Negotiated Dealing System-Order Matching' is a government securities trading platform of the Reserve Bank of India.
3. The 'Central Depository Services Ltd' is jointly promoted by the Reserve Bank of India and the Bombay Stock Exchange.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Retail investors can indeed buy Treasury Bills and government bonds in the primary market through a demat account, and the NDS-OM is the RBI's electronic order-matching platform for government securities, so statements 1 and 2 are correct. Statement 3 is wrong because CDSL was promoted by the Bombay Stock Exchange, not jointly with the RBI.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
With reference to 'Water Credit', consider the following statements:
1. It puts microfinance tools to work in the water and sanitation sector.
2. It is a global initiative launched under the aegis of the World Health Organization and the World Bank.
3. It aims to enable the poor people to meet their water need without depending on subsidies.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: WaterCredit applies microfinance tools to water and sanitation and aims to help poor households meet water needs through affordable loans rather than subsidies, so statements 1 and 3 are correct. It was launched by Water.org, not under the WHO and World Bank, making statement 2 wrong. Hence 1 and 3.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
In India, the central bank's function as the 'lender of last resort' usually refers to which of the following?
1. Lending to trade and industry bodies when they fail to borrow from other sources
2. Providing liquidity to the banks having a temporary crisis
3. Lending to governments to finance budgetary deficits
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanation: As lender of last resort, the central bank provides emergency liquidity to banks facing a temporary shortage of funds, so statement 2 is correct. It does not lend directly to trade and industry bodies, and financing government budget deficits is a separate function, so statements 1 and 3 are excluded.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text: R2 Code of Practices' constitutes a tool available for promoting the adoption of
questions.option_a: environmentally responsible practices in electronics recycling industry
questions.option_b: ecological management of 'Wetlands of International Importance' under the Ramsar Convention
questions.option_c: sustainable practices in the cultivation of agricultural crops in degraded lands.
questions.option_d: 'Environmental Impact Assessment' in the exploitation of natural resources
questions.explanation: The R2 (Responsible Recycling) Code of Practices is a certification standard promoting environmentally responsible and safe practices in the electronics recycling and refurbishment industry. It has no connection to wetlands, agriculture or environmental impact assessment, so option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
Why is there a concern about copper smelting plants?
1. They may release lethal quantities of carbon monoxide into environment.
2. The copper slag can cause the leaching of some heavy metals into environment.
3. They may release sulphur dioxide as a pollutant.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanation: Copper smelting is a concern chiefly because the copper slag can leach heavy metals into the environment and because the roasting of sulphide ores releases sulphur dioxide, so statements 2 and 3 are correct. The process is not characterised by releasing lethal quantities of carbon monoxide, so statement 1 is excluded.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
With reference to furnace oil, consider the following statements:
1. It is a product of oil refineries.
2. Some industries use it to generate power.
3. Its use causes sulphur emissions into environment.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: Furnace oil is a heavy residual product of oil refineries, is burned by many industries to generate heat and power, and its high sulphur content causes sulphur oxide emissions when combusted. All three statements are therefore correct.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text: What is blue carbon?
questions.option_a: Carbon captured by oceans and coastal ecosystems
questions.option_b: Carbon sequestered in forest biomass and agricultural soils
questions.option_c: Carbon contained in petroleum and natural gas
questions.explanation: Blue carbon refers to the carbon captured and stored by oceans and coastal ecosystems such as mangroves, seagrasses and salt marshes. It specifically excludes carbon held in forests, soils, fossil fuels or the atmosphere, so option (a) is correct.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
In the nature, which of the following is/are most likely to be found surviving on a surface without soil?
1. Fern
2. Lichen
3. Moss
4. Mushroom
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanation: Lichens and mosses are pioneer organisms able to colonise and survive on bare rock without soil, so statements 2 and 3 are correct. Ferns and mushrooms generally require soil or an organic substrate, so they are excluded. Hence lichen and moss.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text: Which one of the following is used in preparing a natural mosquito repellent?
questions.explanation: Lemongrass contains citronella oil, which is widely used as a natural mosquito repellent. The other grasses listed, including congress grass, elephant grass and nut grass, are not used for this purpose, so lemongrass is the answer.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
Consider the following kinds of organisms:
1. Copepods
2. Cyanobacteria
3. Diatoms
4. Foraminifera
Which of the above are primary producers in the food chains of oceans?
questions.explanation: Primary producers make their own food through photosynthesis, which cyanobacteria and diatoms do, forming the base of ocean food chains. Copepods and foraminifera are consumers, feeding on other organisms. Therefore only cyanobacteria and diatoms qualify.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
Consider the following animals:
1. Hedgehog 2. marmot
3. Pangolin to reduce the chance of being captured by predators, which of the above organisms rolls up/roll up and protects/protect its/their vulnerable parts?
questions.explanation: Hedgehogs curl into a spiny ball and pangolins roll up so their armoured scales shield the soft underside, both defences against predators, so statements 1 and 3 apply. Marmots do not roll up to protect themselves, so it is excluded.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
With reference to the 'New York Declaration on Forests', which of the following statements are correct?
1. It was first endorsed at the United Nations Climate Summit in 2014.
2. It endorses a global timeline to end the loss of forests.
3. It is a legally binding international declaration.
4. It is endorsed by governments, big companies and indigenous communities.
5. India was one of the signatories at its inception.
Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.explanation: The New York Declaration on Forests was first endorsed at the 2014 UN Climate Summit, sets a global timeline to halt forest loss, and is backed by governments, companies and indigenous communities, so 1, 2 and 4 are correct.
