questions.explanation: By the mid-eighteenth century the English East India Company's principal exports from Bengal were cotton textiles, silk, saltpetre and opium. Saltpetre was valued for gunpowder, while textiles and opium were major trade goods, so this combination fits best.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text: Which one of the following is a very significant aspect of the Champaran Satyagraha?
questions.option_a: Active all-India participation of lawyers, students and women in the National Movement
questions.option_b: Active involvement of Dalit and Tribal communities of India in the National Movement
questions.option_c: Joining of peasant unrest to India's National Movement
questions.option_d: Drastic decrease in the cultivation of plantation crops and commercial crops
questions.explanation: The significance of the Champaran Satyagraha of 1917 lay in linking the grievances of peasants, particularly indigo cultivators, to the wider national movement. It brought rural agrarian unrest into the mainstream of the freedom struggle under Gandhi's leadership.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text: Who among the following were the founders of the "Hind Mazdoor Sabha" established in 1948?
questions.option_a: B. Krishna Pillai, E.M.S. Namboodiripad and K.C. George
questions.option_b: Jayaprakash Narayan, DeenDayal Upadhyay and M.N. Roy
questions.option_c: C.P. Ramaswamy Iyer, K. Kamaraj and VeeresalingamPantulu
questions.option_d: Ashok Mehta, T.S. Ramanujan and G.G. Mehta
questions.explanation: The Hind Mazdoor Sabha, formed in 1948, was founded by socialist trade unionists, and among those associated with its establishment were Ashok Mehta, T.S. Ramanujan and G.G. Mehta. The individuals named in the other options were not its founders.

questions.subject: Art & Culture
questions.text: Which reference to the religious practices in India, the "Sthanakvasi" sect belongs to
questions.explanation: The Sthanakvasi is a sect within Jainism, an offshoot of the Svetambara tradition, distinguished by its rejection of idol worship and temple rituals in favour of meditation in prayer halls. It is not connected with Buddhism, Vaishnavism or Shaivism, so the other options are wrong.

questions.subject: Art & Culture
questions.text:
With reference to the cultural history of India, consider the following statements:
1. White marble was used in making Buland Darwaza and Khankah at Fatehpur Sikri.
2. Red sandstone and marble were used in making Bara Imambara and Rumi Darwaza at Lucknow.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Only statement 1 is treated as correct, associating white marble work with structures at Fatehpur Sikri. Statement 2 is wrong because the Bara Imambara and Rumi Darwaza at Lucknow were built largely of brick and lime plaster rather than red sandstone and marble.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text: Which one of the following foreign travellers elaborately discussed about diamonds and diamond mines of India?
questions.explanation: The French traveller Jean-Baptiste Tavernier, a gem merchant who journeyed through seventeenth-century India, wrote in detail about its diamonds and diamond mines. Bernier, Thevenot and Carre did not focus on this subject in the same elaborate way.

questions.subject: Art & Culture
questions.text: Which reference to Indian history, who among the following is a future Buddha, yet to come to save the world?
questions.explanation: Maitreya is regarded in Buddhist tradition as the future Buddha, still to appear in the world to renew the teaching. Avalokiteshvara, Lokeshvara and Padmapani are names or forms of the Bodhisattva of compassion rather than the future Buddha.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text: Which one of the following statements does not apply to the system of Subsidiary Alliance introduced by Lord Wellesley?
questions.option_a: To maintain a large standing army at other's expense
questions.option_b: To keep India safe from Napoleonic danger
questions.option_c: To secure a fixed income for the Company
questions.option_d: To establish British paramountcy over the Indian States
questions.explanation: The statement that does not apply is securing a fixed income for the Company. The Subsidiary Alliance under Wellesley let the Company keep troops at the allied ruler's expense, guard against the Napoleonic threat and extend British paramountcy, but its aim was not to guarantee the Company a fixed income.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text:
Which of the following led to the introduction of English Education in India?
1. Charter Act of 1813
2. General Committee of Public Instruction, 1823
3. Orientalist and Anglicist Controversy.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: All three contributed to introducing English education in India. The Charter Act of 1813 earmarked funds for education, the General Committee of Public Instruction of 1823 oversaw its spread, and the Orientalist-Anglicist controversy led to the decision favouring English, so every item is relevant.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text: Which one of the following is an artificial lake?
questions.explanation: Kodaikanal lake in Tamil Nadu is a man-made reservoir created in the nineteenth century, making it an artificial lake. Kolleru, Nainital and Renuka are natural lakes formed by geographical processes, so they do not qualify.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
With reference to Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana, consider the following statements:
1. It is the flagship scheme of the Ministry of Labor and Employment.
2. It, among other things, will also impart training in soft skills, entrepreneurship, financial and digital literacy.
3. It aims to align the competencies of the unregulated workforce of the country to the National Skill Qualification Framework.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is wrong because the Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana is run by the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship, not the Labour Ministry. Statements 2 and 3 are correct, as the scheme imparts soft skills, entrepreneurship and financial and digital literacy, and aligns workers' competencies with the National Skill Qualification Framework.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text: In 1920, which of the following changed its name to "Swarajya Sabha"?
questions.explanation: In 1920 the All India Home Rule League, associated with the movement led by figures such as Annie Besant, was renamed the Swarajya Sabha. The other listed bodies did not undergo this change of name.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text: Which among the following events happened earliest?
questions.option_d: Satyendranath Tagore became the first Indian to succeed in the Indian Civil Services Examination.
questions.explanation: The earliest event is Dinabandhu Mitra's writing of 'Nil Darpan' in 1860. The Arya Samaj was founded in 1875, 'Anandamath' appeared in 1882, and Satyendranath Tagore entered the Civil Services in 1863, all later than the play.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
Which of the following is/are the possible consequence/s of heavy sand mining in river beds?
1. Decreased salinity in the river
2. Pollution of groundwater
3. Lowering of the water- table
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is wrong because heavy sand mining tends to increase, not decrease, salinity by allowing seawater intrusion. Statements 2 and 3 are correct, since it can pollute groundwater and lower the water table by disturbing the river bed and reducing aquifer recharge.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
With reference to agricultural soils, consider the following statements:
1. A high content of organic matter in soil drastically reduces its water holding capacity.
2. Soil does not play any role in the sulphur cycle.
3. Irrigation over a period of time can contribute to the salinization of some agricultural lands.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Only statement 3 is correct, as prolonged irrigation can raise salt levels and cause salinisation of farmland. Statement 1 is wrong because a higher organic matter content actually improves water-holding capacity, and statement 2 is wrong because soil is an active part of the sulphur cycle.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text: The Partnership for Action on Green Economy (PAGE), a UN mechanism to assist countries transition towards greener and more inclusive economies, emerged at
questions.option_a: The Earth Summit on Sustainable Development 2002, Johannesburg
questions.option_b: The United Nations Conference on Sustainable Development 2012, Rio de Janeiro
questions.option_c: The United Nations Framework Convention on Climate change 2015, Paris
questions.option_d: The World Sustainable Development Summit 2016, New Delhi
questions.explanation: The Partnership for Action on Green Economy emerged from the United Nations Conference on Sustainable Development held in 2012 at Rio de Janeiro, known as Rio+20. It was not launched at the earlier Johannesburg summit or the later climate meetings listed.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text:
"3D printing" has applications in which of the following?
1. Preparation of confectionery items
2. Manufacture of bionic ears
3. Automotive industry
4. Reconstructive surgeries
5. Data processing technologies
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: 3D printing has a broad range of uses spanning all the listed areas: making edible confectionery, producing bionic ears, manufacturing components for the automotive industry, aiding reconstructive surgery through custom implants, and supporting certain data processing technologies. Hence every item applies.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Consider the following statements:
1. The Barren Island volcano is an active volcano located in the Indian territory.
2. Barren Island lies about 140 km east of Great Nicobar.
3. The last time the Barren Island volcano erupted was in 1991 and it has remained inactive since then.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Only statement 1 is correct, as Barren Island in the Andaman Sea hosts India's only active volcano. Statement 2 misstates its location relative to Great Nicobar, and statement 3 is wrong because the volcano erupted again after 1991, showing renewed activity rather than remaining dormant.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text: Why is a plant called Prosopis juliflora often mentioned in news?
questions.option_a: Its extract is widely used in cosmetics.
questions.option_b: It tends to reduce the biodiversity in the area in which it grows.
questions.option_c: Its extract is used in the synthesis of pesticides.
questions.explanation: Prosopis juliflora, an introduced thorny shrub, is often in the news because it is highly invasive and tends to crowd out native plants, thereby reducing biodiversity in the areas where it spreads. Its notability stems from this ecological impact rather than cosmetic or pesticide uses.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Consider the following statements:
1. Most of the world's coral reefs are in tropical waters.
2. More than one-third of the world's coral reefs are located in the territories of Australia, Indonesia and Philippines.
3. Coral reefs host far more number of animal phyla than those hosted by tropical rainforests.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: All three statements are correct. Most coral reefs occur in warm tropical waters, a large share, more than a third, lies within the waters of Australia, Indonesia and the Philippines, and reefs support a greater number of animal phyla than even tropical rainforests.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text: "Momentum for Change : Climate Neutral Now" is an initiative launched by
questions.option_a: The Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change
questions.explanation: The 'Momentum for Change: Climate Neutral Now' initiative was launched by the secretariat of the UN Framework Convention on Climate Change to encourage voluntary reduction and offsetting of greenhouse gas emissions. It is not an IPCC, UNEP or WMO programme.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text:
With reference to educational institutions during colonial rule in India, consider the following pairs: Institution Founder
1. Sanskrit College at Benaras - William Jones
2. Calcutta Madarsa - Warren Hastings
3. Fort William College - Arthur Wellesley Which of the pairs given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Only pair 2 is correct, as the Calcutta Madrasa was founded by Warren Hastings. The Sanskrit College at Benaras was established under Jonathan Duncan rather than William Jones, and Fort William College was founded by Lord Richard Wellesley, not Arthur Wellesley, so those pairs are wrong.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Consider the following pairs:
Regions sometimes mentioned in news - Country
1. Catalonia - Spain
2. Crimea - Hungary
3. Mindanao - Philippines
4. Oromia - Nigeria Which of the pair given above are correctly matched?
questions.explanation: Pairs 1 and 3 are correct, with Catalonia being a region of Spain and Mindanao an island region of the Philippines. Crimea is part of Ukraine rather than Hungary, and Oromia lies in Ethiopia, not Nigeria, so those pairings are incorrect.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
Consider the following events:
1. The first democratically elected communist party government formed in a State in India.
2. India's then largest bank, 'Imperial Bank of India', was renamed 'State Bank of India'.
3. Air India was nationalised and became the national carrier.
4. Goa became a part of independent India.
Which of the following is the correct chronological sequence of the above events?
questions.explanation: The correct order is 3, 2, 1, 4. Air India was nationalised in 1953, the Imperial Bank of India became the State Bank of India in 1955, the first elected communist State government took office in Kerala in 1957, and Goa was integrated into India in 1961.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text:
Right to Privacy is protected as an intrinsic part of Right to Life and Personal Liberty.
Which of the following in the Constitution of India correctly and appropriately imply the above statement?
questions.option_a: Article 14 and the provisions under the 42nd Amendment to the Constitution
questions.option_b: Article 17 and the Directive Principles of State Policy in Part IV
questions.option_c: Article 21 and the freedoms guaranteed in Part III
questions.option_d: Article 24 and the provisions under the 44th Amendment to the Constitution
questions.explanation: The Supreme Court's affirmation of privacy rests on Article 21, which guarantees the right to life and personal liberty, read together with the other freedoms in Part III of the Constitution. This combination best captures privacy as an intrinsic part of life and liberty.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
Consider the following:
1. Areca nut
2. Barley
3. Coffee
4. Finger millet
5. Groundnut
6. Sesamum
7. Turmeric The Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs has announced the Minimum Support Price for which of the above?
questions.explanation: Among the listed items, Minimum Support Prices are announced for barley, finger millet (ragi), groundnut and sesamum, which corresponds to the correct combination. Areca nut, coffee and turmeric are not covered under the MSP regime, so options including them are wrong.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text: In which one of the following States is Pakhui Wildlife Sanctuary located?
questions.explanation: Pakhui, also known as Pakke, Wildlife Sanctuary and Tiger Reserve is located in Arunachal Pradesh in the north-eastern Himalayan foothills. It is not situated in Manipur, Meghalaya or Nagaland, so the other options are wrong.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text:
With reference to India's satellite launch vehicles, consider the following statements:
1. PSLVs launch the satellite useful for Earth resources monitoring whereas GSLVs are designed mainly to launch communication satellites.
2. Satellites launched by PSLV appear to remain permanently fixed in the same position in the sky, as viewed from a particular location in Earth.
3. GSLV Mk III is a four- staged launch vehicle with the first and third stages using solid rocket motors; and the second and fourth stages using liquid rocket engines.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Only statement 1 is correct: PSLVs mainly place Earth observation satellites in polar orbits, while GSLVs are designed chiefly for communication satellites. Statement 2 is wrong because PSLV payloads are not geostationary and do not appear fixed in the sky, and statement 3 misstates the staging of GSLV Mk III, which is a three-stage vehicle.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
With reference to the governance of public sector banking in India, consider the following statements:
1. Capital infusion into public sector banks by the Government of India has steadily increased in the last decade.
2. To put the public sector banks in order, the merger of associate banks with the parent State Bank of India has been affected.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Only statement 2 is correct, as the associate banks were merged into the parent State Bank of India to strengthen public sector banking. Statement 1 is wrong because government capital infusion into public sector banks has not risen steadily over the decade; it has fluctuated according to need.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
Consider the following items:
1. Cereals grains hulled
2. Chicken eggs cooked
3. Fish processed and canned
4. Newspapers containing advertising material
Which of the above items is/are exempted under GST (Goods and Services Tax)?
questions.explanation: Only item 1 is exempt, since hulled cereal grains fall under the list of goods free of GST. Cooked chicken eggs, processed and canned fish, and newspapers carrying advertising material do not enjoy the same exemption, so the other combinations are wrong.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
Consider the following statements:
1. The definition of "Critical Wildlife Habitat" is incorporated in the Forest Rights Act, 2006.
2. For the first time in India, Baigas have been given Habitat Rights.
3. Union Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change officially decides and declares Habitat Rights for Primitive and Vulnerable Tribal Groups in any part of India.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statements 1 and 2 are correct: the concept of a 'Critical Wildlife Habitat' is defined in the Forest Rights Act of 2006, and the Baiga community was among the first to be granted Habitat Rights. Statement 3 is wrong because such rights are decided at the State level rather than declared directly by the Union environment ministry.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text:
Consider the following
1. Birds
2. Dust blowing
3. Rain
4. Wind blowing
Which of the above spread plant diseases?
questions.explanation: Plant diseases can be carried and spread by all the listed agents. Birds and wind can transport pathogens or infected material, wind-blown dust can carry spores, and rain can splash and disperse disease-causing organisms, so every option applies.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
With reference to organic farming in India, consider the following statements:
1. 'The National Programme for Organic Production' (NPOP) is operated under the guidelines and directions of the Union Ministry of Rural Development.
2. 'The Agricultural and Processed Food Products Export Development Authority' (APEDA) functions as the Secretariat for the implementation of NPOP.
3. Sikkim has become India's first fully organic State.
Which of the statements given above is/are corect?
questions.explanation: Statements 2 and 3 are correct: APEDA acts as the secretariat implementing the National Programme for Organic Production, and Sikkim became India's first fully organic State. Statement 1 is wrong because the NPOP operates under the Ministry of Commerce and Industry, not the Ministry of Rural Development.

papers.source_note: Real questions from UPSC Civil Services Prelims 2017 (GS Paper I), a public government examination paper. Explanations are AIVORA-authored and original; not copied from any coaching material.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text: Which one of the following was a very important seaport in the Kakatiya kingdom?
questions.explanation: Motupalli, on the coast of present-day Andhra Pradesh, served as the principal trading port of the Kakatiya rulers, who issued the famous Motupalli inscription granting protection to foreign merchants. Kakinada and Machilipatnam rose to prominence in later periods, so Motupalli is the answer linked to the Kakatiya kingdom.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
With reference to ‘Global Climate Change Alliance’, which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. It is an initiative of the European Union.
2. It provides technical and financial support to targeted developing countries to integrate climate change into their development policies and budgets.
3. It is coordinated by World Resources Institute (WRI) and World Business Council for Sustainable Development (WBCSD).
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: The Global Climate Change Alliance was launched by the European Union to help vulnerable developing countries build resilience and mainstream climate concerns into their development planning and budgets, making statements 1 and 2 correct. It is not coordinated by WRI and WBCSD (that pairing relates to the Greenhouse Gas Protocol), so statement 3 is wrong.

questions.subject: Art & Culture
questions.text:
With reference to the religious history of India, consider the following statements:
1. Sautrantika and Sammitiya were the sects of Jainism.
2. Sarvastivadin held that the constituents of phenomena were not wholly momentary, but existed forever in a latent form.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Sautrantika and Sammitiya were schools of Buddhism, not Jainism, so statement 1 is incorrect. The Sarvastivada school did hold that the elements (dharmas) of phenomena exist across past, present and future in a subtle latent form rather than being purely momentary, making statement 2 correct.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Mediterranean Sea is a border of which of the following countries?
1. Jordan
2. Iraq
3. Lebanon
4. Syria
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Among the listed countries, only Lebanon and Syria have coastlines along the Mediterranean Sea. Jordan is largely landlocked with only a short Red Sea outlet at Aqaba, and Iraq borders the Persian Gulf, so statements 3 and 4 alone are correct.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
With reference to ‘National Investment and Infrastructure Fund’, which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. It is an organ of NITI Aayog.
2. It has a corpus of Rs. 4,00,000 crore at present.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: The National Investment and Infrastructure Fund is a government-backed fund managed as a separate entity, not an organ of NITI Aayog, so statement 1 is wrong. Its planned corpus was around Rs 40,000 crore, far short of Rs 4,00,000 crore, so statement 2 is also wrong, making neither statement correct.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text: The Global Infrastructure Facility is a/an
questions.option_a: ASEAN initiative to upgrade infrastructure in Asia and financed by credit from the Asian Development Bank.
questions.option_b: World Bank collaboration that facilities the preparation and structuring of complex infrastructure Public-Private Partnership (PPPs) to enable mobilization of private sector and institutional investor capital.
questions.option_c: Collaboration among the major banks of the world working with the OECD and infrastructure projects that have the potential to mobilize private investment.
questions.option_d: UNCTAD funded initiative that seeks to finance and facilitate infrastructure development in the World.
questions.explanation: The Global Infrastructure Facility is a World Bank-led platform that helps governments prepare and structure complex infrastructure projects, especially public-private partnerships, so as to attract private and institutional investment. It is not an ASEAN, OECD-bank consortium or UNCTAD initiative, making option B the accurate description.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text: For election to the Lok Sabha, a nomination paper can be filed by
questions.option_b: a resident of the constituency from which the election is to be contested.
questions.option_c: any citizen of India whose name appears in the electoral roll of a constituency.
questions.explanation: To contest a Lok Sabha election, a person must be a citizen of India whose name is entered in the electoral roll of any parliamentary constituency in the country. One need not reside in or be a voter of the specific constituency being contested, so option C is the correct condition.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Consider the following statements:
1. In India, the Himalayas are spread over five states only.
2. Western Ghats are spread over five states only.
3. Pulicat Lake is spread over two States only.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The Himalayas stretch across far more than five Indian states, and the Western Ghats run through six states, so statements 1 and 2 are wrong. Pulicat Lake straddles only Andhra Pradesh and Tamil Nadu, so statement 3 alone is correct.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text: Biological Oxygen Demand (BOD) is a standard criterion for
questions.option_b: Computing oxygen levels in forest ecosystems
questions.option_d: Assessing oxygen levels in high altitude regions
questions.explanation: Biological Oxygen Demand measures the amount of dissolved oxygen consumed by micro-organisms while decomposing organic matter in water. A high BOD indicates heavy organic pollution and low available oxygen, so it is used as an indicator of pollution in aquatic ecosystems.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
With reference to the role of UN-Habitat in the United Nations Programme working towards a better urban future, which of the statements is/are correct?
1. UN-Habitat has been mandated by the United Nations General Assembly to promote socially and environmentally sustainable towns and cities to provide adequate shelter for all.
2. Its partners are either governments or local urban authorities only.
3. UN-Habitat contributed to the overall objective of the United Nations system to reduce poverty and to promote access to safe drinking water and basic sanitation.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: UN-Habitat is mandated by the UN General Assembly to promote sustainable towns and cities and adequate shelter for all, and it supports poverty reduction and access to safe water and sanitation, making statements 1 and 3 correct. Its partners include NGOs, civil society and others, not only governments or local authorities, so statement 2 is wrong.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
With reference to ‘National Skills Qualification Framework (NSQF)’, which of the statements given below is/are correct?
1. Under NSQF, a learner can acquire the certification for competency only through formal learning.
2. An outcome expected from the implementation of NSQF is the mobility between vocational and general education.
Select the correct answer using the code give below:
questions.explanation: The National Skills Qualification Framework recognises competencies acquired through formal, non-formal and informal learning, so certification is not limited to formal learning, making statement 1 wrong. A key aim of NSQF is to enable mobility between vocational and general education streams, so statement 2 is correct.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text: In the context of Indian history, the principle of ‘Dyarchy (diarchy)’ refers to
questions.option_a: Division of the central legislature into two houses.
questions.option_b: Introductions of double government i.e., Central and Statement governments.
questions.option_c: Having two sets of rulers; one in London and another in Delhi.
questions.option_d: Division of the subjects delegated to the provinces into two categories.
questions.explanation: Dyarchy, introduced by the Government of India Act of 1919, split provincial subjects into two categories: 'reserved' subjects administered by the Governor and his executive council, and 'transferred' subjects handled by ministers responsible to the legislature. It thus refers to dividing provincial subjects into two categories.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
Consider the following in respect of ‘National Career Services’:
1. National Career Service is an initiative of the Department of Personnel and Training, Government of India.
2. National Career Service has been launched in a Mission Mode to improve the employment opportunities to uneducated youth of the country.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The National Career Service portal was launched by the Ministry of Labour and Employment, not the Department of Personnel and Training, so statement 1 is wrong. It was indeed launched in mission mode to improve employment opportunities and career services, so statement 2 is correct.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text: Which of the following statements best describes the term ‘Scheme for Sustainable Structuring of Stressed Assets (S4A)’, recently seen in the news?
questions.option_a: It is a procedure for considering ecological costs of developmental schemes formulated by the Government.
questions.option_b: It is a scheme of RBI for reworking the financial structure of big corporate entities facing genuine difficulties.
questions.option_c: It is a disinvestment plan of the Government regarding Central Public Sector Undertakings.
questions.option_d: It is an important provision in ‘The Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code’ recently implemented by the Government.
questions.explanation: The Scheme for Sustainable Structuring of Stressed Assets was an RBI mechanism allowing lenders to restructure the debt of large corporate borrowers facing genuine difficulties by separating sustainable from unsustainable debt. It was neither an environmental costing tool, a disinvestment plan, nor a clause of the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, so option B is correct.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
Consider the following statements:
1. Climate and Clean Air Coalition (CCAC) to Reduce Short Lived Climate Pollutants is a unique initiative of G2O group of countries.
2. The CCAC focuses on methane, black carbon and hydrofluorocarbons.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The Climate and Clean Air Coalition is a voluntary partnership of governments and organisations, not an initiative of the G20, so statement 1 is wrong. It specifically targets short-lived climate pollutants such as methane, black carbon and hydrofluorocarbons, making statement 2 correct.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
With reference to ‘Indian Ocean Dipole (IOD)’ sometimes mentioned in the news while forecasting Indian monsoon, which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. IOD phenomenon is characterised by a difference in sea surface temperature between tropical Western Indian Ocean and tropical Eastern Pacific Ocean.
2. An IOD phenomenon can influence an El Nino’s impact on the monsoon.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: The Indian Ocean Dipole is defined by the difference in sea surface temperature between the tropical western and tropical eastern Indian Ocean, not the eastern Pacific, so statement 1 is wrong. A positive IOD can offset the drying effect of an El Nino on the Indian monsoon, so statement 2 is correct.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text: If you want to see gharials in their natural habitat, which one of the following is the best place to visit?
questions.explanation: Gharials are fish-eating crocodilians that thrive in clean, fast-flowing rivers, and the Chambal River, protected as the National Chambal Sanctuary, hosts one of their most important wild populations. Mangroves, brackish lagoons and wetlands like the other options are unsuitable, making the Chambal River the best place to see them.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
Consider the following in respect of Indian Ocean Naval Symposium (IONS):
1. Inaugural IONS was held in India in 2015 under the chairmanship of the Indian Navy.
2. IONS is a voluntary initiative that seeks to increase maritime co- operation among navies of the littoral states of the Indian Ocean Region.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The inaugural Indian Ocean Naval Symposium was held in India in 2008, not 2015, so statement 1 is wrong. IONS is indeed a voluntary initiative to boost maritime cooperation among the navies of Indian Ocean littoral states, making statement 2 correct.

questions.subject: Art & Culture
questions.text: The painting of Bodhisattva Padmapani is one of the most famous and oft- illustrated paintings at
questions.explanation: The celebrated mural of Bodhisattva Padmapani, shown holding a lotus, is painted in Cave 1 of the Ajanta cave complex in Maharashtra. It is one of the most reproduced works of ancient Indian painting, so Ajanta is the correct site.

questions.subject: Art & Culture
questions.text:
Consider the following pairs:
Traditions Communities
1. Chaliha Sahib Festival – Sindhis
2. Nanda Raj Jaat Yatra – Gonds
3. Wari-Warkari - Santhals Which of the pairs given above is/are correctly matched?
questions.explanation: The Chaliha Sahib festival is celebrated by the Sindhi community, so pair 1 is correctly matched. The Nanda Raj Jat Yatra is associated with Uttarakhand rather than the Gonds, and the Wari-Warkari tradition belongs to Maharashtra's Vithoba devotees rather than the Santhals, so pairs 2 and 3 are wrong.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
Which of the following statements can help in water conservation in agriculture?
1. Reduced or zero tillage of the land
2. Applying gypsum before irrigating the field
3. Allowing crop residue to remain in the field
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: All three practices conserve water: reduced or zero tillage limits soil disturbance and evaporation, applying gypsum improves soil structure and water infiltration in problem soils, and leaving crop residue on the field acts as mulch that retains soil moisture. Hence all three statements are correct.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
Consider the following statements:
The nation-wide ‘Soil Health Card Scheme’ aims at 1. expanding the cultivable area under irrigation. 2. enabling the banks to assess the quantum of loans to be granted to farmers on the basis of soil quality. 3. checking the overuse of fertilizers in farmlands.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The Soil Health Card scheme tests soils and advises farmers on balanced nutrient use, which chiefly helps curb the excessive application of fertilizers, so statement 3 is correct. It does not aim to expand irrigated area or serve as a basis for assessing farm loans, so statements 1 and 2 are wrong.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text:
Consider the following pairs:
Commonly used/consumed materials Unwanted or controversial chemicals likely to be found in them
1. Lipstick – Lead
2. Soft drinks – Brominated vegetable oils
3. Chinese fast food – Monosodium glutamate Which of the pairs given above is/are correctly matched?
questions.explanation: All three pairs are correctly matched: lipsticks have been found to contain trace lead, brominated vegetable oil has been used as a controversial additive in some soft drinks, and monosodium glutamate is a flavour enhancer commonly associated with Chinese fast food. Hence all three are correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text:
Organic Light Emitting Diodes (OLEDs) are used to create digital display in many devices. What are the advantages of OLED displays over Liquid Crystal displays?
1. OLED displays can be fabricated on flexible plastic substrates.
2. Roll-up displays embedded in clothing can be made using OLEDs.
3. Transparent displays are possible using OLEDs.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.option_d: None of the above statements is correct
questions.explanation: OLED displays are self-emissive and built from thin organic layers, so they can be fabricated on flexible plastic substrates, made into roll-up displays embedded in fabric, and even produced as transparent panels. All three advantages over LCDs are therefore valid.

questions.subject: Art & Culture
questions.text:
Which of the following is/are famous for Sun temples?
1. Arasavalli
2. Amarakantak
3. Omkareshwar
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Arasavalli in Andhra Pradesh is renowned for its ancient Suryanarayana Sun temple, so statement 1 is correct. Amarkantak is known as the source of the Narmada and for Shiva shrines, while Omkareshwar is famous for a Jyotirlinga, so neither is noted for Sun temples.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text:
Consider the following statements:
1. In the election for Lok Sabha or State Assembly, the winning candidate must get at least 50 percent of the votes polled, to be declared elected.
2. According to the provisions laid down in the Constitution of India, in Lok Sabha, the Speaker’s post goes to the majority party and the Deputy Speaker’s to the Opposition.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Indian elections use the first-past-the-post system, where the candidate with the most votes wins even without an absolute majority, so statement 1 is wrong. The Constitution does not reserve the Deputy Speaker's post for the Opposition; it is a matter of convention, so statement 2 is also wrong.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
Which of the following has/have occurred in India after its liberalization of economic policies in 1991?
1. Share of agriculture in GDP increased enormously.
2. Share of India’s exports in world trade increased.
3. FDI inflows increased.
4. India’s foreign exchange reserves increased enormously.
Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.explanation: After the 1991 reforms, agriculture's share in GDP actually declined rather than increased, so statement 1 is wrong. India's share in world exports rose, FDI inflows grew, and foreign exchange reserves expanded enormously, making statements 2, 3 and 4 correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text: What is the application of Somatic Cell Nuclear Transfer Technology?
questions.option_d: Production of organisms free of diseases
questions.explanation: Somatic Cell Nuclear Transfer involves inserting the nucleus of a body (somatic) cell into an egg whose own nucleus has been removed, which is the technique used for reproductive cloning of animals, famously demonstrated with Dolly the sheep. Hence its application is reproductive cloning.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
Consider the following statements:
1. National Payment Corporation of India (NPCI) helps in promoting the financial inclusion in the country.
2. NPCI has launched RuPay, a card payment scheme.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The National Payments Corporation of India is the umbrella body for retail payments and works to widen financial inclusion, and it developed the RuPay domestic card payment scheme. Both statements are therefore correct.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text: The term ‘M-STrIPES’ is sometimes seen in the news in the context of
questions.explanation: M-STrIPES (Monitoring System for Tigers - Intensive Protection and Ecological Status) is a software-based tool used by forest staff to record patrols, sightings and habitat data in tiger reserves. It thus relates to the maintenance and monitoring of tiger reserves.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
What is/are the most likely advantages of implementing ‘Goods and Services Tax (GST)’?
1. It will replace multiple taxes collected by multiple authorities and will thus create a single market in India.
2. It will drastically reduce the ‘Current Account Deficit’ of India and will enable it to increase its foreign exchange reserves.
3. It will enormously increase the growth and size of economy of India and will enable it to overtake China in the near future.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: GST replaced a web of central and state indirect taxes with a unified levy, helping create a single national market, so statement 1 is correct. Claims that it would sharply cut the current account deficit or let India overtake China's economy soon are unsupported, so statements 2 and 3 are wrong.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text: 'Broad-based Trade and Investment Agreement (BTIA)’ is sometimes seen in the news in the context of negotiations held between India and
questions.option_c: Organization for Economic Cooperation and Development
questions.explanation: The Broad-based Trade and Investment Agreement refers to the long-running free-trade negotiations between India and the European Union. It is not linked with the Gulf Cooperation Council, OECD or the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation, so option A is correct.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
Consider the following statements:
1. India has ratified to Trade Facilitation Agreement (TFA) of WTO.
2. TFA is a part of WTO’s Bali Ministerial Package of 2013.
3. TFA came into force in January 2016.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: India ratified the WTO Trade Facilitation Agreement, and the TFA was indeed part of the Bali Ministerial Package of 2013, so statements 1 and 2 are correct. However, the TFA entered into force in February 2017, not January 2016, so statement 3 is wrong.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text: What is the importance of developing Chabahar Port by India?
questions.option_a: India’s trade with African countries will enormously increase.
questions.option_b: India’s relations with oil- producing Arab countries will be strengthened.
questions.option_c: India will not depend on Pakistan for access to Afghanistan and Central Asia.
questions.option_d: Pakistan will facilitate and protect the installation of a gas pipeline between Iraq and India.
questions.explanation: Developing Iran's Chabahar Port gives India a sea-land route to Afghanistan and Central Asia that bypasses Pakistan, which has denied India overland transit. Its strategic value lies in reducing dependence on Pakistan for such access, making option C correct.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
In India, it is legally mandatory for which of the following to report on cyber security incidents?
1. Service providers
2. Data centres
3. Body corporate
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Under India's Information Technology Act and CERT-In rules, service providers, intermediaries, data centres and body corporates are all legally required to report cyber security incidents to CERT-In. Hence all three are covered.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text: Right to vote and to be elected in India is a
questions.explanation: In India the right to vote and to contest elections is created by the Representation of the People Act and related statutes rather than by the Constitution's fundamental rights chapter, so it is regarded as a legal right that Parliament can regulate. Hence it is classified as a legal right.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text: What is the purpose of ‘evolved Laser Interferometer Space Antenna (eLISA)’ project?
questions.option_c: To detect the effectiveness of missile defence system
questions.option_d: To study the effect of solar flares on our communication systems
questions.explanation: The evolved Laser Interferometer Space Antenna is a planned space-based observatory designed to detect gravitational waves, the ripples in spacetime predicted by Einstein, especially at low frequencies inaccessible to ground detectors. Its purpose is therefore detecting gravitational waves.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
What is the purpose of ‘Vidyanjali Yojana’?
1. To enable the famous foreign educational institutions to open their campuses in India.
2. To increase the quality of education provided in government schools by taking help from the private sector and the community.
3. To encourage voluntary monetary contributions from private individuals and organizations so as to improve the infrastructure facilities for primary and secondary schools.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Vidyanjali Yojana is a school volunteer programme that lets private individuals and organisations contribute their time and skills to improve the quality of teaching in government schools, matching statement 2. It is not about foreign campuses or soliciting monetary donations for infrastructure, so statements 1 and 3 are wrong.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text: What is the aim of the programme ‘Unnat Bharat Abhiyan’?
questions.option_a: Achieving 100% literacy by promoting collaboration between voluntary organizations and government’s education system and local communities.
questions.option_b: Connecting institutions of higher education with local communities to address development challenges through appropriate technologies.
questions.option_c: Strengthening India’s scientific research institutions in order to make India a scientific and technological power.
questions.option_d: Developing human capital by allocating special funds for health care and education of rural and urban poor, and organizing skill development programmes and vocational training for them.
questions.explanation: Unnat Bharat Abhiyan links higher education institutions such as the IITs and other colleges with nearby rural communities so that appropriate technologies and knowledge can be applied to address local development challenges. Hence option B best describes its aim.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text:
Consider the following statements:
1. The Election Commission of India is a five- member body.
2. Union Ministry of Home Affairs decides the election schedule for the conduct of both general elections and bye-elections.
3. Election Commission resolves the disputes relating to splits/mergers of recognized political parties.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The Election Commission is presently a three-member body, not five, so statement 1 is wrong, and it is the Commission, not the Home Ministry, that fixes the election schedule, so statement 2 is wrong. The Commission does adjudicate disputes over splits and mergers of recognised parties, so statement 3 alone is correct.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text: In India, if a species of tortoise is declared protected under Schedule I of the Wildlife (Protection) Act, 1972, what does it imply?
questions.option_a: It enjoys the same level of protection as the tiger.
questions.option_b: It no longer exists in the wild, a few individuals are under captive protection; and not it is impossible to prevent its extinction.
questions.option_c: It is endemic to a particular region of India.
questions.option_d: Both (b) and (c) stated above are correct in this context.
questions.explanation: Schedule I of the Wildlife (Protection) Act provides the highest level of protection with the strictest penalties, the same category that covers the tiger. So a tortoise placed under Schedule I enjoys protection equal to that of the tiger, and the other options about extinction or endemism are not implied.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text: In India, Judicial Review implies
questions.option_a: the power of the Judiciary to pronounce upon the constitutionality of laws and executive orders.
questions.option_b: the power of the Judiciary to question the wisdom of the laws enacted by the Legislatures.
questions.option_c: the power of the Judiciary to review all the legislative enactments before they are assented to by the President.
questions.option_d: the power of the Judiciary to review its own judgements given earlier in similar or different cases.
questions.explanation: Judicial review is the power of the courts to examine laws made by legislatures and actions taken by the executive and to strike them down if they violate the Constitution. It concerns constitutionality, not the wisdom of laws, pre-assent scrutiny, or review of past judgments, so option A is correct.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text:
With reference to Indian freedom struggle, consider the following events:
1. Mutiny in Royal Indian Navy
2. Quit Indian Movement launched
3. Second Round Table Conference What is the correct chronological sequence of the above events?
questions.explanation: Arranged chronologically, the Second Round Table Conference took place in 1931, the Quit India Movement was launched in 1942, and the Royal Indian Navy mutiny occurred in 1946. The correct sequence is therefore 3-2-1.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
Consider the following statements:
1. Tax revenue as a percent of GDP of India has steadily increased in the last decade.
2. Fiscal deficit as a percent of GDP of India has steadily increased in the last decade.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: India's tax-to-GDP ratio has fluctuated rather than risen steadily over the decade, so statement 1 is wrong. The fiscal deficit as a share of GDP has generally been on a declining or uneven path following fiscal consolidation, not a steady rise, so statement 2 is also wrong.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text: Recently there was a proposal to translocate some of the lions from their habitat in Gujarat to which one of the following sites?
questions.explanation: The proposal to establish a second home for the Asiatic lions of Gujarat's Gir forest identified Kuno Palpur Wildlife Sanctuary in Madhya Pradesh as the relocation site. The other parks were not the designated destination, so option B is correct.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text:
Which of the following are not necessarily the consequences of the proclamation of the President’s rule in a State?
1. Dissolution of the State Legislative Assembly
2. Removal of the Council of Ministers in the State
3. Dissolution of the local bodies
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: When President's Rule is imposed, the state Council of Ministers is dismissed, so removal of ministers is a necessary consequence and is not the answer. However, the Assembly may only be suspended rather than dissolved, and local bodies are not necessarily dissolved, so statements 1 and 3 are the ones not necessarily entailed.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text:
Which of the following are envisaged by the Right against Exploitation in the Constitution of India?
1. Prohibition of traffic in human beings and forced labour
2. Abolition of untouchability
3. Protection of the interests of minorities
4. Prohibition of employment of children in factories and mines
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: The Right against Exploitation, in Articles 23 and 24, prohibits human trafficking and forced labour and bans the employment of children in hazardous work such as factories and mines, so statements 1 and 4 belong to it. Abolition of untouchability and protection of minorities fall under other articles, so 2 and 3 are excluded.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text: Which of the following is geographically closest to Great Nicobar?
questions.explanation: Great Nicobar, the southernmost island of the Andaman and Nicobar chain, lies closest to the Indonesian island of Sumatra across the narrow stretch near Indira Point. Borneo, Java and Sri Lanka are all considerably farther away, so Sumatra is correct.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text: Out of the following statements, choose the one that brings out the principle underlying the Cabinet form of Government:
questions.option_a: An arrangement for minimizing the criticism against the Government whose responsibilities are complex and hard to carry out the satisfaction of all.
questions.option_b: A mechanism for speeding up the activities of the Government whose responsibilities are increasing day by day.
questions.option_c: A mechanism of parliamentary democracy for ensuring collective responsibility of the Government to the people.
questions.option_d: A device for strengthening the hands of the head of the Government whose hold over the people is in a state of decline.
questions.explanation: The cabinet system rests on the principle of collective responsibility, under which the council of ministers acts as a unit and remains jointly answerable to the legislature, and through it to the people. Option C captures this underlying principle of parliamentary democracy.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text: Which one of the following is not a feature to Indian federalism?
questions.option_a: There is an independent judiciary in India.
questions.option_b: Powers have been clearly divided between the Centre and the States.
questions.option_c: The federating units have been given unequal representation in the Rajya Sabha.
questions.option_d: It is the result of an agreement among the federating units.
questions.explanation: Indian federalism was not created by an agreement among pre-existing states; the Constituent Assembly formed an indestructible union, so option D is not a feature and is the answer. An independent judiciary, division of powers, and unequal state representation in the Rajya Sabha are all genuine features.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text: The object of the Butler Committee of 1927 was to
questions.option_a: Define the jurisdiction of the Central and Provincial Governments.
questions.option_b: Define the powers of the Secretary of State for India.
questions.option_d: Improve the relationship between the Government of India and the Indian States.
questions.explanation: The Butler Committee of 1927 was appointed to examine and clarify the relationship between the British Government of India (the paramount power) and the Indian princely states. Its object was therefore to improve relations between the Government of India and the Indian states.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text: The term ‘Domestic Content Requirement’ is sometimes seen in the news with reference to
questions.option_a: Developing solar power production in our country
questions.option_b: Granting licenses to foreign T.V. channels in our country
questions.option_c: Exporting our food products to other countries
questions.option_d: Permitting foreign educational institutions to set up their campuses in our country
questions.explanation: The Domestic Content Requirement clause, which mandated using domestically manufactured cells and modules, featured prominently in India's solar power programme and drew a WTO challenge from the United States. It is thus associated with developing solar power production in the country.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
Consider the following statements:
1. The Nuclear Security Summits are periodically held under the aegis of the United Nations.
2. The International Panel on Fissile Materials is an organ of International Atomic Energy Agency.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The Nuclear Security Summits were a series of meetings initiated by the United States, not held under the aegis of the United Nations, so statement 1 is wrong. The International Panel on Fissile Materials is an independent group of experts, not an organ of the IAEA, so statement 2 is also wrong.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text: Who among the following can join the National Pension System (NPS)?
questions.option_b: Persons of age from 21 to 55 only
questions.option_c: All State Government employees joining the services after the state of notification by the respective State Governments
questions.option_d: All Central Government employees including those of Armed Forces joining the services on or after 1st April, 2004
questions.explanation: The National Pension System is open widely to citizens and NRIs across a broad age band, so it is not restricted to resident citizens or a 21-to-55 age limit. For states, the scheme applies to employees joining after the respective state government issues its notification, making option C correct.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
With reference to river Teesta, consider the following statements:
1. The source of river Teesta is the same as that of Brahmaputra but it flows through Sikkim.
2. River Rangeet originates in Sikkim and it is a tributary of river Teesta.
3. River Teesta flows into Bay of Bengal on the border of India and Bangladesh.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The Teesta rises from glaciers and lakes in Sikkim and does not share the source of the Brahmaputra, so statement 1 is wrong, and it joins the Brahmaputra in Bangladesh rather than flowing directly into the Bay of Bengal, so statement 3 is wrong. The Rangeet does originate in Sikkim and is a tributary of the Teesta, so statement 2 alone is correct.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
Consider the following statements:
1. In tropical regions, Zika virus disease is transmitted by the same mosquito that transmits dengue.
2. Sexual transmission of Zika virus disease is possible.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The Zika virus is spread mainly by Aedes mosquitoes, the same genus that transmits dengue, so statement 1 is correct, and the virus can also be transmitted sexually, so statement 2 is correct. Both statements therefore hold.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
Consider the following statements:
1. The Standard Mark of Bureau of Indian Standards (BIS) is mandatory for automotive tyres and tubes.
2. AGMARK is a quality Certification Mark issues by the Food and Agriculture Organisation (FAO).
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: BIS certification (ISI mark) is indeed mandatory for automotive tyres and tubes, so statement 1 is correct. AGMARK is issued by the Directorate of Marketing and Inspection under India's agriculture ministry, not by the FAO, so statement 2 is wrong.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
What is/are the advantage/advantages of implementing the ‘National Agriculture Market’ scheme?
1. It is a pan-India electronic trading portal for agricultural commodities.
2. It provides the farmers access to nationwide market, with prices commensurate with the quality of their produce.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: The National Agriculture Market (e-NAM) is a pan-India electronic trading portal that networks agricultural produce market yards, so statement 1 is correct. By linking mandis, it gives farmers access to a wider market and prices reflecting the quality of their produce, so statement 2 is also correct.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
With reference to the ‘National Intellectual Property Rights Policy’, consider the following statements:
1. It reiterates India’s commitment to the Doha Development Agenda and the TRIPS Agreement.
2. Department of Industrial Policy and Promotion is the nodal agency for regulating intellectual property rights in India.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The National IPR Policy reaffirms India's commitment to the Doha Development Agenda and the TRIPS Agreement, so statement 1 is correct, and the Department of Industrial Policy and Promotion was designated the nodal agency for IPR matters, so statement 2 is correct. Both statements hold.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
According to the Wildlife (Protection) Act, 1972, which of the following animals cannot be hunted by any person except under some provisions provided by law?
1. Gharial
2. Indian wild ass
3. Wild buffalo
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: The gharial, the Indian wild ass and the wild buffalo are all placed under Schedule I of the Wildlife (Protection) Act, which grants the highest protection and prohibits their hunting except under special legal provisions. Hence all three are correct.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text:
Which of the following statements is/are true of the Fundamental Duties of an Indian citizen?
1. A legislative process has been provided to enforce these duties.
2. They are correlative to legal duties.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: The Fundamental Duties in Article 51A are non-justiciable and have no legislative mechanism built in to enforce them, so statement 1 is wrong. They are moral obligations of citizens rather than being correlative to legal duties, so statement 2 is also wrong, making neither correct.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text:
Consider the following pairs:
1. Radhakanta Deb – First President of the British Indian Association
2. Gazulu Lakshminarasu Chetty – Founder of the Madras Mahajana Sabha
3. Surendranath Banerjee – Founder of the Indian Association
Which of the above pairs is/are correctly matched?
questions.explanation: Radhakanta Deb was the first president of the British Indian Association, so pair 1 is correct, and Surendranath Banerjee founded the Indian Association, so pair 3 is correct. Gazulu Lakshminarasu Chetty is linked with the Madras Native Association rather than founding the Madras Mahajana Sabha, so pair 2 is wrong.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text: Which one of the following objectives is not embodied in the Preamble to the Constitution of India?
questions.explanation: The Preamble promises liberty of thought, expression, belief, faith and worship, but it does not mention 'economic liberty' as such. Hence economic liberty is the objective not embodied in the Preamble.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
With reference to the ‘Quality Council of India (QCI)’, consider the following statements:
1. QCI was set up jointly by the Government of India and the Indian Industry.
2. Chairman of QCI is appointed by the Prime Minister on the recommendations of the industry to the Government.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The Quality Council of India was set up jointly by the Government of India and Indian industry associations, so statement 1 is correct, and its chairman is appointed by the Prime Minister on the recommendation of industry, so statement 2 is correct. Both statements hold.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
What is the purpose of setting up of Small Finance Banks (SFBs) in India?
1. To supply credit to small business units
2. To supply credit to small and marginal farmers
3. To encourage young entrepreneurs to set up business particularly in rural areas.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Small Finance Banks were created to extend credit to underserved segments such as small business units and small and marginal farmers, so statements 1 and 2 are correct. There is no specific mandate limited to encouraging young rural entrepreneurs, so statement 3 is not a stated purpose.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
With reference to ‘Asia Pacific Ministerial Conference on Housing and Urban Development (APMCHUD)’, consider the following statements:
1. The first APMCHUD was held in India in 2006 on the theme ‘Emerging Urban Forms – Policy Responses and Governance Structure’.
2. India hosts all the Annual Ministerial Conferences in partnership with ADB, APEC and ASEAN.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The first APMCHUD was held in India in 2006, but its theme was on empowering the poor in an urbanising context rather than 'Emerging Urban Forms', so statement 1 as worded is incorrect. India also does not host all the annual conferences in partnership with ADB, APEC and ASEAN, so statement 2 is wrong, making neither correct.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text: Democracy’s superior virtue lies in the fact that it calls into activity
questions.option_a: the intelligence and character of ordinary men and women.
questions.option_b: the methods for strengthening executive leadership.
questions.option_c: a superior individual with dynamism and vision.
questions.option_d: a band on dedicated party workers.
questions.explanation: Democracy's distinctive strength is that it draws out and relies on the intelligence, judgement and character of ordinary citizens by involving them in self-government. It is not chiefly about strengthening a single leader or executive, so option A best captures this virtue.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text: Which of the following is a most likely consequence of implementing the ‘Unified Payments Interface (UPI)?
questions.option_a: Mobile wallets with not be necessary for online payments.
questions.option_b: Digital currency will totally replace the physical currency in about two decades.
questions.option_d: Direct transfer of subsidies to poor people will become very effective.
questions.explanation: The Unified Payments Interface lets users transfer money directly between bank accounts through a mobile app using a virtual address, so a separate mobile wallet is no longer essential for online payments. This makes option A the most likely consequence.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text: The terms ‘Event Horizon’, ‘Singularity’, ‘String Theory’ and ‘Standard Model’ are sometimes seen in the news in the context of
questions.option_a: Observation and understanding of the Universe
questions.option_b: Study of the solar and the lunar eclipses
questions.option_c: Placing satellites in the orbit of the Earth
questions.option_d: Origin and evolution of living organisms on the earth
questions.explanation: Terms like event horizon, singularity, string theory and the Standard Model belong to physics and cosmology concerning the nature of black holes, fundamental particles and the structure of the universe. They relate to the observation and understanding of the universe, so option A is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text:
With reference to agriculture in India, how can the technique of ‘genome sequencing’, often seen in the news, be used in the immediate future?
1. Genome sequencing can be used to identify genetic markers for disease resistance and drought tolerance in various crop plants.
2. This technique helps in reducing the time required to develop new varieties of crop plants.
3. It can be used to decipher the host- pathogen relationships in crops.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Genome sequencing lets scientists identify genetic markers for traits like disease resistance and drought tolerance, speeds up the breeding of new crop varieties, and helps decode host-pathogen interactions. All three applications are therefore valid.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text: The main advantage of the parliamentary form of governments is that
questions.option_a: the executive and legislature work independently.
questions.option_b: it provides continuity of policy and is more efficient.
questions.option_c: the executive remains responsible to the legislature.
questions.option_d: the head of the government cannot be changed without election.
questions.explanation: The chief merit of the parliamentary system is that the executive is drawn from and remains continuously accountable to the legislature, which can remove it through a vote of no confidence. This responsibility of the executive to the legislature is captured by option C.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text: In the context of India, which one of the following is the correct relationship between Rights and Duties?
questions.option_b: Rights are personal and hence independent of society and Duties.
questions.option_c: Rights, not Duties, are important for the advancement of the personality of the citizen.
questions.option_d: Duties, not Rights, are important for the stability of the State.
questions.explanation: In democratic theory rights and duties are correlative: the rights an individual enjoys impose corresponding duties on others and on that individual toward society. Rights are not independent of society, so option A correctly states the relationship.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text: The mind of the makers of the Constitution of India is reflected in which of the following?
questions.option_c: The Directive Principles of State Policy
questions.explanation: The Preamble sets out the ideals, aims and philosophy underlying the Constitution, which is why it is regarded as reflecting the mind and intentions of its framers. Hence option A is correct.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text: If you travel by road from Kohima to Kottayam, what is the minimum number of States within India through which you can travel, including the origin and the destination?
questions.explanation: Travelling by road from Kohima in Nagaland to Kottayam in Kerala, the shortest feasible route passes through a minimum of seven states, including the origin and destination. Hence the answer is 7.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text:
The Parliament of India exercises control over the functions of the Council of Ministers through
1. Adjournment motion
2. Questions hour
3. Supplementary questions
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: Parliament checks the Council of Ministers through several devices, and the adjournment motion, the question hour, and supplementary questions are all such instruments of oversight and accountability. All three are therefore correct.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text:
With reference to the Parliament of India, consider the following statements:
1. A private member’s bill is a bill presented by a Member of Parliament who is not elected but only nominated by the President of India.
2. Recently, a private member’s bill has been passed in the Parliament of India for the first time in its history.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: A private member's bill is one introduced by any MP who is not a minister, whether elected or nominated, so limiting it to nominated members makes statement 1 wrong. Private member's bills have been passed earlier in Indian parliamentary history, so the claim of a first-ever passage is also wrong, making neither correct.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text:
With reference to the difference between the culture of Rigvedic Aryans and Indus Valley people, which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. Rigvedic Aryans used the coat of mail and helmet in warfare whereas the people of Indus Valley Civilization did not leave any evidence of using them.
2. Rigvedic Aryans knew gold, silver and copper whereas Indus Valley people knew only copper and iron.
3. Rigvedic Aryans had domesticated the horse whereas there is no evidence of Indus Valley people having aware of this animal.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: The Rigvedic Aryans used the horse and there is no clear evidence of the horse in the Indus civilization, so statement 3 is correct while statement 1 about armour is not established as a difference. Statement 2 is wrong because the Indus people knew gold, silver, copper and bronze but not iron, so only statement 3 holds—the intended key marks statement 1 as the answer based on horse and warfare evidence.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text: Recognition of Prior Learning Scheme’ is sometimes mentioned in the news with reference to
questions.option_a: Certifying the skills acquire by construction workers through traditional channels.
questions.option_b: Enrolling the persons in Universities for distance learning programmes.
questions.option_c: Reserving some skilled jobs to rural and urban poor in some public sector undertakings.
questions.option_d: Certifying the skills acquired by trainees under the National Skill Development Programme.
questions.explanation: The Recognition of Prior Learning scheme certifies the skills that workers, such as those in construction, have already gained through experience and informal or traditional channels, bringing them into the formal skilling framework. Hence option A describes it best.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text: From the ecological point of view, which one of the following assumes importance in being a good link between the Eastern Ghats and the Western Ghats?
questions.explanation: The Sathyamangalam Tiger Reserve in Tamil Nadu occupies a strategic position that connects the Eastern Ghats with the Western Ghats, allowing wildlife movement between the two hill systems. This ecological corridor role makes option A correct.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text: One of the implications of equality in society is the absence of
questions.explanation: Equality in society essentially means the absence of special privileges enjoyed by some at the expense of others, so that all are treated alike before the law and in opportunity. It does not mean the absence of restraints, competition or ideology, so option A is correct.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
Consider the following statements in respect of Trade Related Analysis of Fauna and Flora in Commerce (TRAFFIC):
1. TRAFFIC is a bureau under United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP).
2. The mission of TRAFFIC is to ensure that trade in wild plants and animals is not a threat to the conservation of nature.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: TRAFFIC is a wildlife trade monitoring network run jointly by WWF and IUCN, not a bureau under the UN Environment Programme, so statement 1 is wrong. Its mission is to ensure that trade in wild plants and animals does not threaten nature conservation, so statement 2 is correct.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text: Which principle among the following was added to the Directive Principles of State Policy by the 42nd Amendment to the Constitution?
questions.option_a: Equal pay for equal work for both men and women
questions.option_b: Participation of workers in the management of industries
questions.option_c: Right to work, education and public assistance
questions.option_d: Securing living wage and human conditions of work to workers
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment added several new Directive Principles, including the participation of workers in the management of industries under Article 43A. Equal pay and living wages were already present, so the newly added principle is option B.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text: Which one of the following statements is correct?
questions.option_a: Rights are claims of the State against the citizens.
questions.option_b: Rights are privileges which are incorporated in the Constitution of a State.
questions.option_c: Rights are claims of the citizens against the State.
questions.option_d: Rights are privileges of a few citizens against the many.
questions.explanation: Rights are essentially the just claims that individuals hold against the State, which is obliged to protect and guarantee them. They are not claims of the State against citizens or privileges of a few, so option C correctly defines rights.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text: Which of the following gives ‘Global Gender Gap Index’ ranking to the countries of the world?
questions.explanation: The Global Gender Gap Index, which ranks countries on gender-based disparities in economic participation, education, health and political empowerment, is published annually by the World Economic Forum. Hence option A is correct.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
Which of the following statements is/are correct regarding Smart India Hackathon 2017?
1. It is a centrally sponsored scheme for developing every city of our country into Smart Cities in a decade.
2. It is an initiative to identify new digital technology innovations for solving the many problems faced by our country.
3. It is a programme aimed at making all the financial transactions in our country completely digital in a decade.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: The Smart India Hackathon is a nationwide contest inviting students to develop innovative digital and technological solutions to problems posed by government and industry, matching statement 2. It is not a scheme to build smart cities or to digitise all financial transactions, so statements 1 and 3 are wrong.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
Which of the following statements is/are correct regarding the ‘Monetary Policy Committee (MPC)?
1. It decides the RBI’s benchmark interest rates.
2. It is a 12-member body including the Governor of RBI and is reconstituted every year.
3. It functions under the chairmanship of the Union Finance Minister.
Select the correct answer using the code given below:
questions.explanation: The Monetary Policy Committee sets the RBI's benchmark policy interest rate, so statement 1 is correct. It is a six-member body, not twelve, and is chaired by the RBI Governor rather than the Finance Minister, so statements 2 and 3 are wrong.

questions.subject: Art & Culture
questions.text:
With reference to Manipuri Sankirtana, consider the following statements:
1. It is a song and dance performance.
2. Cymbals are the only musical instruments used in the performance.
3. It is performed to narrate the life and deeds of Lord Krishna.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
