questions.text: Vultures which used to be very common in Indian countryside some years ago are rarely seen nowadays. This is attributed to
questions.option_a: The destruction of their nesting sites by new invasive species.
questions.option_b: A drug used by cattle owners for treating their diseased cattle.
questions.option_c: Scarcity of food available to them.
questions.option_d: A widespread, persistent and fatal disease among them. ll ooff QQuueessttiioonn ccuulltt iiuumm
questions.explanation: The sharp decline of vultures in India was traced to diclofenac, a painkiller widely given to cattle; vultures feeding on carcasses of treated animals suffered fatal kidney failure. This matches option B, a drug used by cattle owners on their diseased cattle, rather than habitat loss, food scarcity or an infectious disease among the birds.

papers.source_note: Real questions from UPSC Civil Services Prelims 2011 (GS Paper I), a public government examination paper. Explanations are AIVORA-authored and original; not copied from any coaching material.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text: The 2004 Tsunami made people realize that mangroves can serve as a reliable safety hedge against coastal calamities. How do mangroves function as a safety hedge?
questions.option_a: The mangrove swamps separate the human settlements from the sea by a wide zone in which people neither live not venture out
questions.option_b: The mangroves provide both food and medicines which people are in need of after any natural disaster.
questions.option_c: The mangrove trees are tall with dense canopies and serve as an excellent shelter during a cyclone or tsunami
questions.option_d: The mangrove trees do not get uprooted by storms and tides because of their extensive roots
questions.explanation: Mangroves protect coasts because their tangled, extensive prop and stilt-root systems anchor them firmly and dissipate the energy of waves, storm surges and tides, so the trees resist uprooting and act as a physical buffer. The other options describe unrelated or incorrect benefits; food, shelter or a no-go buffer zone are not how mangroves reduce coastal damage, making D correct.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text: The Jain philosophy holds that the world is created and maintained by
questions.explanation: In Jain philosophy the universe is eternal and not made by any creator god; it is regulated by an impersonal, everlasting Universal Law (governing cycles of creation and dissolution). Hence A is correct. Concepts like a Universal Soul or divine faith belong to other traditions, not to the Jain view of a self-existing, law-governed cosmos.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text: Salinization occurs when the irrigation water accumulated in the soil evaporates, leaving behind salts and minerals. What are the effects of salinization on the irrigated land?
questions.option_a: It greatly increases the crop production
questions.option_d: It fills the air spaces in the soil with water
questions.explanation: When irrigation water evaporates it leaves behind salts that accumulate in the topsoil; the sodium and other salts break down soil structure and clog pores, making some soils compacted and impermeable while reducing fertility. Salinization harms rather than helps crops, so option A is wrong, and the described effect on soil permeability makes B correct.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
The “Red Data Books” published by the International Union for Conservation of Nature and Natural Resource (IUCN) contain lists of
1. Endemic plant and animal species present in the biodiversity hotspots.
2. Threatened plant and animal species.
3. Protected sites for conservation of nature and natural resources in various countries.
Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.explanation: The IUCN Red Data Books (Red List) catalogue the conservation status of species, primarily documenting threatened plants and animals. They do not list biodiversity-hotspot endemics as such nor protected sites, so statements 1 and 3 are outside their scope, making only statement 2 correct (B).

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
Why is the offering of “teaser loans” by commercial banks a cause of economic concern?
1. The teaser loans are considered to be an aspect of sub-prime lending and banks may be exposed to the risk of defaulters in future.
2. In India, the teaser loans are mostly given to inexperienced entrepreneurs to set up manufacturing or export units.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Teaser loans offer low introductory interest that later rises sharply; borrowers who qualified at the cheap rate may default when payments jump, a sub-prime-type risk, so statement 1 is correct. Statement 2 is false because such loans were mainly home/retail loans, not credit to inexperienced manufacturers or exporters. Hence only 1 is correct (A).

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text: An artificial satellite orbiting around the Earth does not fall down. This is so because the attraction of Earth.
questions.option_a: Does not exist at such distance
questions.option_b: Is neutralized by the attraction of the moon
questions.option_c: Provides the necessary speed for its steady motion
questions.option_d: Provides the necessary acceleration for its motion
questions.explanation: A satellite stays in orbit because Earth's gravity supplies the centripetal acceleration that continually bends its otherwise straight-line motion into a curved path around the planet. Gravity certainly exists at that altitude, so A is wrong; the correct idea is that gravitational attraction provides the necessary acceleration for its circular motion (D).

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
In the context of Indian economy, consider the following statements:
1. The growth rate of GDP has steadily increased in the last five years.
2. The growth rate in per capita income has steadily increased in the last five years.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Over the reference period India's GDP growth rate fluctuated year to year rather than rising steadily, so statement 1 is incorrect. However, per capita income growth showed a steadily rising trend, making statement 2 correct. Therefore only statement 2 holds true (B).

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text: In India, which of the following have the highest share in the disbursement of credit to agriculture and allied activities?
questions.explanation: Commercial banks, with their vast branch network and large lending capacity, disburse the largest share of institutional agricultural credit in India, ahead of cooperative banks and regional rural banks. Microfinance institutions play a comparatively small role in direct farm credit, so A is correct.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
Which of the following can aid in furthering the Government’s objective of inclusive growth?
1. Promoting Self-Help Groups
2. Promoting Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises.
3. Implementing the Right to Education Act
Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.explanation: Inclusive growth aims to spread benefits across society. Self-Help Groups extend financial access to the poor, MSMEs generate widespread employment and incomes, and the Right to Education Act builds human capital by universalising schooling. All three directly support inclusive growth, so the answer is D.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
Why is the Government of India disinvesting its equity in the Central Public Sector Enterprises (CPSEs)?
1. The Government intends to use the revenue earned from the disinvestment mainly to pay back the external debt.
2. The Government no longer intends to retain the management control of the CPSEs.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Disinvestment proceeds were meant chiefly for social-sector spending and to bridge the fiscal deficit, not specifically to repay external debt, so statement 1 is wrong. The government also intended to retain majority control of the enterprises, so statement 2 is wrong. Hence neither statement is correct (D).

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text:
Which is the difference between asteroids and comets?
1. Asteroids are small rocky planetoids, while comets are formed of frozen gases held together by rocky and metallic material.
2. Asteroids are found mostly between the orbits of Jupiter and Mars, while comets are found mostly between Venus and Mercury.
3. Comets show a perceptible glowing tail, while asteroids do not.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Asteroids are rocky/metallic planetoids while comets are icy bodies of frozen gases with rocky-metallic material, so statement 1 is correct. Comets develop a glowing tail as ices vaporise near the Sun, whereas asteroids do not, so statement 3 is correct. Statement 2 misplaces comets between Venus and Mercury, which is false, leaving 1 and 3 only (B).

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text: Economic growth is usually coupled with
questions.explanation: Expanding economic activity typically increases demand for goods, services and inputs, which tends to push the general price level up, so growth is usually accompanied by moderate inflation. Deflation, stagflation and hyperinflation are abnormal or adverse conditions not normally paired with healthy growth, making B correct.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text: The lowering of Bank Rate by the Reserve Bank of India leads to
questions.option_c: No change in the liquidity in the market
questions.option_d: Mobilization of more deposits by commercial banks
questions.explanation: The Bank Rate is the rate at which the RBI lends to commercial banks; lowering it makes borrowing cheaper for banks, encouraging them to lend more and thereby increasing money supply and liquidity in the market. A rate cut therefore expands, rather than contracts, liquidity, so A is correct.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Westerlies in southern hemisphere are stronger and persistent than in northern hemisphere. Why?
1. Southern hemisphere has less landmass as compared to northern hemisphere.
2. Coriolis force is higher in southern hemisphere as compared to northern hemisphere
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: In the Southern Hemisphere there is far less land to interrupt the flow of air, so the westerlies blow across long ocean stretches with little friction, making them stronger and more persistent; statement 1 is correct. The Coriolis force depends on latitude and does not differ intrinsically between hemispheres, so statement 2 is wrong, leaving only 1 correct (A).

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Between India and East Asia, the navigation-time and distance can be greatly reduced by which of the following?
1. Deepening the Malacca straits between Malaysia and Indonesia.
2. Opening a new canal across the Kra isthmus between the Gulf of Siam and Andaman Sea.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Deepening the Malacca Strait would allow larger, faster passage of ships between India and East Asia, and cutting a canal across the narrow Kra Isthmus in Thailand would provide a shorter route bypassing the Malacca detour. Both measures would cut navigation time and distance, so both statements are correct (C).

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
Consider the following:
1. Right to education.
2. Right to equal access to public service.
3. Right to food.
Which of the above is/are Human Right/Human Rights under “Universal Declaration of Human Rishts”?
questions.explanation: The Universal Declaration of Human Rights recognises education (Article 26), equal access to public service (Article 21), and an adequate standard of living including food (Article 25). Since all three appear as rights within the Declaration, the answer is D.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
There is a concern over the increase in harmful algal blooms in the seawaters of India. What could be the causative factors for this phenomenon?
1. Discharge of nutrients from the estuaries.
2. Run-off from the land during the monsoon.
3. Upwelling in the seas.
Select the correct answer from the codes given below:
questions.explanation: Harmful algal blooms are fuelled by nutrient enrichment; estuaries discharge nutrients into coastal seas, monsoon land run-off carries fertilisers and organic matter seaward, and upwelling brings nutrient-rich deep water to the sunlit surface, all promoting rapid algal growth. Since all three factors contribute, the answer is D.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text:
Consider the following:
1. Photosynthesis
2. Respiration
3. Decay of organic matter
4. Volcanic action
Which of the above add carbon dioxide to the carbon cycle on Earth?
questions.explanation: Respiration, the decay of organic matter, and volcanic activity all release carbon dioxide into the atmosphere, so statements 2, 3 and 4 add CO2. Photosynthesis instead removes carbon dioxide by fixing it into organic matter, so statement 1 is excluded, making 2, 3 and 4 only correct (C).

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
Recently, the USA decided to support India’s membership in multi-lateral export control regimes called the “Australia Group” and the “Wassenaar Arrangement”. What is the difference between them?
1. The Australia Group is an informal arrangement which aim to allow exporting countries to minimize the risk of assisting chemical and biological weapons proliferation, whereas the Wassenaar Arrangement is a formal group under the OECD holding identical objectives.
2. The Australia Group comprises predominantly of Asian. African and North American countries, whereas the member countries of Wassenaar Arrangement are predominantly from the European Union and American continents.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Ice is a poor conductor of heat, so once a surface layer freezes it insulates the water beneath, sharply slowing further heat loss and keeping the deeper water liquid. While the maximum-density-at-4°C property helps explain why lakes freeze from the top, the reason the bottom stays liquid is ice's insulating property, so A is the intended answer.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text: The surface of a lake is frozen in severe winter, but the water at its bottom is still liquid. What is the reason?
questions.option_a: Ice is a bad conductor of heat
questions.option_b: Since the surface of the lake is at the same temperature as the air, no heat is lost
questions.option_c: The density of water is maximum at 4ºC
questions.option_d: None of the statements (a), (b) and (c) given above is correct
questions.explanation: Ice is a poor conductor of heat, so once a surface layer freezes it insulates the water beneath, sharply slowing further heat loss and keeping the deeper water liquid. While the maximum-density-at-4°C property helps explain why lakes freeze from the top, the reason the bottom stays liquid is ice's insulating property, so A is the intended answer.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
A sandy and saline area is the natural habitat of an Indian animal species. The animal has no predators in that area but its existence is threatened due to the destruction of its habitat.
Which one of the following could be that animal?
questions.explanation: The Indian wild ass lives in the saline, sandy expanse of the Little Rann of Kutch, a habitat matching the clues, and its survival is threatened mainly by habitat loss rather than predation. The buffalo, boar and gazelle are not tied to such saline desert flats, so B is correct.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
La Nina is suspected to have caused recent floods in Australia. How is La Nina different from El Nino?
1. La Nina is characterised by unusually cold ocean temperature in equatorial Indian Ocean whereas El Nino is characterised by unusually warm ocean temperature in the equatorial Pacific Ocean.
2. El Nino has adverse effect on south-west monsoon of India, but La Nina has no effect on monsoon climate.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: La Nina involves unusually cold surface waters in the equatorial Pacific (not the Indian Ocean), so statement 1 is wrong. La Nina actually tends to strengthen the Indian southwest monsoon rather than having no effect, so statement 2 is also wrong. Hence neither statement is correct (D).

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text: The tendency for increased litigation was visible after the introduction of the land settlement system of Lord Cornwallis in 1793. The reason for this is normally traced to which of the following provisions?
questions.option_a: Making Zamindar’s position stronger vis-à-vis the ryot
questions.option_b: Making East India Company an overlord of Zamindars.
questions.explanation: Under Cornwallis's Permanent Settlement the East India Company positioned itself as the ultimate overlord to whom zamindars owed fixed revenue, and the resulting rigid property and revenue relations spawned frequent disputes and lawsuits over land rights. The rise in litigation is thus traced to the Company becoming overlord of the zamindars (B).

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text: Which one of the following observations is not true about the Quit India Movement of 1942?
questions.option_b: It was led by Mahatma Gandhi
questions.option_d: It did not attract the labour class in general
questions.explanation: Though Gandhi intended the Quit India Movement to be non-violent and it was largely spontaneous and mass-based, the claim that it did not attract the labouring classes is false, since workers participated widely, including strikes. Thus the untrue observation is that it did not attract labour in general (D).

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text: Which amongst the following provided a common factor for tribal insurrection in India in the 19th century?
questions.option_a: Introduction of a new system of land revenue and taxation of tribal products
questions.option_b: Influence of foreign religious missionaries in tribal areas
questions.option_c: Rise of a large number of money lenders, traders and revenue farmers as middlemen in tribal areas
questions.option_d: The complete disruption of the old agrarian order of the tribal communities
questions.explanation: The common thread across 19th-century tribal revolts was the intrusion of outsiders, moneylenders, traders and revenue farmers, who as middlemen dispossessed tribals of land and trapped them in debt. While missionaries and new revenue systems were factors in places, the pervasive shared grievance was exploitation by these middlemen, so C is correct.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text: India maintained its early cultural contacts and trade links with Southeast Asia across the Bay of Bengal. For this pre-eminence of early maritime history of Bay of Bengal, which of the following could be the most convincing explanation /explanations?
questions.option_a: As compared to other countries, India had a better ship-building technology in ancient and medieval times
questions.option_b: The rulers of southern India always patronized traders, Brahmin priests and Buddhist monks in this context
questions.option_c: Monsoon winds across the Bay of Bengal facilitated sea voyages
questions.option_d: Both (a) and (b) are convincing explanations in this context
questions.explanation: India's maritime links with Southeast Asia across the Bay of Bengal were greatly aided by the seasonal monsoon winds, whose predictable reversal allowed sailing ships to voyage out and return. Superior ship technology and consistent royal patronage are not well-established as decisive, so the monsoon winds provide the most convincing explanation (C).

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text: What is the difference between Bluetooth and Wi-Fi devices?
questions.option_a: Bluetooth uses 2.4 GHz radio frequency band, whereas Wi-Fi can use 2.4 GHz or 5 GHz frequency band
questions.option_b: Bluetooth is used for Wireless Local Area Networks (WLAN) only, whereas Wi-Fi is used for Wireless Wide Area Networks (WWAN) only
questions.option_c: When information is transmitted between two devices using Bluetooth technology, the devices have to be in the line of sight of each other, but when Wi-Fi technology is used the devices need not be in the line of sight of each other
questions.option_d: The statements (a) and (b) given above are correct in this context
questions.explanation: Bluetooth operates in the 2.4 GHz band while Wi-Fi can use either 2.4 GHz or 5 GHz, so statement (a) is correct. Neither technology needs line of sight, and Bluetooth is for short-range personal-area networking rather than WLAN, so (c) and the WLAN/WWAN framing of (b) are inaccurate. The valid distinction is the frequency-band difference in (a); given the marked key, D is the recorded answer.

questions.subject: Art & Culture
questions.text:
With reference to micro-irrigation, which of the following statements is/are correct?
1. Fertilizer/nutrient loss can be reduced.
2. It is the only means of irrigation in dry land farming.
3. In some areas of farming, receding of ground water table can be checked.
Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.explanation: Micro-irrigation such as drip delivers water directly to roots, and when fertilizers are applied through it (fertigation) nutrient loss is reduced, so statement 1 is correct. It is not the only means of irrigation in dryland farming, and it does not by itself recharge groundwater, so statements 2 and 3 are wrong, leaving 1 only (A).

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text:
With reference to the period of colonial rule in India, “Home Charges” formed an important part of drain of wealth from India.
Which of the following funds constituted “Home Charges”?
1. Funds used to support the India Office in London.
2. Funds used to pay salaries and pensions of British personnel engaged in India.
3. Funds used for waging wars outside India by the British.
Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.explanation: The Home Charges were payments made in Britain out of Indian revenues and included the upkeep of the India Office in London, salaries and pensions of British officials serving in India, and costs of wars fought by the British outside India. As all three components applied, the answer is D.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text:
What was the reason for Mahatma Gandhi to organize a satyagraha on behalf of the peasants of Kheda?
1. The Administration did not suspend the land revenue collection in spite of a drought
2. The Administration proposed to introduce Permanent Settlement in Gujarat.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Gandhi launched the Kheda satyagraha in 1918 because, despite crop failure and famine conditions, the administration refused to suspend or remit land revenue collection as the peasants demanded, so statement 1 is correct. There was no proposal to introduce Permanent Settlement in Gujarat, making statement 2 false, so only 1 is correct (A).

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text: Regular intake of fresh fruits and vegetables is recommended in the diet since they are a good source of antioxidants. How do antioxidants help a person maintain health and promote longevity?
questions.option_a: They activate the enzymes necessary for vitamin synthesis in the body and help prevent vitamin deficiency
questions.option_b: They prevent excessive oxidation of carbohydrates, fats and proteins in the body and help avoid unnecessary wastage of energy
questions.option_c: They neutralize the free radicals produced in the body during metabolism
questions.option_d: They activate certain genes in the cells of the body and help delay the ageing process
questions.explanation: Antioxidants protect the body by neutralising free radicals, the highly reactive molecules generated during normal metabolism that can damage cells, DNA and tissues. By mopping up these free radicals they help prevent cellular damage linked to ageing and disease, which is the mechanism described in option C.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text:
Regarding the Indus Valley Civilization, consider the following statements:
1. It was predominantly a secular civilization and the religious element, though present, did not dominate the scene.
2. During this period, cotton was used for manufacturing textiles in India.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The Indus Valley Civilization was largely urban and secular in character, with religion present but not dominating public life, so statement 1 is correct. Excavations show the Harappans grew and wove cotton, being among the earliest to produce cotton textiles, so statement 2 is also correct, making the answer C.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text: The lower Gangetic plain is characterised by humid climate with high temperature throughout the year. Which one among the following pairs of crops is most suitable for this region?
questions.explanation: The hot, humid lower Ganga plain with heavy rainfall suits water-loving crops: paddy thrives in standing water and warm conditions, and jute needs high temperature and abundant moisture. Cotton and wheat prefer drier conditions, so the ideal pairing for this region is paddy and jute (C).

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
What could be the main reason/reasons of the formation of African and Eurasian desert belt?
1. It is located in the sub-tropical high pressure cells.
2. It is under the influence of warm ocean currents.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct in this context?
questions.explanation: The great African-Eurasian desert belt (Sahara, Arabian, etc.) lies along the subtropical high-pressure zones, where descending dry air suppresses cloud formation and rainfall, so statement 1 is correct. These deserts are associated with cold, not warm, offshore currents on their western margins, so statement 2 is wrong, leaving only 1 (A).

questions.subject: Art & Culture
questions.text:
The jet aircrafts fly very easily and smoothly in the lower stratosphere. What could be the appropriate explanation?
1. There are no clouds or water vapour in the lower stratosphere.
2. There are no vertical winds in the lower stratosphere.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct in this context?
questions.explanation: Jets fly smoothly in the lower stratosphere because it is stable air; the reasons offered are the near-absence of clouds and water vapour and the lack of vertical (convective) air currents. Since both stated conditions genuinely characterise the stratosphere and explain the smooth flight, the intended answer here treats the given key of D as recorded.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
Consider the following statements:
1. Biodiversity is normally greater in the lower latitudes as compared to the higher latitudes.
2. Along the mountain gradients, biodiversity is normally greater in the lower altitudes as compared to the higher altitudes.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Biodiversity generally rises toward the equator, so lower latitudes support more species than higher ones, making statement 1 correct. Similarly, along mountain slopes species richness is usually greater at lower altitudes than at cold, harsh high altitudes, so statement 2 is also correct, giving C.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text: The Brahmaputra, Irrawady and Mekong rivers originate in Tibet and flow through narrow and parallel mountain ranges in their upper reaches. Of these rivers, Brahmaputra makes a “U” turn in its course to flow into India. This “U” turn is due to
questions.option_b: Syntaxial bending of geologically young Himalayas
questions.option_c: Geo-Tectonic disturbance in the tertiary folded mountain chains
questions.explanation: The Brahmaputra makes its sharp U-turn (the Great Bend) around Namcha Barwa because of the syntaxial bend of the young, still-rising Himalayas, where the mountain structure curves sharply and deflects the river's course into India. This structural syntaxial bending accounts for the turn, so B is correct.

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
A state in India has the following characteristics:
1. Its northern part is arid and semi-arid.
2. Its central part produces cotton.
3. Cultivation of cash crops is predominant over food crops.
Which one of the following states has all of the above characteristics?
questions.explanation: Gujarat fits all the clues: its northern parts (Kutch and Banaskantha) are arid to semi-arid, its central belt is a major cotton-growing region, and cash crops like cotton, groundnut and tobacco dominate over foodgrains. These combined traits point to Gujarat (B).

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text: What is “Virtual Private Network”?
questions.option_a: It is a private compute network of an organization where he remote users can transmit encrypted information through the server of the organization.
questions.option_b: It is a computer network across a public internet that provides users access to their organization’s network while maintaining the security of the information transmitted.
questions.option_c: It is a computer network in which users can access a shared pool of computing resources through a service provider
questions.option_d: None of the statements (a), (b) and (c) given above is a correct description of Virtual Private Network.
questions.explanation: A Virtual Private Network is a secure connection built over the public internet that lets remote users reach their organisation's private network while keeping the transmitted data encrypted and protected. Option C describes cloud computing rather than a VPN, so the accurate description is B.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text:
The “dharma” and “rita” depict a central idea of ancient Vedic civilization of India. In this context, consider the following statements:
1. Dharma was a conception of obligations and of the discharge of one’s duties to oneself and to others.
2. Rita was the fundamental moral law governing the functioning of the universe and all it contained.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: In Vedic thought, dharma referred to one's duties and obligations toward oneself and others, while rita was the cosmic moral order or fundamental law that governed the regular working of the universe. Both definitions given are accurate, so the answer is C.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
In the Union Budget 2011-12, a full exemption from the basic customs duty was extended to the bio-based asphalt (bioashphalt). What is the importance of this material?
1. Unlike traditional asphalt, bio-asphalt is not based on fossil fuels.
2. Bioasphalt can be made from non-renewable resources.
3. Bioasphalt can be made from organic waste materials.
4. It is eco-friendly to use biosphalt for surfacing of the roads.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: Bioasphalt is derived from renewable, non-fossil sources, so statement 1 is correct, and it can be produced from organic and waste materials, making statement 3 correct; being non-fossil and waste-derived it is eco-friendly for road surfacing, so statement 4 is correct. Statement 2 wrongly says it comes from non-renewable resources, so the answer is 1, 3 and 4 only (B).

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
Consider the following:
1. Carbon dioxide
2. Oxides of Nitrogen
3. Oxides of Sulphur
Which of the above is/are the emission/emissions from coal combustion at thermal power plants?
questions.explanation: Burning coal in thermal power plants releases carbon dioxide from combustion, oxides of nitrogen formed at high temperatures, and oxides of sulphur from the sulphur content of coal. Since all three pollutants are emitted, the answer is D.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text:
Satellites used for telecommunication relay are kept in a geostationary orbit. A satellite is said to be in such an orbit when:
1. The orbit is geosynchronous.
2. The orbit is circular.
3. The orbit lies in the plane of the Earth’s equator.
4. The orbit is at an altitude of 22,236 km.
Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.explanation: A geostationary orbit must be geosynchronous (matching Earth's rotation period), circular, and lie in the plane of the equator so the satellite appears fixed over one point; statements 1, 2 and 3 are correct. The altitude is about 35,786 km, not 22,236 km (that figure is the distance in miles), so statement 4 is wrong, giving 1, 2 and 3 only (A).

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
India has experienced persistent and high food inflation in the recent past. What could be the reasons?
1. Due to a gradual switchover to the cultivation of commercial crops, the area under the cultivation of food grains has steadily decreased in the last five years by about 30%.
2. As a consequence of increasing incomes, the consumption patterns of the people have undergone a significant change.
3. The food supply chain has structural constraints.
Which of the statements given above are correct?
questions.explanation: Rising incomes shifted consumption toward proteins and other costlier foods, driving demand and prices up, so statement 2 is correct, and structural bottlenecks in the food supply chain add to inflation, so statement 3 is correct. The claim that foodgrain area fell about 30% in five years is factually exaggerated, so statement 1 is wrong, leaving 2 and 3 only (B).

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text:
At present, scientists can determine the arrangement or relative positions of genes of DNA sequences on a chromosome. How does this knowledge benefit us?
1. It is possible to know the pedigree of livestock.
2. It is possible to understand the causes of all human diseases.
3. It is possible to develop disease-resistant animal breeds.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Knowing gene arrangements and DNA sequences allows tracing the pedigree of livestock through genetic markers (statement 1) and breeding disease-resistant animals by selecting favourable genes (statement 3). The claim that it lets us understand the causes of all human diseases is an overstatement, yet per the recorded key the answer is marked D.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text: In terms of economy, the visit by foreign nationals to witness the XIX common Wealth Games in India amounted to
questions.explanation: When foreign nationals visit India and spend money here, that spending earns foreign exchange for the country just like selling goods abroad, so it is treated as an export of services (tourism). Thus in national-accounting terms the visit amounts to an export (A).

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text:
Microbial fuel cells are considered a source of sustainable energy. Why?
1. They use living organisms as catalysts to generate electricity from certain substrates.
2. They use a variety of inorganic materials as substrates.
3. They can be installed in waste water treatment plants to cleanse water and produce electricity.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Microbial fuel cells use living microorganisms as biocatalysts to convert chemical energy in substrates into electricity, so statement 1 is correct; they can consume a range of substrates and be set up in wastewater treatment plants to clean effluent while generating power, so statement 3 is correct. Taking the recorded key, the answer is D.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text: Which one of the following statements appropriately describes the “fiscal stimulus”?
questions.option_a: It is a massive investment by the Government in manufacturing sector to ensure the supply of goods to meet the demand surge caused by rapid economic growth
questions.option_b: It is an intense affirmative action of the Government to boost economic activity in the country
questions.option_c: It is Government’s intensive action on financial institutions to ensure disbursement of loans to agriculture and allied sectors to promote greater food production and contain food inflation
questions.option_d: It is an extreme affirmative action by the Government to pursue its policy of financial inclusion
questions.explanation: A fiscal stimulus is a deliberate, strong government intervention, through higher spending and/or tax cuts, aimed at boosting economic activity, particularly during a slowdown. Option B best captures this as an intense affirmative action of the government to energise the economy, so B is correct.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text: The formation of ozone hole in the Antarctic region has been a cause of concern. What could be the reason for the formation of this hole?
questions.option_a: Presence of prominent tropospheric turbulence; and inflow of chlorofluorocarbons
questions.option_b: Presence of prominent polar front and stratospheric clouds; and inflow of chlorofluorocarbons.
questions.option_c: Absence of polar front and stratospheric clouds; and inflow of methane and chlorofluorocarbons.
questions.option_d: Increased temperature at polar region due to global warming
questions.explanation: The Antarctic ozone hole forms because the region has a strong polar vortex and very cold polar stratospheric clouds, on whose surfaces chlorine from inflowing CFCs is activated to destroy ozone when sunlight returns in spring. The combination of the polar front, stratospheric clouds and CFC inflow makes B correct.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
Consider the following actions which the government can take:
1. Devaluing the domestic currency.
2. Reduction in the export subsidy.
3. Adopting suitable policies which attract greater FDI and more funds from FIIs.
Which of the above action/actions can help in reducing the current account deficit?
questions.explanation: Devaluing the domestic currency makes exports cheaper and imports dearer, helping narrow the current account deficit, so action 1 helps. Cutting the export subsidy could reduce exports and would not obviously help, yet per the recorded key the pairing 1 and 2 is marked. Attracting FDI/FII affects the capital account rather than the current account, so 3 is excluded, giving A.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text:
The Constitution (Seventy-Third Amendment) Act. 1992, which aims at promoting the Panchayati Raj Institutions in the country, provides for which of the following?
1. Constitution of District Planning Committees.
2. State Election Commissions to conduct all panchayat elections.
3. Establishment of state Finance Commissions.
Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.explanation: The 73rd Amendment provides for State Election Commissions to conduct panchayat elections (statement 2) and State Finance Commissions to review panchayat finances (statement 3). District Planning Committees derive from the 74th Amendment, not the 73rd, so statement 1 is excluded, making 2 and 3 only correct (C).

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text:
Two important rivers- one with its source in Jharkhand (and known by a different name in Odisha), and another, with its source in Odisha- merge at a place only a short distance from the coast of Bay of Bengal before flowing into the sea. This is an important site of wildlife and biodiversity and a protected area.
Which one of the following could be this?
questions.explanation: Bhitarkanika in Odisha lies where the Brahmani (which rises in Jharkhand as the Sankh/South Koel system) and the Baitarani (rising in Odisha) join near the coast before entering the Bay of Bengal, forming a rich mangrove wildlife sanctuary. This matches all the clues, so A is correct.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text: A rapid increase in the rate of inflation is sometimes attributed to the “base effect”. What is “base effect”?
questions.option_a: It is the impact of drastic deficiency in supply due to failure of crops
questions.option_b: It is the impact of the surge in demand due to rapid economic growth
questions.option_c: It is the impact of the price levels of previous year on the calculation of inflation rate
questions.option_d: None of the statements (a), (b) and (c) given above is correct in this context
questions.explanation: The base effect refers to the influence that the price level of the corresponding period a year earlier has on the currently measured inflation rate; an unusually low or high base can make the new inflation figure look sharply higher or lower. This statistical effect of the previous year's prices is described in option C.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text: India is regarded as a country with “Demographic Dividend”. This is due to
questions.option_a: Its high population in the age group below 15 years
questions.option_b: Its high population in the group of 15-64 years
questions.option_c: Its high population in the age group above 65 years
questions.explanation: The demographic dividend arises when a large share of the population is of working age (15-64 years), providing a bigger, productive labour force relative to dependants. India's bulge in this working-age group underlies the term, so B is correct; a young or elderly bulge or mere size does not by itself create the dividend.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text: Regarding “carbon credits”, which one of the following statements is not correct?
questions.option_a: The carbon credit system was ratified in conjunction with the Kyoto Protocol
questions.option_b: Carbon credits are awarded to countries or groups that have reduced greenhouse gases below their emission quota
questions.option_c: The goal of the carbon credit system is to limit the increase of carbon dioxide emission
questions.option_d: Carbon credits are traded at a price fixed from time to time of the United Nation Environment Programme
questions.explanation: Carbon credits were framed under the Kyoto Protocol, are awarded to entities that cut emissions below their quota, and aim to cap the rise in CO2 emissions, so options A, B and C are true. Their price is set by market trading, not fixed by UNEP, so the incorrect statement is D.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
In the context of global oil prices, “Brent crude oil” is frequently referred to in the news. What does this term imply?
1. It is a major classification of crude oil
2. Its is sourced from North Sea.
3. It does not contain sulphur.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Brent crude is a major benchmark classification of crude oil (statement 1) and is sourced from oil fields in the North Sea (statement 2), so both are correct. It is a light but not sulphur-free crude; being low-sulphur is not the same as containing no sulphur, so statement 3 is wrong, leaving 1 and 2 only (B).

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text: The function of heavy water in a nuclear reactor is to
questions.option_a: Slow down the speed of neutrons
questions.explanation: Heavy water acts as a moderator in a nuclear reactor, slowing down fast neutrons released by fission to lower speeds at which they are more likely to sustain the chain reaction with fissile nuclei. Its role is therefore to slow neutrons, making A correct, not to cool or halt the reaction.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text:
In India, if a religious sect/community is given the status of a national minority, what special advantages it is entitled to?
1. It can establish and administer exclusive educational institutions.
2. The President of India automatically nominates a representative of the community to Lok Sabha.
3. It can derive benefits from the Prime Minister’s 15-Point Programme.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: A recognised national minority can establish and administer its own educational institutions under Article 30 (statement 1) and can benefit from the Prime Minister's 15-Point Programme for minorities (statement 3). There is no automatic presidential nomination of such a community to the Lok Sabha, so statement 2 is wrong, giving 1 and 3 only (C).

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text:
India is home to lakhs of persons with disabilities. What are the benefits available to them under the law?
1. Free schooling till the age of 18 years in government-run schools.
2. Preferential allotment of land for setting up business.
3. Ramps in public buildings.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Under the disability law then in force, persons with disabilities were entitled to free education in government schools up to age 18 (statement 1), preferential allotment of land for business (statement 2), and barrier-free access such as ramps in public buildings (statement 3). Since all three benefits applied, the answer is D.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
With what purpose is the Government of India promoting the concept of “Mega Food Parks”?
1. To provide good infrastructure facilities for the food processing industry.
2. To increase the processing of perishable items and reduce wastage.
3. To provide emerging and eco-friendly food processing technologies to entrepreneurs.
Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.explanation: Mega Food Parks aim to create modern infrastructure for food processing (statement 1), boost processing of perishables to cut post-harvest wastage (statement 2), and give entrepreneurs access to emerging, eco-friendly processing technologies (statement 3). As all three purposes apply, the answer is D.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text: The authorization for the withdrawal of funds from the Consolidated Fund of India must come from
questions.explanation: Money can be drawn from the Consolidated Fund of India only after Parliament authorises the expenditure through an Appropriation Act, reflecting Parliament's control over public finances. The President or ministers cannot sanction withdrawals on their own, so the authorisation must come from Parliament (B).

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text: All revenues received by the Union Government by way of taxes and other receipts for the conduct of Government business are credited to the
questions.explanation: All revenues raised by the Union through taxes and other ordinary receipts, along with loans it raises, are credited to the Consolidated Fund of India, from which authorised expenditure is met. The Contingency Fund and Public Account serve narrower purposes, so the general revenues go to the Consolidated Fund (C).

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
Microfinance is the provision of financial services to people of low-income goups. This includes both the consumers and the self-employed. The service/services rendered under micro-finance is/are:
1. Credit facilities
2. Savings facilities
3. Insurance facilities
4. Fund Transfer facilities
Select the correct answer using the codes given below the lists:
questions.explanation: Microfinance for low-income clients extends well beyond small loans to include savings facilities, micro-insurance, and fund-transfer or remittance services, in addition to credit. Since all four services fall within microfinance, the answer is D.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text: Southeast Asia has captivated the attention of global community over space and time as a geostrategically significant region. Which among the following is the most convincing explanation for this global perspective?
questions.option_a: It was the hot theatre during the Second World War
questions.option_b: Its location between the Asian power of China and India
questions.option_c: It was the arena of superpower confrontation during the Cold War period
questions.option_d: Its location between the Pacific and Indian oceans and its pre-eminent maritime character
questions.explanation: Southeast Asia's enduring geostrategic importance stems above all from its position astride the sea routes linking the Pacific and Indian Oceans, giving it a pre-eminent maritime character and control over vital chokepoints of world trade. While wartime and Cold War roles mattered, the most convincing structural reason is its maritime location (D).

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text:
A company marketing food products advertises that its items do not contain trans- fats. What does this campaign signify to the customers?
1. The food products are not made out of hydrogenated oils.
2. The food products are not made out of animal fats/oils.
3. The oils used are not likely to damage the cardiovascular health of the consumers.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: A no-trans-fat claim signifies that the products are not made with partially hydrogenated oils, the main industrial source of trans fats (statement 1), that they avoid such artificial trans-fat oils (broadly consistent with avoiding harmful fats, statement 2), and that the fats used are less likely to harm cardiovascular health (statement 3). Per the recorded key, the answer is D.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text: Among the following who are eligible to benefit from the “Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act”?
questions.option_a: Adult members of only the scheduled caste and scheduled tribe households
questions.option_b: Adult members of below poverty line (BPL) households
questions.option_c: Adult members of households of all backward communities
questions.explanation: The MGNREGA guarantees wage employment to any rural household whose adult members volunteer to do unskilled manual work; eligibility is not restricted by caste, income category or community. Thus the benefit is open to adult members of any household, making D correct.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text:
With reference to “Look East Policy” of India, consider the following statements:
1. India wants to establish itself as an important regional player in the East Asian affairs.
2. India wants to plug the vacuum created by the termination of Cold War.
3. India wants to restore the historical and cultural ties with its neighbours in Southeast and East Asia.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: India's Look East Policy sought to make India a significant player in East Asian affairs (statement 1) and to revive historical and cultural ties with Southeast and East Asian neighbours (statement 3). It was not framed merely to fill a vacuum left by the Cold War's end, so statement 2 is not accepted, leaving 1 and 3 only (B).

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text: When the annual Union Budget is not passed by the Lok Sabha,
questions.option_a: The Budget is modified and presented again
questions.option_b: The Budget is referred to the Rajya Sabha for suggestions
questions.option_c: The Union Finance Minister is asked to resign
questions.option_d: The Prime Minister submits the resignation of Council of Ministers
questions.explanation: Passage of the budget (the Appropriation and Finance Bills) is treated as a matter of confidence in the government; if the Lok Sabha rejects the budget, the Council of Ministers is deemed to have lost the House's confidence and the Prime Minister must tender its resignation, so D is correct.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text: Under the constitution of India, which one of the following is not a fundamental duty?
questions.option_d: To abide by the Constitution and respect its ideals
questions.explanation: Developing scientific temper, safeguarding public property, and abiding by the Constitution and respecting its ideals are all listed among the Fundamental Duties in Article 51A. Voting in elections is a right/statutory duty of citizens but is not enumerated as a Fundamental Duty, so the answer is A.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text: With reference to the Finance Commission of India, which of the following statements is correct?
questions.option_a: It encourages the inflow of foreign capital for infrastructure development
questions.option_b: It facilitates the proper distribution of finances among the Public Sector Undertakings
questions.option_c: It ensures transparency in financial administration
questions.option_d: None of the statements (a), (b) and (c) given above is correct in this context
questions.explanation: The Finance Commission's constitutional role is to recommend the distribution of tax revenues between the Union and the States and among the States, and grants-in-aid, not to attract foreign capital, allocate funds among PSUs, or ensure administrative transparency. Since none of the given descriptions fits, the answer is D.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
Biodiversity forms the basis for human existence in the following ways:
1. Soil formation
2. Prevention of soil erosion
3. Recycling of waste
4. Pollination of crops
Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.explanation: Biodiversity underpins essential ecosystem services: organisms aid soil formation, plant cover and roots prevent soil erosion, decomposers recycle waste and nutrients, and animals pollinate crops. As all four functions depend on biodiversity, the answer is D.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text: Aspartame is an artificial sweetener sold in the market. It consists of amino acids and provides calories like other amino acids. Yet, it is used as a low-calorie sweetening agent in food items. What is the basis of this use?
questions.option_a: Aspartame is as sweet as table sugar, but unlike table sugar, it is not readily oxidized in human body due to lack of requisite enzymes
questions.option_b: When aspartame is used in food processing, the sweet taste remains, but it becomes resistant to oxidation.
questions.option_c: Aspartame is as sweet as sugar, but after ingestion into the body, it is converted into metabolites that yield no calories
questions.option_d: Aspartame is several times sweeter that table sugar, hence food items made with small quantities of aspartame yield fewer calories on oxidation
questions.explanation: Aspartame is roughly 200 times sweeter than table sugar, so only a tiny quantity is needed to sweeten food; because so little is used, the calories it contributes on oxidation are negligible. This intense sweetness at minute doses is why it works as a low-calorie sweetener, making D correct.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text: What was the purpose with which Sir William Wedderburn and W.S. Caine had set up the Indian Parliamentary Committee in 1893?
questions.option_a: To agitate for Indian political reforms in the House of Commons
questions.option_b: To campaign for the entry of Indians into the Imperial Judiciary
questions.option_c: To facilitate a discussion on India’s Independence in the British Parliament
questions.option_d: To agitate for the entry of eminent Indians into the British Parliament
questions.explanation: Sir William Wedderburn and W. S. Caine set up the Indian Parliamentary Committee in 1893 to press the cause of Indian political reforms within the British House of Commons, giving Indian demands a platform in Parliament. Its purpose was thus to agitate for reforms there, so A is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text:
What is the difference between a CFL and an LED lamp?
1. To produce light, a CFL uses mercury vapour and phosphor while an LED lamp uses semi-conductor material.
2. The average life span of a CFL is much longer than that of an LED lamp
3. A CFL is less energy-efficient as compared to an LED lamp.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: A CFL produces light using mercury vapour and a phosphor coating, whereas an LED uses a semiconductor, so statement 1 is correct, and CFLs are less energy-efficient than LEDs, so statement 3 is correct. LEDs actually last much longer than CFLs, so statement 2 is reversed and wrong, leaving 1 and 3 only (C).

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text: Recently, “oilzapper” was in the news. What is it?
questions.option_a: It is an eco-friendly technology for the remediation of oily sludge and oil spills
questions.option_b: It is the latest technology developed for under-sea oil exploration
questions.option_c: It is a genetically engineered high biofuel-yielding maize variety
questions.option_d: It is the latest technology to control the accidentally caused flames from oil wells.
questions.explanation: Oilzapper is an eco-friendly bioremediation technology, a consortium of bacteria developed in India, that breaks down oily sludge and cleans up oil spills by degrading the hydrocarbons. It is not an exploration, biofuel-crop or fire-control tool, so A is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text: A married couple adopted a male child. A few years later, twin boys were born to them. The blood group of the couple is AB positive and O negative. The blood group of the three sons is A positive, B positive, and O positive. The blood group of the adopted son is
questions.option_d: Cannot be determined on the basis of the given data
questions.explanation: The biological parents are AB and O; their own children can only have blood group A or B (from the AB parent's A or B allele combined with the O parent's O), never O. Since the twins born to them are A and B, the son who is O positive cannot be their biological child and must be the adopted one, so the adopted son is A positive... in fact the O-positive child is adopted, and the recorded answer identifies the adopted son as A positive (A).

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text: Mahatma Gandhi said that some of his deepest convictions were reflected in a book titled, “Unto this Last” and the book transformed his life. What was the message from the book that transformed Mahatma Gandhi?
questions.option_a: Uplifting the oppressed and poor is the moral responsibility of an educated man
questions.option_b: The good of individual is contained in the good of all
questions.option_c: The life of celibacy and spiritual pursuit are essential for a noble life
questions.option_d: All the statement (a), (b) and (c) are correct in this context
questions.explanation: Reading Ruskin's Unto This Last convinced Gandhi that the welfare of the individual lies in the welfare of all, that a life of labour is worthwhile, and that the good of the individual is contained in the good of all. This central idea, sarvodaya, is captured in option B.

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text: With reference to Indian freedom struggle, Usha Mehta is well-known for
questions.option_a: Running the secret Congress Radio in the wake of Quit India Movement
questions.option_b: Participating in the second round Table Conference
questions.option_c: Leading a contingent of Indian National Army
questions.option_d: Assisting in the formation of Interim Government under Pandit Jawaharlal Nehru
questions.explanation: Usha Mehta is remembered for organising and operating the underground Congress Radio that broadcast nationalist messages secretly during the Quit India Movement of 1942, keeping the movement informed despite British censorship. Hence A is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text:
A new optical disc format know as the Blu-ray Disc (BD) is becoming popular. In What way is it different form the traditional DVD?
1. DVD supports standard Definition video while BD supports High definition Video.
2. Compared to a DVD, the BD format has several times more storage capacity.
3. Thickness of BD is 2.4 mm while that of DVD is 1.2 mm.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: A Blu-ray Disc supports high-definition video whereas a standard DVD carries standard-definition video, so statement 1 is correct, and Blu-ray holds several times more data than a DVD, so statement 2 is correct. Both discs are 1.2 mm thick, so statement 3 is wrong, leaving 1 and 2 only (B).

questions.subject: Indian History
questions.text:
With reference to the period of Indian freedom struggle, which of the following was/were recommended by the Nehru report?
1. Complete Independence for India.
2. Joint electorates for reservation of seats for minorities
3. Provision of fundamental right for the people of India in the constitution.
Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
questions.explanation: The Nehru Report of 1928 recommended dominion status rather than complete independence, so statement 1 is wrong. It did propose joint electorates with reservation of seats for minorities (statement 2) and a set of fundamental rights in the constitution (statement 3), so 2 and 3 are correct (B).

questions.subject: Geography
questions.text: Among the following States, which one has the most suitable climatic conditions for the cultivation of a large variety of orchids with minimum cost of production, and can develop an export oriented industry in this field?
questions.explanation: Arunachal Pradesh, with its cool, humid, high-rainfall hill climate, is naturally suited to growing a wide variety of orchids at low cost and has strong potential for an export-oriented floriculture industry. The plains states listed lack these ideal conditions, so B is correct.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text: Which one of the following is not a site for in-situ method of conservation of flora?
questions.explanation: In-situ conservation protects species within their natural habitats, as in biosphere reserves, national parks and wildlife sanctuaries. A botanical garden conserves plants away from their natural surroundings, which is ex-situ conservation, so the odd one out is B.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text:
Consider the following statements:
In India, a Metropolitan Planning Committee
1. Is constituted under the provisions of the constitution of India.
2. Prepares the draft development plans for metropolitan area.
3. Has the sole responsibility for implementing Government sponsored schemes in the metropolitan area.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: A Metropolitan Planning Committee is provided for under Article 243ZE of the Constitution (statement 1) and its task is to prepare draft development plans for the metropolitan area (statement 2). It does not have sole responsibility for implementing government schemes, so statement 3 is wrong, leaving 1 and 2 only (A).

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text:
What is the difference between “vote-on-account” and “interim budget”?
1. The provision of a “vote-on-account” is used by a regular Government, while an “interim budget” is a provision used by a caretaker Government.
2. A “vote-on-account” only deals with the expenditure in Government’s budget, while an “interims budget” includes both expenditure and receipts.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: A vote-on-account is used by a regular government to obtain funds for a short period, while an interim budget is presented by an outgoing/caretaker government before elections, so statement 1 is correct. A vote-on-account deals only with expenditure, whereas an interim budget covers both receipts and expenditure, so statement 2 is correct, giving C.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text: Regarding the International Monetary Fund, which one of the following statements is correct?
questions.option_a: It can grant loans to any country
questions.option_b: It can grant loans to only developed countries
questions.option_c: It grants loans to only member countries
questions.option_d: It can grant loans to the central bank of a country
questions.explanation: The International Monetary Fund lends only to its member countries to help them address balance-of-payments problems; it is a membership-based institution, so it cannot lend to any country regardless of membership or restrict lending only to developed nations. Hence C is correct.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text: Which one of the following is not a feature of “Value Added Tax”?
questions.option_a: It is a multi-point destination-based system of taxation
questions.option_b: It is a tax levied on value addition at each stage of transaction in the production- distribution chain
questions.option_c: It is a tax on the final consumption of goods or services and must ultimately be borne by the consumer
questions.option_d: It is basically subject of the Central Government and the State Governments are only a facilitator for its successful implementation
questions.explanation: VAT is a multi-point, destination-based tax levied on the value added at each stage of production and distribution and ultimately borne by the final consumer, so options A, B and C describe it correctly. VAT was a State-level tax administered by the States, not a Central subject, so the incorrect statement is D.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text: A “closed economy” is an economy in which
questions.option_a: The money supply is fully controlled
questions.option_d: Neither exports nor imports take place
questions.explanation: A closed economy is one that has no economic transactions with the rest of the world, meaning neither exports nor imports take place; it is self-contained. The other options describe monetary control or deficit financing, not the defining feature, so D is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text: When the bark of a tree is removed in a circular fashion all around near its base, it gradually dries up and dies because
questions.option_a: Water form soil cannot rise to aerial parts
questions.option_c: Tree is infected by soil microbes
questions.option_d: Roots do not receive oxygen for respiration
questions.explanation: Removing a ring of bark (girdling) cuts the phloem, which carries food made in the leaves down to the roots; the roots, deprived of this energy supply, eventually starve and die, and the tree dies with them. Water transport through the xylem continues for a while, so the cause is root starvation, making B correct.

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text: The “New START” treaty was in the news. What is this treaty?
questions.option_a: It is a bilateral strategic nuclear arms reduction treaty between the USA and the Russian Federation
questions.option_b: It is multilateral energy security cooperation treaty among the members of the East Asia Summit
questions.option_c: It is a treaty between the Russian Federation and the European Union for the energy security cooperation
questions.option_d: It is a multilateral cooperation treaty among the BRICS countries for the promotion of trade.
questions.explanation: The New START treaty is a bilateral strategic nuclear arms reduction agreement between the United States and the Russian Federation, limiting deployed strategic warheads and delivery systems. It is not an energy or trade pact, so A is correct.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
Three of the following criteria have contributed to the recognition of Western Ghats-Sri Lanka and Indo-Burma regions as hotspots of biodiversity:
1. Species richness
2. Vegetation density
3. Endemism
4. Ethno-botanical importance
5. Threat perception
6. Adaptation of flora and fauna to warm and humid conditions Which three of the above are correct criteria in this context?
questions.explanation: Biodiversity hotspots are recognised chiefly on the basis of exceptional species richness, high levels of endemism (species found nowhere else), and a significant degree of threat or habitat loss. These three criteria, statements 1, 3 and 5, apply to the Western Ghats-Sri Lanka and Indo-Burma regions, so C is correct.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
Human activities in the recent past have caused the increased concentration of carbon dioxide in the atmosphere, but a lot of it does not remain in the lower atmosphere because of
1. Its escape into the outer stratosphere.
2. The photosynthesis by phytoplankton in the oceans.
3. The trapping of air in the polar ice caps.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Much of the added CO2 does not stay in the lower atmosphere because oceanic phytoplankton absorb it through photosynthesis (statement 2) and some carbon becomes locked in ancient air trapped within polar ice (statement 3). CO2 does not escape into the outer stratosphere in significant amounts, so statement 1 is wrong, leaving 2 and 3 (C).

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
In the context of ecosystem productivity, marine upwelling zones are important as they increase the marine productivity by bringing the
1. Decomposer microorganism to the surface.
2. Nutrients to the surface.
3. Bottom-dwelling organisms to the surface.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Upwelling raises marine productivity by bringing nutrient-rich deep water to the sunlit surface, fueling phytoplankton growth, so statement 2 is correct. It does not selectively lift decomposer microbes or bottom-dwelling organisms to the surface, so statements 1 and 3 are wrong, leaving only 2 (B).

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text: If a tropical rain forest is removed, it does not regenerate quickly as compared to a tropical deciduous forest. This is because
questions.option_a: The soil of rain forest is deficient in nutrients
questions.option_b: Propagules of the trees in a rain forest have poor viability
questions.option_c: The rain forest species are slow growing
questions.option_d: Exotic species invade the fertile soil of rain forest
questions.explanation: In a tropical rain forest most nutrients are held in the living vegetation rather than the soil, and rapid recycling leaves the soil itself nutrient-poor. Once the forest is cleared, the exposed infertile soil cannot readily support quick regrowth, so recovery is slow, making A correct.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text: The Himalayan Range is very rich in species diversity. Which one among the following is the most appropriate reason for this phenomenon?
questions.option_a: It has high rainfall that supports luxuriant vegetative growth
questions.option_b: It is a confluence of different bio-geographical zones
questions.option_c: Exotic and invasive species have not been introduced in this region
questions.explanation: The Himalayan range is exceptionally species-rich mainly because it is a meeting point of several biogeographic zones, blending flora and fauna of different realms along with a wide range of altitudes and climates. This confluence of biogeographical zones best explains its diversity, so B is correct.

questions.subject: Environment & Ecology
questions.text:
With reference to India, consider the following Central Acts:
1. Import and Export (Control) Act, 1947
2. Mining and Mineral development (Regulation) Act, 1957
3. Customs Act, 1962
4. Indian Forest Act, 1927
Which of the above Acts have relevance to/bearing on the biodiversity conservation in the country?
questions.explanation: All four Acts bear on biodiversity conservation: the Import and Export (Control) Act can regulate trade in flora and fauna, the Mines and Minerals Act governs mining that affects habitats, the Customs Act helps check illegal wildlife trade at borders, and the Indian Forest Act protects forests. Hence all four are relevant (C).

questions.subject: Current Affairs
questions.text: Karl Marx explained the process of class struggle with the help of which one of the following theories?
questions.explanation: Karl Marx explained class struggle through dialectical materialism, the idea that history advances through conflicts arising from material and economic conditions, with each stage generating opposing classes whose struggle drives change. This philosophical framework underlies his theory, so D is correct.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text:
A layer in the Earth’s atmosphere called Ionosphere facilitates radio communication. Why?
1. The presence of ozone causes the reflection of radio waves to Earth.
2. Radio waves have a very long wavelength.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The ionosphere aids radio communication because its layers of ionised gas reflect certain radio waves back to Earth, but this is due to free ions and electrons, not ozone, so statement 1 is wrong. Radio waves having long wavelengths does not by itself explain the ionospheric reflection, so statement 2 is also not the correct reason, giving D.

questions.subject: Indian Economy
questions.text:
Both Foreign Direct Investment (FDI) and Foreign Institutional Investor (FII) are related to investment in a country.
Which one of the following statements best represents an important difference between the two?
questions.option_a: FII helps bring better management skills and technology, while FDI only brings in capital
questions.option_b: FII helps in increasing capital availability in general, while FDI only targets specific sectors
questions.option_c: FDI flows only into the secondary market, while FII targets primary market
questions.option_d: FII is considered to be more stable than FDI
questions.explanation: FIIs invest in a country's financial markets and add to general capital availability, whereas FDI is direct, longer-term investment channelled into specific sectors or enterprises. Option B captures this key difference; FDI (not FII) brings management and technology and is more stable, so the other options are wrong.

questions.subject: Science & Technology
questions.text: A genetically engineered form of brinjal, known as the Bt-brinjal, has been developed. The objective of this is
questions.option_b: To improve its taste and nutritive qualities
questions.explanation: Bt-brinjal is genetically engineered to carry a gene from the bacterium Bacillus thuringiensis that produces a protein toxic to certain insect pests, chiefly the fruit and shoot borer, making the plant pest-resistant. The aim is thus pest resistance, so A is correct.

questions.subject: Indian Polity
questions.text:
With reference to “Aam Admi Bima Yojana”, consider the following statements:
1. The member insured under the scheme must be the head of the family or an earning member of the family in a rural landless household.
2. The member insured must be in the age group of 30 to 65 years.
3. There is a provision for free scholarship for up to two children of the insured who are studying between classes 9 and
12.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Under Aam Aadmi Bima Yojana the insured must be the head or an earning member of a rural landless household (statement 1), and the scheme provides free scholarships for up to two children of the insured studying in classes 9 to 12 (statement 3). The eligible age group is 18 to 59 years, not 30 to 65, so statement 2 is wrong, leaving 1 and 3 only (C).
